`ROLE_USER`, owns booking only)\n\n### Area E — Payment Failure Handling\n\n- `[MUST]` Expose `POST /api/v1/payments/failed` accepting `{ paymentIntentId }` for frontend-reported failures\n- `[MUST]` Publish `PaymentFailedEvent(cartId, userId)` → `booking-inventory` listener releases `HARD_LOCKED` seats\n- `[MUST]` Update `payments.status = FAILED`\n- `[MUST]` `payment_intent.payment_failed` and `payment_intent.canceled` Stripe webhooks must also trigger the same failure path (idempotent)\n\n### Area F — Cancellation & Refund (Buyer-Initiated)\n\n- `[MUST]` Expose `POST /api/v1/bookings/{bookingRef}/cancel` (JWT `ROLE_USER`, owns booking only)\n- `[MUST]` Validate cancellation policy: `booking.status = CONFIRMED`, not already cancelled, within cancellation window\n- `[MUST]` Call `StripeRefundService.createRefund(paymentIntentId, amount, reason)` — full or partial based on policy\n- `[MUST]` Set `Booking.status = CANCELLATION_PENDING` before Stripe call; `CANCELLED` after refund succeeds\n- `[MUST]` Persist `Refund` record with `stripeRefundId`, `amount`, `reason`, `status`\n- `[MUST]` Void all `ETicket` records for the booking (`status = VOIDED`)\n- `[MUST]` Set all `BookingItem.status = CANCELLED`\n- `[MUST]` Publish `BookingCancelledEvent(bookingId, cartId, seatIds, userId, reason)` → `booking-inventory` (seat release) + `engagement`\n- `[SHOULD]` Support partial refund when a cancellation fee is configured (deduct fee, refund remainder)\n- `[WONT]` Organizer-initiated bulk cancel — lives in admin/scheduling, not this flow\n\n### Area G — Stripe Webhook Safety Net\n\n- `[MUST]` Expose `POST /api/v1/webhooks/stripe` (public, no JWT, Stripe-Signature verified)\n- `[MUST]` Verify signature via `StripeWebhookHandler.constructEvent(payload, sigHeader)` — reject `400` on invalid signature\n- `[MUST]` Handle `payment_intent.succeeded` → idempotent booking confirmation (skip if already `CONFIRMED`)\n- `[MUST]` Handle `payment_intent.payment_failed` + `payment_intent.canceled` → `PaymentFailedEvent`\n- `[MUST]` Handle `refund.updated` → update `refunds.status`\n- `[MUST]` Handle `refund.failed` → mark `refunds.status = FAILED`, log ops alert\n- `[SHOULD]` Return `200 OK` for all handled events (Stripe retries on non-200)\n- `[WONT]` EB `order.placed`, `order.refunded`, `order.updated` webhook handling — Stripe is the order authority\n\n### Area H — Internal Payment Status API (Cross-Module)\n\n- `[MUST]` Expose `GET /api/v1/internal/payments/by-cart/{cartId}` (internal — no public JWT) for `booking-inventory`'s `PaymentTimeoutWatchdog`\n- `[MUST]` Return `{ confirmed: true/false }` based on whether a `CONFIRMED` booking exists for the cart\n\n### Area I — Booking History\n\n- `[MUST]` Expose `GET /api/v1/bookings` (JWT `ROLE_USER`) — paginated list of caller's bookings\n- `[MUST]` Expose `GET /api/v1/bookings/{bookingRef}` (JWT `ROLE_USER`, owns booking) — single booking detail with items + e-tickets\n\n### Area J — Async Refund Handling\n\n- `[MUST]` `refund.updated` webhook updates `Refund.status` in DB\n- `[SHOULD]` On `refund.failed`, send notification alert (log ops-level alert; no email delivery in this flow)\n- `[WONT]` Email delivery of refund confirmation (token/status only)\n\n---\n\n## Stage 2 — Domain Model\n\nA `Booking` is created as a `PENDING` shell when `CartAssembledEvent` arrives, promoted to `CONFIRMED` after Stripe payment succeeds, and optionally transitioned to `CANCELLATION_PENDING → CANCELLED` via buyer-initiated cancellation. A `Booking` owns many `BookingItem` records (one per cart item — reserved seat or GA claim), one `Payment` record, and many `ETicket` records (one per `BookingItem`). A `Refund` belongs to a `Booking` and is created if the buyer cancels. A `CancellationRequest` records the buyer's intent and tracks approval/rejection state independently of the refund outcome.\n\n### Entities & Relationships\n\n**`Booking`**\n- Identity: `id`, `bookingRef` (human-readable unique — `BK-{YYYYMMDD}-{seq}`)\n- Lifecycle: `PENDING → CONFIRMED → CANCELLATION_PENDING → CANCELLED`\n- Relationships: belongs to one `User` (via `userId` — cross-module ID only); references one `Cart` (via `cartId` — cross-module ID only); has many `BookingItem`; has one `Payment`\n- Ownership: `payments-ticketing`\n\n**`BookingItem`**\n- Identity: `id`\n- Lifecycle: `ACTIVE → CANCELLED`\n- Relationships: belongs to one `Booking`; references one seat (via `seatId` nullable — cross-module ID) or one GA claim (via `gaClaimId` nullable — cross-module ID); references pricing tier via `ticketClassId`\n- Ownership: `payments-ticketing`\n\n**`Payment`**\n- Identity: `id`, `stripePaymentIntentId` (unique)\n- Lifecycle: `PENDING → SUCCESS | FAILED`\n- Relationships: belongs to one `Booking` (1:1)\n- Ownership: `payments-ticketing`\n\n**`Refund`**\n- Identity: `id`, `stripeRefundId` (unique)\n- Lifecycle: `PENDING → SUCCEEDED | FAILED`\n- Relationships: belongs to one `Booking`\n- Ownership: `payments-ticketing`\n\n**`ETicket`**\n- Identity: `id`, `ticketCode` = `\"{bookingRef}:{bookingItemId}\"` (unique per item)\n- Lifecycle: `ACTIVE → VOIDED`\n- Relationships: belongs to one `BookingItem` (1:1, enforced by UNIQUE constraint)\n- Ownership: `payments-ticketing`\n\n**`CancellationRequest`**\n- Identity: `id`\n- Lifecycle: `PENDING → APPROVED | REJECTED`\n- Relationships: belongs to one `Booking`\n- Ownership: `payments-ticketing`\n\n### Non-Persisted Concepts\n\n- `PaymentIntentResult` — DTO from `StripePaymentService`; carries `clientSecret` and `paymentIntentId`; never stored server-side\n- `RefundResult` — DTO from `StripeRefundService`; carries `stripeRefundId` and `status`\n\n### Cross-Module References (IDs only — no entity imports)\n\n| Field | Points To | Module |\n|---|---|---|\n| `Booking.userId` | `users.id` | identity |\n| `Booking.cartId` | `carts.id` | booking-inventory |\n| `Booking.slotId` | `show_slots.id` | scheduling |\n| `Booking.eventId` | `event_catalog.id` | discovery-catalog |\n| `BookingItem.seatId` | `seats.id` (nullable) | booking-inventory |\n| `BookingItem.gaClaimId` | `ga_inventory_claims.id` (nullable) | booking-inventory |\n\n---\n\n## Stage 3 — Architecture & File Structure\n\n### Architecture Pattern\n**Modular Monolith (Fixed)** — module registry in `PRODUCT.md`.\n\n### Module Ownership\n**Primary module:** `payments-ticketing`\n**Supporting:** `shared/stripe/` (ACL — already created in pre-FR5)\n**Event consumers (no new files needed):** `booking-inventory` (`PaymentFailedListener`, `BookingConfirmedListener`), `engagement`, `identity`\n\n### Already Exists (pre-FR5 scaffold — do not recreate)\n\n**`payments-ticketing`:**\n- `domain/Booking.java`, `domain/Payment.java`\n- `domain/enums/BookingStatus.java`, `domain/enums/PaymentStatus.java`\n- `repository/BookingRepository.java`, `repository/PaymentRepository.java`\n- `api/internal/InternalPaymentController.java`\n- `event/listener/CartAssembledListener.java` (stub)\n- `event/listener/BookingConfirmedByPaymentListener.java` (stub)\n\n**`shared/stripe/` (all exist):**\n- `service/StripePaymentService.java`, `service/StripeRefundService.java`\n- `webhook/StripeWebhookHandler.java`\n- `config/StripeConfig.java`\n- `dto/StripePaymentIntentRequest.java`, `dto/StripePaymentIntentResponse.java`\n- `dto/StripeRefundRequest.java`, `dto/StripeRefundResponse.java`\n- `dto/StripeWebhookEvent.java`\n- `exception/StripeIntegrationException.java`, `exception/StripeWebhookSignatureException.java`\n\n### New Files to Create for FR5\n\n```\npayments-ticketing/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/paymentsticketing/\n\ndomain/\n├── BookingItem.java                        ← new entity\n├── Refund.java                             ← new entity\n├── ETicket.java                            ← new entity\n├── CancellationRequest.java                ← new entity\n├── enums/BookingItemStatus.java            ← ACTIVE, CANCELLED\n├── enums/RefundStatus.java                 ← PENDING, SUCCEEDED, FAILED\n├── enums/ETicketStatus.java                ← ACTIVE, VOIDED\n├── enums/CancellationRequestStatus.java    ← PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED\n└── enums/RefundReason.java                 ← REQUESTED_BY_CUSTOMER, DUPLICATE, FRAUDULENT\n\nrepository/\n├── BookingItemRepository.java\n├── RefundRepository.java\n├── ETicketRepository.java\n└── CancellationRequestRepository.java\n\nservice/\n├── PaymentService.java           ← PaymentIntent creation + confirmation logic\n├── BookingService.java           ← booking record lifecycle + history queries\n├── ETicketService.java           ← ticket generation + QR code + void\n├── CancellationService.java      ← buyer-initiated cancel + refund orchestration\n└── RefundService.java            ← refund status updates (webhook-driven)\n\nevent/listener/\n├── CartAssembledListener.java    ← extend stub: listens CartAssembledEvent → PaymentService.createCheckout()\n└── BookingConfirmedByPaymentListener.java  ← extend stub: publishes BookingConfirmedEvent after confirm\n\napi/controller/\n├── PaymentController.java        ← GET /checkout/{cartId}, POST /confirm, POST /failed\n├── BookingController.java        ← GET /bookings, GET /bookings/{ref}, POST /bookings/{ref}/cancel\n├── ETicketController.java        ← GET /tickets/{bookingRef}\n└── StripeWebhookController.java  ← POST /webhooks/stripe\n\napi/dto/request/\n├── PaymentConfirmRequest.java    ← { paymentIntentId }\n├── PaymentFailedRequest.java     ← { paymentIntentId }\n└── CancellationRequest.java      ← { reason }\n\napi/dto/response/\n├── CheckoutInitResponse.java     ← { cartId, bookingRef, paymentIntentId, clientSecret, amount, currency }\n├── BookingResponse.java          ← booking + items + e-ticket stubs\n├── BookingItemResponse.java      ← item-level detail\n├── BookingSummaryResponse.java   ← light summary for list endpoint\n├── ETicketResponse.java          ← { ticketCode, qrData, pdfUrl, status }\n└── RefundResponse.java           ← { stripeRefundId, amount, status }\n\nmapper/\n├── BookingMapper.java            ← Booking → BookingResponse / BookingSummaryResponse\n└── ETicketMapper.java            ← ETicket → ETicketResponse\n\nexception/\n├── BookingNotFoundException.java\n├── PaymentNotConfirmedException.java\n├── DuplicatePaymentException.java\n├── CancellationNotAllowedException.java\n├── RefundFailedException.java\n└── CartItemsFetchException.java\n\narch/\n└── PaymentsTicketingArchTest.java   ← ArchUnit rules\n\nsrc/test/java/com/eventplatform/paymentsticketing/\n├── domain/\n│   ├── BookingTest.java\n"
  }
}

| Issue | Type | Priority | Status | Files Changed |\n|---|---|---|---|---|---|\n| I1 | `payments-ticketing` module absent | GAP | P0 | ✅ Fixed | root `pom.xml`, new module scaffold |\n| I2 | Stripe SDK not declared | GAP | P0 | ✅ Fixed | parent `pom.xml`, `shared/pom.xml` |\n| I3 | `CartAssembledEvent` missing amount + email | BUG | P0 | ✅ Fixed | `CartAssembledEvent.java`, `CartService.java` |\n| I4 | `BookingConfirmedEvent.ebOrderId` wrong name | BUG | P0 | ✅ Fixed | `BookingConfirmedEvent.java` |\n| I5 | `BookingCancelledEvent` missing | GAP | P0 | ✅ Fixed | new `BookingCancelledEvent.java` |\n| I6 | `shared/stripe/` ACL package absent | GAP | P1 | ✅ Fixed | 9 new files under `shared/stripe/` |\n| I7 | `PaymentTimeoutWatchdog` uses `EbOrderService` | CRITICAL BUG | P1 | ✅ Fixed | `PaymentTimeoutWatchdog.java`, new `PaymentStatusClient.java` |\n| I8 | `Seat.ebOrderId` wrong column name | BUG | P1 | ✅ Fixed | `Seat.java`, new V25 migration |\n| I9 | DB migrations V25–V31 missing | GAP | P1 | ✅ Fixed | 7 new SQL migration files |\n| I10 | No Stripe config in `application.yaml` | GAP | P1 | ✅ Fixed | `application.yaml` |\n| I11 | `BookingConfirmedListener` will fail to compile | BUG | P2 | ✅ Fixed | `BookingConfirmedListener.java`, `CartService.java` |\n| I12 | `SeatActionContext.orderId` semantically wrong | BUG | P2 | ✅ Fixed | `SeatLockStateMachineService.java` + callers |\n| I13 | `SeatLockEvent.CHECKOUT_INITIATE` EB-named | ISSUE | P2 | ✅ Fixed | `SeatLockEvent.java`, config, callers |\n| I14 | `CartItem.ebTicketClassId` — kept by design | — | N/A | ✅ Intentional | No change |\n| I15 | Audit reason `\"ORDER_PLACED\"` stale | CLEANUP | P3 | ✅ Fixed | `CartService.java` |\n| I16 | `CartAssembledEvent` stale comment | CLEANUP | P3 | ✅ Fixed | `CartAssembledEvent.java` |\n\n---\n\n## Detailed Implementation\n\n---\n\n### I1 — `payments-ticketing` Module Created\n\n**Problem:** The `payments-ticketing/` Maven module did not exist. FR5 domain (`BookingService`, `PaymentService`, `ETicketService`, `CancellationService`, `RefundService`) had nowhere to live.\n\n**Fix Applied:**\n- Created `payments-ticketing/` directory with full standard Maven layout:\n  ```\n  payments-ticketing/\n    pom.xml\n    src/main/java/com/eventplatform/paymentsticketing/\n      PaymentsTicketingApplication.java\n      api/internal/InternalPaymentController.java\n      domain/ (Booking.java, Payment.java, etc.)\n      repository/ (BookingRepository.java, PaymentRepository.java)\n      service/ (BookingService.java)\n      event/listener/ (CartAssembledListener.java, BookingConfirmedByPaymentListener.java)\n    src/main/resources/\n    src/test/java/com/eventplatform/paymentsticketing/\n      PaymentsTicketingTestApplication.java\n      api/internal/InternalPaymentControllerTest.java\n  ```\n- Added `<module>payments-ticketing</module>` to root `pom.xml` after `booking-inventory`.\n- `payments-ticketing/pom.xml` declares parent, dependencies on `shared`, `booking-inventory` (REST only), `spring-boot-starter-web`, `spring-boot-starter-data-jpa`.\n\n**Key files:**\n- [payments-ticketing/pom.xml](payments-ticketing/pom.xml)\n- [payments-ticketing/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/paymentsticketing/PaymentsTicketingApplication.java](payments-ticketing/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/paymentsticketing/PaymentsTicketingApplication.java)\n\n---\n\n### I2 — Stripe Java SDK Added\n\n**Problem:** `com.stripe:stripe-java` was not declared in any `pom.xml`. All Stripe ACL classes failed to compile.\n\n**Fix Applied:**\n- Added to parent `pom.xml` `<dependencyManagement>`:\n  ```xml\n  <dependency>\n      <groupId>com.stripe</groupId>\n      <artifactId>stripe-java</artifactId>\n      <version>25.3.0</version>\n  </dependency>\n  ```\n- Added to `shared/pom.xml` `<dependencies>`:\n  ```xml\n  <dependency>\n      <groupId>com.stripe</groupId>\n      <artifactId>stripe-java</artifactId>\n  </dependency>\n  ```\n\n**Key files:**\n- [pom.xml](pom.xml)\n- [shared/pom.xml](shared/pom.xml)\n\n---\n\n### I3 — `CartAssembledEvent` Extended with Amount and Email\n\n**Problem:** `CartAssembledEvent` only had 6 fields (`cartId`, `slotId`, `userId`, `orgId`, `ebEventId`, `couponCode`). `payments-ticketing` needs `totalAmountInSmallestUnit`, `currency`, and `userEmail` to create a Stripe `PaymentIntent`.\n\n**Before:**\n```java\npublic record CartAssembledEvent(\n    Long cartId, Long slotId, Long userId, Long orgId,\n    String ebEventId, String couponCode\n) {}\n```\n\n**After:**\n```java\npublic record CartAssembledEvent(\n    Long cartId,\n    Long slotId,\n    Long userId,\n    Long orgId,\n    String ebEventId,\n    String couponCode,\n    long totalAmountInSmallestUnit,   // paise — for Stripe PaymentIntent `amount`\n    String currency,                  // e.g. \"inr\"\n    String userEmail                  // for Stripe `receipt_email`\n) {}\n```\n\n**`CartService.confirm()` updated** to accept `userEmail` as a new parameter and populate the event:\n```java\npublic CartSummary confirm(Long cartId, Long orgId, String userEmail, ConfirmCartRequest request) {\n    // ...\n    CartPricingResult pricing = cartPricingService.price(cart, request.couponCode());\n    long amountInSmallestUnit = pricing.total().amount()\n        .multiply(BigDecimal.valueOf(100)).longValue();\n\n    applicationEventPublisher.publishEvent(new CartAssembledEvent(\n        cartId, cart.getSlotId(), cart.getUserId(), orgId,\n        ebEventId, request.couponCode(),\n        amountInSmallestUnit, pricing.total().currency().toLowerCase(), userEmail\n    ));\n}\n```\n\nAlso updated the stale comment (I16): `// Published by booking-inventory when cart lock is confirmed. Consumed by payments-ticketing to create a Stripe PaymentIntent.`\n\n**Key files:**\n- [shared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/common/event/published/CartAssembledEvent.java](shared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/common/event/published/CartAssembledEvent.java)\n- [booking-inventory/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/service/CartService.java](booking-inventory/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/service/CartService.java)\n\n---\n\n### I4 — `BookingConfirmedEvent.ebOrderId` Renamed to `stripePaymentIntentId`\n\n**Problem:** `BookingConfirmedEvent` had a field named `ebOrderId`, but in FR5 this carries a Stripe `PaymentIntent` ID. Misleading name → silent runtime bugs and confused developers.\n\n**Before:**\n```java\npublic record BookingConfirmedEvent(Long cartId, List<Long> seatIds, String ebOrderId, Long userId) {}\n```\n\n**After:**\n```java\npublic record BookingConfirmedEvent(\n    Long cartId,\n    List<Long> seatIds,\n    String stripePaymentIntentId,\n    Long userId\n) {}\n```\n\n**Key files:**\n- [shared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/common/event/published/BookingConfirmedEvent.java](shared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/common/event/published/BookingConfirmedEvent.java)\n\n---\n\n### I5 — `BookingCancelledEvent` Created\n\n**Problem:** No `BookingCancelledEvent` existed. FR6 (Buyer-Initiated Cancellation) requires `payments-ticketing` to publish this event so `booking-inventory` can release confirmed seats back to `AVAILABLE`.\n\n**Fix Applied:** Created new event record in `shared/common/event/published/`:\n```java\npublic record BookingCancelledEvent(\n    Long bookingId,\n    Long cartId,\n    List<Long> seatIds,\n    Long userId,\n    String reason                  // \"BUYER_CANCEL\", \"ADMIN_CANCEL\", \"PAYMENT_FAILED\"\n) {}\n```\n\n**Key files:**\n- [shared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/common/event/published/BookingCancelledEvent.java](shared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/common/event/published/BookingCancelledEvent.java)\n\n---\n\n### I6 — `shared/stripe/` ACL Package Created\n\n**Problem:** The entire Stripe ACL layer was absent. Per `PRODUCT.md` Hard Rule #2, no module may call Stripe HTTP directly.\n\n**Fix Applied:** Created the full Stripe ACL package under `shared/`:\n\n```\nshared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/stripe/\n  config/\n    StripeConfig.java              — reads stripe.secret-key; calls Stripe.apiKey = secretKey at @PostConstruct\n  dto/\n    StripePaymentIntentRequest.java\n    StripePaymentIntentResponse.java\n    StripeRefundRequest.java\n    StripeRefundResponse.java\n    StripeWebhookEvent.java\n  exception/\n    StripeIntegrationException.java\n    StripeWebhookSignatureException.java\n  service/\n    StripePaymentService.java      — createPaymentIntent(), getPaymentIntent(), cancelPaymentIntent()\n    StripeRefundService.java       — createRefund(), getRefund()\n  webhook/\n    StripeWebhookHandler.java      — parseEvent(), extractObjectId()\n```\n\n**`StripeConfig.java`** reads from `application.yaml` and initialises the SDK:\n```java\n@Configuration\npublic class StripeConfig {\n    @Value(\"${stripe.secret-key}\")\n    private String secretKey;\n\n    @PostConstruct\n    public void init() {\n        Stripe.apiKey = secretKey;\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**`StripePaymentService.java`** wraps SDK static calls in exception handling:\n```java\npublic StripePaymentIntentResponse createPaymentIntent(StripePaymentIntentRequest request) {\n    try {\n        PaymentIntentCreateParams params = PaymentIntentCreateParams.builder()\n            .setAmount(request.amount())\n            .setCurrency(request.currency())\n            .setReceiptEmail(request.receiptEmail())\n            .setCaptureMethod(PaymentIntentCreateParams.CaptureMethod.AUTOMATIC)\n            .build();\n        PaymentIntent intent = PaymentIntent.create(params);\n        return new StripePaymentIntentResponse(intent.getId(), intent.getClientSecret(), intent.getStatus());\n    } catch (StripeException e) {\n        throw new StripeIntegrationException(\"Failed to create PaymentIntent\", e);\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**`StripeWebhookHandler.java`** verifies signatures using the webhook secret:\n```java\npublic StripeWebhookEvent parseEvent(String payload, String sigHeader) {\n    try {\n        Event event = Webhook.constructEvent(payload, sigHeader, webhookSecret);\n        // extract objectId from event data\n        return new StripeWebhookEvent(event.getId(), event.getType(), objectId);\n    } catch (SignatureVerificationException e) {\n        throw new StripeWebhookSignatureException(\"Invalid Stripe webhook signature\", e);\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Key files:**\n- [shared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/stripe/config/StripeConfig.java](shared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/stripe/config/StripeConfig.java)\n"
  }

    "output": "Chunk ID: 45188c\nWall time: 0.0517 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 2669\nOutput:\n# PRE-FR5 Changes — Implementation Record\n\n**Date Implemented:** March 4, 2026\n**Scope:** All 16 issues (I1–I16) from `docs/PRE5 changes.md` resolved before FR5 (Stripe-based Payments & Ticketing).\n**Final Test Result:** 228 tests across 7 modules — ALL PASS ✅\n\n---\n\n## Summary Table\n\n| # | Issue | Type | Priority | Status | Files Changed |\n|---|---|---|---|---|---|\n| I1 | `payments-ticketing` module absent | GAP | P0 | ✅ Fixed | root `pom.xml`, new module scaffold |\n| I2 | Stripe SDK not declared | GAP | P0 | ✅ Fixed | parent `pom.xml`, `shared/pom.xml` |\n| I3 | `CartAssembledEvent` missing amount + email | BUG | P0 | ✅ Fixed | `CartAssembledEvent.java`, `CartService.java` |\n| I4 | `BookingConfirmedEvent.ebOrderId` wrong name | BUG | P0 | ✅ Fixed | `BookingConfirmedEvent.java` |\n| I5 | `BookingCancelledEvent` missing | GAP | P0 | ✅ Fixed | new `BookingCancelledEvent.java` |\n| I6 | `shared/stripe/` ACL package absent | GAP | P1 | ✅ Fixed | 9 new files under `shared/stripe/` |\n| I7 | `PaymentTimeoutWatchdog` uses `EbOrderService` | CRITICAL BUG | P1 | ✅ Fixed | `PaymentTimeoutWatchdog.java`, new `PaymentStatusClient.java` |\n| I8 | `Seat.ebOrderId` wrong column name | BUG | P1 | ✅ Fixed | `Seat.java`, new V25 migration |\n| I9 | DB migrations V25–V31 missing | GAP | P1 | ✅ Fixed | 7 new SQL migration files |\n| I10 | No Stripe config in `application.yaml` | GAP | P1 | ✅ Fixed | `application.yaml` |\n| I11 | `BookingConfirmedListener` will fail to compile | BUG | P2 | ✅ Fixed | `BookingConfirmedListener.java`, `CartService.java` |\n| I12 | `SeatActionContext.orderId` semantically wrong | BUG | P2 | ✅ Fixed | `SeatLockStateMachineService.java` + callers |\n| I13 | `SeatLockEvent.CHECKOUT_INITIATE` EB-named | ISSUE | P2 | ✅ Fixed | `SeatLockEvent.java`, config, callers |\n| I14 | `CartItem.ebTicketClassId` — kept by design | — | N/A | ✅ Intentional | No change |\n| I15 | Audit reason `\"ORDER_PLACED\"` stale | CLEANUP | P3 | ✅ Fixed | `CartService.java` |\n| I16 | `CartAssembledEvent` stale comment | CLEANUP | P3 | ✅ Fixed | `CartAssembledEvent.java` |\n\n---\n\n## Detailed Implementation\n\n---\n\n### I1 — `payments-ticketing` Module Created\n\n**Problem:** The `payments-ticketing/` Maven module did not exist. FR5 domain (`BookingService`, `PaymentService`, `ETicketService`, `CancellationService`, `RefundService`) had nowhere to live.\n\n**Fix Applied:**\n- Created `payments-ticketing/` directory with full standard Maven layout:\n  ```\n  payments-ticketing/\n    pom.xml\n    src/main/java/com/eventplatform/paymentsticketing/\n      PaymentsTicketingApplication.java\n      api/internal/InternalPaymentController.java\n      domain/ (Booking.java, Payment.java, etc.)\n      repository/ (BookingRepository.java, PaymentRepository.java)\n      service/ (BookingService.java)\n      event/listener/ (CartAssembledListener.java, BookingConfirmedByPaymentListener.java)\n    src/main/resources/\n    src/test/java/com/eventplatform/paymentsticketing/\n      PaymentsTicketingTestApplication.java\n      api/internal/InternalPaymentControllerTest.java\n  ```\n- Added `<module>payments-ticketing</module>` to root `pom.xml` after `booking-inventory`.\n- `payments-ticketing/pom.xml` declares parent, dependencies on `shared`, `booking-inventory` (REST only), `spring-boot-starter-web`, `spring-boot-starter-data-jpa`.\n\n**Key files:**\n- [payments-ticketing/pom.xml](payments-ticketing/pom.xml)\n- [payments-ticketing/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/paymentsticketing/PaymentsTicketingApplication.java](payments-ticketing/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/paymentsticketing/PaymentsTicketingApplication.java)\n\n---\n\n### I2 — Stripe Java SDK Added\n\n**Problem:** `com.stripe:stripe-java` was not declared in any `pom.xml`. All Stripe ACL classes failed to compile.\n\n**Fix Applied:**\n- Added to parent `pom.xml` `<dependencyManagement>`:\n  ```xml\n  <dependency>\n      <groupId>com.stripe</groupId>\n      <artifactId>stripe-java</artifactId>\n      <version>25.3.0</version>\n  </dependency>\n  ```\n- Added to `shared/pom.xml` `<dependencies>`:\n  ```xml\n  <dependency>\n      <groupId>com.stripe</groupId>\n      <artifactId>stripe-java</artifactId>\n  </dependency>\n  ```\n\n**Key files:**\n- [pom.xml](pom.xml)\n- [shared/pom.xml](shared/pom.xml)\n\n---\n\n### I3 — `CartAssembledEvent` Extended with Amount and Email\n\n**Problem:** `CartAssembledEvent` only had 6 fields (`cartId`, `slotId`, `userId`, `orgId`, `ebEventId`, `couponCode`). `payments-ticketing` needs `totalAmountInSmallestUnit`, `currency`, and `userEmail` to create a Stripe `PaymentIntent`.\n\n**Before:**\n```java\npublic record CartAssembledEvent(\n    Long cartId, Long slotId, Long userId, Long orgId,\n    String ebEventId, String couponCode\n) {}\n```\n\n**After:**\n```java\npublic record CartAssembledEvent(\n    Long cartId,\n    Long slotId,\n    Long userId,\n    Long orgId,\n    String ebEventId,\n    String couponCode,\n    long totalAmountInSmallestUnit,   // paise — for Stripe PaymentIntent `amount`\n    String currency,                  // e.g. \"inr\"\n    String userEmail                  // for Stripe `receipt_email`\n) {}\n```\n\n**`CartService.confirm()` updated** to accept `userEmail` as a new parameter and populate the event:\n```java\npublic CartSummary confirm(Long cartId, Long orgId, String userEmail, ConfirmCartRequest request) {\n    // ...\n    CartPricingResult pricing = cartPricingService.price(cart, request.couponCode());\n    long amountInSmallestUnit = pricing.total().amount()\n        .multiply(BigDecimal.valueOf(100)).longValue();\n\n    applicationEventPublisher.publishEvent(new CartAssembledEvent(\n        cartId, cart.getSlotId(), cart.getUserId(), orgId,\n        ebEventId, request.couponCode(),\n        amountInSmallestUnit, pricing.total().currency().toLowerCase(), userEmail\n    ));\n}\n```\n\nAlso updated the stale comment (I16): `// Published by booking-inventory when cart lock is confirmed. Consumed by payments-ticketing to create a Stripe PaymentIntent.`\n\n**Key files:**\n- [shared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/common/event/published/CartAssembledEvent.java](shared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/common/event/published/CartAssembledEvent.java)\n- [booking-inventory/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/service/CartService.java](booking-inventory/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/service/CartService.java)\n\n---\n\n### I4 — `BookingConfirmedEvent.ebOrderId` Renamed to `stripePaymentIntentId`\n\n**Problem:** `BookingConfirmedEvent` had a field named `ebOrderId`, but in FR5 this carries a Stripe `PaymentIntent` ID. Misleading name → silent runtime bugs and confused developers.\n\n**Before:**\n```java\npublic record BookingConfirmedEvent(Long cartId, List<Long> seatIds, String ebOrderId, Long userId) {}\n```\n\n**After:**\n```java\npublic record BookingConfirmedEvent(\n    Long cartId,\n    List<Long> seatIds,\n    String stripePaymentIntentId,\n    Long userId\n) {}\n```\n\n**Key files:**\n- [shared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/common/event/published/BookingConfirmedEvent.java](shared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/common/event/published/BookingConfirmedEvent.java)\n\n---\n\n### I5 — `BookingCancelledEvent` Created\n\n**Problem:** No `BookingCancelledEvent` existed. FR6 (Buyer-Initiated Cancellation) requires `payments-ticketing` to publish this event so `booking-inventory` can release confirmed seats back to `AVAILABLE`.\n\n**Fix Applied:** Created new event record in `shared/common/event/published/`:\n```java\npublic record BookingCancelledEvent(\n    Long bookingId,\n    Long cartId,\n    List<Long> seatIds,\n    Long userId,\n    String reason                  // \"BUYER_CANCEL\", \"ADMIN_CANCEL\", \"PAYMENT_FAILED\"\n) {}\n```\n\n**Key files:**\n- [shared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/common/event/published/BookingCancelledEvent.java](shared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/common/event/published/BookingCancelledEvent.java)\n\n---\n\n### I6 — `shared/stripe/` ACL Package Created\n\n**Problem:** The entire Stripe ACL layer was absent. Per `PRODUCT.md` Hard Rule #2, no module may call Stripe HTTP directly.\n\n**Fix Applied:** Created the full Stripe ACL package under `shared/`:\n\n```\nshared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/stripe/\n  config/\n    StripeConfig.java              — reads stripe.secret-key; calls Stripe.apiKey = secretKey at @PostConstruct\n  dto/\n    StripePaymentIntentRequest.java\n    StripePaymentIntentResponse.java\n    StripeRefundRequest.java\n    StripeRefundResponse.java\n    StripeWebhookEvent.java\n  exception/\n    StripeIntegrationException.java\n    StripeWebhookSignatureException.java\n  service/\n    StripePaymentService.java      — createPaymentIntent(), getPaymentIntent(), cancelPaymentIntent()\n    StripeRefundService.java       — createRefund(), getRefund()\n  webhook/\n    StripeWebhookHandler.java      — parseEvent(), extractObjectId()\n```\n\n**`StripeConfig.java`** reads from `application.yaml` and initialises the SDK:\n```java\n@Configuration\npublic class StripeConfig {\n    @Value(\"${stripe.secret-key}\")\n    private String secretKey;\n\n    @PostConstruct\n    public void init() {\n        Stripe.apiKey = secretKey;\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**`StripePaymentService.java`** wraps SDK static calls in exception handling:\n```java\npublic StripePaymentIntentResponse createPaymentIntent(StripePaymentIntentRequest request) {\n    try {\n        PaymentIntentCreateParams params = PaymentIntentCreateParams.builder()\n            .setAmount(request.amount())\n            .setCurrency(request.currency())\n            .setReceiptEmail(request.receiptEmail())\n            .setCaptureMethod(PaymentIntentCreateParams.CaptureMethod.AUTOMATIC)\n            .build();\n        PaymentIntent intent = PaymentIntent.create(params);\n        return new StripePaymentIntentResponse(intent.getId(), intent.getClientSecret(), intent.getStatus());\n    } catch (StripeException e) {\n        throw new StripeIntegrationException(\"Failed to create PaymentIntent\", e);\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**`StripeWebhookHandler.java`** verifies signatures using the webhook secret:\n```java\npublic StripeWebhookEvent parseEvent(String payload, String sigHeader) {\n    try {\n        Event event = Webhook.constructEvent(payload, sigHeader, webhookSecret);\n        // extract objectId from event data\n        return new StripeWebhookEvent(event.getId(), event.getType(), objectId);\n    } catch (SignatureVerificationException e) {\n        throw new StripeWebhookSignatureException(\"Invalid Stripe webhook signature\", e);\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Key files:**\n- [shared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/stripe/config/StripeConfig.java](shared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/stripe/config/StripeConfig.java)\n"
  }
}
```

{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T17:23:21.423Z",

  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_xb8CmTefpTZ9QDx9cPtW0OZt",
    "output": "Chunk ID: c9e2f1\nWall time: 0.0509 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 2600\nOutput:\n        );\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Writing to Modules — REST API Only\n\n```java\n// ❌ WRONG — bypasses module's validation and events\nprivate final BookingRepository bookingRepository;\npublic void bulkCancel(...) {\n    bookingRepository.deleteAll(bookings); // skips business rules\n}\n\n// ✅ CORRECT — calls module's REST API\npublic void bulkCancel(List<UUID> bookingIds) {\n    for (UUID id : bookingIds) {\n        restClient.delete(\"/api/bookings/{id}\", id);\n    }\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## Layer-by-Layer Coding Standards\n\n---\n\n### Domain Layer (`domain/`)\n\nThe domain is the most important layer. It owns all business rules. It has no knowledge\nof Spring, JPA, HTTP, or any infrastructure.\n\n**Rules:**\n- No `@Service`, `@Component`, `@Repository`, `@Autowired` in domain classes\n- No Spring framework imports of any kind\n- Entities have **behaviour methods** — not just getters and setters\n- All state transitions go through domain methods — never set a status field directly from a service\n- Throw `BusinessRuleException` (from `shared/`) for rule violations\n- All entities extend `BaseEntity` from `shared/common/domain/`\n\n**Note on JPA in domain:**\nThis codebase intentionally keeps JPA annotations on domain entities (standard Spring Data JPA\napproach). We accept the tradeoff (domain is not fully persistence-ignorant) to avoid the\noverhead of a separate persistence model. Do not introduce a second model unless explicitly\napproved.\n\n```java\n// ✅ Correct domain entity — has behaviour, enforces its own rules\n@Entity\n@Table(name = \"bookings\")\npublic class Booking extends BaseEntity {\n\n    @Enumerated(EnumType.STRING)\n    @Column(nullable = false)\n    private BookingStatus status;\n\n    @Column(nullable = false)\n    private UUID userId;\n\n    // Behaviour method — enforces the rule itself\n    public void confirm() {\n        if (this.status != BookingStatus.PAYMENT_PENDING) {\n            throw new BusinessRuleException(\n                \"Cannot confirm a booking in status: \" + this.status);\n        }\n        this.status = BookingStatus.CONFIRMED;\n    }\n\n    public void cancel() {\n        if (this.status == BookingStatus.CANCELLED) {\n            throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Booking is already cancelled\");\n        }\n        if (this.status == BookingStatus.FAILED) {\n            throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Cannot cancel a failed booking\");\n        }\n        this.status = BookingStatus.CANCELLED;\n    }\n\n    // ❌ Never expose raw status setter — callers must use behaviour methods\n    // public void setStatus(BookingStatus status) { ... }\n}\n```\n\n**Value objects are immutable records:**\n\n```java\n// ✅ Correct — value object as Java record\npublic record SeatReference(UUID seatId, String row, int number, PricingTier tier) {\n\n    // Validate in the compact constructor\n    public SeatReference {\n        Objects.requireNonNull(seatId, \"seatId must not be null\");\n        Objects.requireNonNull(row, \"row must not be null\");\n        if (number < 1) throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"seat number must be positive\");\n    }\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Service Layer (`service/`)\n\nThe service orchestrates: it calls domain methods, saves via repositories, publishes events,\nand calls ACL facades for external systems. It owns no business logic itself — the domain does.\n\n**Rules:**\n- Annotate with `@Service`\n- Use constructor injection — never `@Autowired` on fields\n- One service per capability group (e.g. `BookingService`), not one per entity\n- Methods map to use cases, not CRUD operations\n- **Mapper ownership:** Controllers call the mapper to convert the returned domain object → response DTO. Services call the mapper to convert inbound request DTOs / commands → domain objects before persistence. Neither layer maps fields manually.\n- Never map fields manually in a service or controller — always delegate to a `@Mapper` class\n- Wrap multi-step operations in `@Transactional`\n- Never call another module's `@Service` directly — use REST or Events (exception: `admin/` read-only aggregation)\n- **CRITICAL: All external system calls go ONLY through ACL facade services from `shared/`. Never call Eventbrite or OpenAI HTTP directly. See \"Eventbrite ACL Facades\" section above in \"Coding Standards\".**\n\n```java\n// ✅ Correct service\n@Service\n@RequiredArgsConstructor\n@Slf4j\npublic class BookingService {\n\n    private final BookingRepository bookingRepository;\n    private final ApplicationEventPublisher eventPublisher;\n    private final EbOrderService ebOrderService; // ACL facade from shared/eventbrite/service/\n\n    @Transactional\n    public Booking confirmBooking(UUID bookingId) {\n        // 1. Load the entity — throw if not found\n        Booking booking = bookingRepository.findById(bookingId)\n            .orElseThrow(() -> new BookingNotFoundException(bookingId));\n\n        // 2. Delegate to domain — domain enforces the rule\n        booking.confirm();\n\n        // 3. Persist\n        bookingRepository.save(booking);\n\n        // 4. Publish event — other modules react asynchronously\n        eventPublisher.publishEvent(new BookingConfirmedEvent(\n            booking.getId(), booking.getUserId(), booking.getShowSlotId()));\n\n        log.info(\"Booking confirmed. bookingId={} userId={}\", bookingId, booking.getUserId());\n        return booking;\n    }\n\n    @Transactional\n    public Booking initiateBooking(CreateBookingCommand command) {\n        // 5. Checkout happens via Eventbrite JS widget; backend only reads order after callback\n        EbOrder order = ebOrderService.getOrderById(command.eventbriteOrderId());\n\n        Booking booking = new Booking(command.userId(), command.cartId(), order.id());\n        bookingRepository.save(booking);\n\n        eventPublisher.publishEvent(new BookingInitiatedEvent(booking.getId(), booking.getUserId()));\n        return booking;\n    }\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Repository Layer (`repository/`)\n\nSpring Data JPA interfaces. No business logic. No service calls. No event publishing.\n\n**Rules:**\n- Extend `JpaRepository<Entity, UUID>` — always use UUID primary keys\n- Custom queries use `@Query` with JPQL — avoid native SQL unless absolutely necessary\n- No other module imports this interface\n- Projection interfaces for read-only queries that return partial data\n\n```java\n// ✅ Correct repository\npublic interface BookingRepository extends JpaRepository<Booking, UUID> {\n\n    // JPQL — preferred over native SQL\n    @Query(\"SELECT b FROM Booking b WHERE b.userId = :userId ORDER BY b.createdAt DESC\")\n    List<Booking> findAllByUserIdOrderByCreatedAtDesc(@Param(\"userId\") UUID userId);\n\n    // Paginated query — use Page<T> for any list endpoint exposed to the API\n    Page<Booking> findByUserId(UUID userId, Pageable pageable);\n\n    // Projection for read-only use case\n    Page<BookingSummaryProjection> findByShowSlotId(UUID showSlotId, Pageable pageable);\n\n    // Existence check — more efficient than findById\n    boolean existsByUserIdAndShowSlotId(UUID userId, UUID showSlotId);\n}\n```\n\n### Pagination Rules\n\n- **Always use `Page<T>` and `Pageable` for any list endpoint** exposed via the API — never return unbounded `List<T>` from a controller.\n- Accept `Pageable` as a controller parameter via `@PageableDefault` — Spring auto-binds `?page=0&size=20&sort=createdAt,desc`.\n- Pass `Pageable` through service → repository unchanged. Never construct a `PageRequest` inside a service unless the page size is a business rule (e.g. max 8 seats).\n- Return `Page<ResponseDTO>` from the controller, wrapped in `ApiResponse`.\n\n```java\n// ✅ Correct paginated controller\n@GetMapping\n@PreAuthorize(\"hasRole('USER')\")\npublic ResponseEntity<ApiResponse<Page<BookingSummaryResponse>>> listBookings(\n        @PageableDefault(size = 20, sort = \"createdAt\", direction = Sort.Direction.DESC) Pageable pageable) {\n    Page<Booking> page = bookingService.listBookings(pageable);\n    return ResponseEntity.ok(ApiResponse.success(page.map(bookingMapper::toSummaryResponse)));\n}\n\n// ✅ Correct paginated service method\n@Transactional(readOnly = true)\npublic Page<Booking> listBookings(Pageable pageable) {\n    return bookingRepository.findAll(pageable);\n}\n\n// ❌ Wrong — unbounded list; will break under load\npublic List<Booking> listBookings() {\n    return bookingRepository.findAll();\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Mapper Layer (`mapper/`)\n\nMapStruct interfaces. Pure translation — no business logic, no service calls, no conditions\nother than null checks.\n\n**Rules:**\n- Annotate with `@Mapper(componentModel = \"spring\")`\n- Always declare `@Mapping` for fields with different names\n- Never call a service from inside a mapper\n- Never apply business rules inside a mapper\n- Test every mapper in isolation — instantiate via `Mappers.getMapper()`\n\n```java\n// ✅ Correct mapper\n@Mapper(componentModel = \"spring\")\npublic interface BookingMapper {\n\n    @Mapping(source = \"showSlotId\", target = \"slotId\")\n    @Mapping(source = \"status\", target = \"bookingStatus\")\n    BookingResponse toResponse(Booking booking);\n\n    @Mapping(target = \"id\", ignore = true)           // DB generates the ID\n    @Mapping(target = \"createdAt\", ignore = true)    // BaseEntity sets this\n    @Mapping(target = \"updatedAt\", ignore = true)\n    @Mapping(target = \"status\", constant = \"INITIATED\")\n    Booking toDomain(CreateBookingRequest request);\n}\n\n// ❌ Wrong — business logic inside a mapper\n@Mapper(componentModel = \"spring\")\npublic interface BookingMapper {\n    default BookingResponse toResponse(Booking booking) {\n        if (booking.getStatus() == BookingStatus.CANCELLED) {\n            // applying logic — wrong layer\n            return new BookingResponse(booking.getId(), \"REFUNDED\");\n        }\n        ...\n    }\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Controller Layer (`api/controller/`)\n\nHandles HTTP. Validates input. Delegates to service. Maps result to response DTO. Nothing else.\n\n**Rules:**\n- Annotate with `@RestController` and `@RequestMapping`\n- Use `@PreAuthorize` for role-based access — never check roles in service or domain\n- Validate request body with `@Valid` — never manually validate in controller code\n- Call service, pass the result to the mapper, return the response — that is all\n- Never catch exceptions in the controller — let `GlobalExceptionHandler` handle them\n- Return `ResponseEntity<ApiResponse<T>>` for all responses\n- Never define `@ExceptionHandler` in a controller — use `GlobalExceptionHandler` only\n\n```java\n"
  }
}

## Entry 37
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-04T17:23:21.427Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T17:23:21.427Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_y7wZHmgacVaDpD7izgv7Mdtc",
    "output": "Chunk ID: eb16f9\nWall time: 0.0512 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 2686\nOutput:\n│   ├── BookingTest.java\n│   ├── RefundTest.java\n│   └── ETicketTest.java\n├── service/\n│   ├── PaymentServiceTest.java\n│   ├── CancellationServiceTest.java\n│   ├── RefundServiceTest.java\n│   └── ETicketServiceTest.java\n├── api/\n│   ├── PaymentControllerTest.java\n│   ├── BookingControllerTest.java\n│   ├── ETicketControllerTest.java\n│   ├── StripeWebhookControllerTest.java\n│   └── InternalPaymentControllerTest.java (extend existing stub)\n└── arch/\n    └── PaymentsTicketingArchTest.java\n```\n\n### Shared Events (all exist — no new files)\n\n| Event | Direction | Shared File |\n|---|---|---|\n| `CartAssembledEvent` | Consumed | ✅ `shared/common/event/published/CartAssembledEvent.java` |\n| `BookingConfirmedEvent` | Published | ✅ `shared/common/event/published/BookingConfirmedEvent.java` |\n| `PaymentFailedEvent` | Published | ✅ `shared/common/event/published/PaymentFailedEvent.java` |\n| `BookingCancelledEvent` | Published | ✅ `shared/common/event/published/BookingCancelledEvent.java` |\n\n---\n\n## Stage 4 — DB Schema\n\nAll 6 tables were created by pre-FR5 migrations (V26–V31). One new migration V32 fixes `e_tickets.status` default and adds the `UNIQUE (booking_item_id)` constraint.\n\n---\n\n### `bookings` — V26 (no changes)\n\n```sql\nbookings (\n  id                       BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,\n  booking_ref              VARCHAR(50)  NOT NULL UNIQUE,   -- BK-{YYYYMMDD}-{seq}\n  cart_id                  BIGINT       NOT NULL,          -- FK → carts.id (booking-inventory, cross-module ID)\n  user_id                  BIGINT       NOT NULL,          -- FK → users.id (identity, cross-module ID)\n  event_id                 BIGINT       NOT NULL,          -- FK → event_catalog.id\n  slot_id                  BIGINT       NOT NULL,          -- FK → show_slots.id\n  status                   VARCHAR(30)  NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING',\n                                                           -- PENDING | CONFIRMED | CANCELLATION_PENDING | CANCELLED\n  stripe_payment_intent_id VARCHAR(255),\n  stripe_charge_id         VARCHAR(255),\n  total_amount             BIGINT       NOT NULL,          -- smallest currency unit (paise for INR)\n  currency                 VARCHAR(10)  NOT NULL DEFAULT 'INR',\n  created_at               TIMESTAMPTZ  DEFAULT NOW(),\n  updated_at               TIMESTAMPTZ  DEFAULT NOW()\n)\nINDEXES: idx_bookings_user_id, idx_bookings_cart_id, idx_bookings_booking_ref\n```\n\n**Lifecycle note:** Created as `PENDING` when `CartAssembledEvent` arrives. Promoted to `CONFIRMED` after `retrievePaymentIntent` returns `succeeded`. Transitions to `CANCELLATION_PENDING` when buyer requests cancel. Transitions to `CANCELLED` after refund finalises.\n\n---\n\n### `booking_items` — V27 (no changes)\n\n```sql\nbooking_items (\n  id              BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,\n  booking_id      BIGINT       NOT NULL REFERENCES bookings(id),\n  seat_id         BIGINT,                         -- nullable: null for GA items\n  ga_claim_id     BIGINT,                         -- nullable: null for reserved seats\n  ticket_class_id VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,           -- eb_ticket_class_id from pricing tier\n  unit_price      BIGINT       NOT NULL,           -- smallest currency unit\n  currency        VARCHAR(10)  NOT NULL DEFAULT 'INR',\n  status          VARCHAR(30)  NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ACTIVE',   -- ACTIVE | CANCELLED\n  created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ  DEFAULT NOW()\n)\nINDEXES: idx_booking_items_booking_id\n```\n\n---\n\n### `payments` — V28 (no changes)\n\n```sql\npayments (\n  id                       BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,\n  booking_id               BIGINT       NOT NULL REFERENCES bookings(id),\n  stripe_payment_intent_id VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL UNIQUE,\n  stripe_charge_id         VARCHAR(255),          -- latest_charge from Stripe; filled on succeed\n  amount                   BIGINT       NOT NULL,\n  currency                 VARCHAR(10)  NOT NULL DEFAULT 'inr',\n  status                   VARCHAR(30)  NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING', -- PENDING | SUCCESS | FAILED\n  failure_code             VARCHAR(100),\n  failure_message          TEXT,\n  created_at               TIMESTAMPTZ  DEFAULT NOW(),\n  updated_at               TIMESTAMPTZ  DEFAULT NOW()\n)\nINDEXES: idx_payments_booking_id, idx_payments_stripe_payment_intent_id\n```\n\n**Note:** `booking_id` is populated immediately because a `PENDING` `Booking` shell is always created before calling Stripe (Option B from Stage 4 outline).\n\n---\n\n### `e_tickets` — V29 + V32 patch\n\n```sql\ne_tickets (\n  id              BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,\n  booking_id      BIGINT NOT NULL REFERENCES bookings(id),\n  booking_item_id BIGINT NOT NULL REFERENCES booking_items(id),\n  qr_code_data    TEXT   NOT NULL,    -- Base64(\"{bookingRef}:{bookingItemId}\")\n  pdf_url         TEXT,               -- object storage URL; nullable until generated\n  status          VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ACTIVE',  -- ACTIVE | VOIDED  (V32 fixes 'VALID')\n  issued_at       TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()\n)\nINDEXES: idx_e_tickets_booking_id\nCONSTRAINT: UNIQUE (booking_item_id)   -- one ticket per item; added by V32\n```\n\n---\n\n### `refunds` — V30 (no changes)\n\n```sql\nrefunds (\n  id               BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,\n  booking_id       BIGINT       NOT NULL REFERENCES bookings(id),\n  stripe_refund_id VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE,\n  amount           BIGINT       NOT NULL,          -- smallest currency unit\n  currency         VARCHAR(10)  NOT NULL DEFAULT 'inr',\n  reason           VARCHAR(100),  -- REQUESTED_BY_CUSTOMER | DUPLICATE | FRAUDULENT\n  status           VARCHAR(30)  NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING', -- PENDING | SUCCEEDED | FAILED\n  created_at       TIMESTAMPTZ  DEFAULT NOW(),\n  updated_at       TIMESTAMPTZ  DEFAULT NOW()\n)\nINDEXES: idx_refunds_booking_id\n```\n\n---\n\n### `cancellation_requests` — V31 (no changes)\n\n```sql\ncancellation_requests (\n  id           BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,\n  booking_id   BIGINT NOT NULL REFERENCES bookings(id),\n  user_id      BIGINT NOT NULL,\n  reason       TEXT,\n  status       VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING', -- PENDING | APPROVED | REJECTED\n  requested_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),\n  resolved_at  TIMESTAMPTZ\n)\nINDEXES: idx_cancellation_requests_booking_id\n```\n\n---\n\n### V32 — New Migration\n\n```sql\n-- V32__fix_e_tickets_status_default_and_unique_item.sql\n\n-- Fix status default from VALID to ACTIVE to match domain enum\nALTER TABLE e_tickets\n    ALTER COLUMN status SET DEFAULT 'ACTIVE';\n\nUPDATE e_tickets SET status = 'ACTIVE' WHERE status = 'VALID';\n\n-- Enforce one ticket per booking item\nALTER TABLE e_tickets\n    ADD CONSTRAINT uq_e_tickets_booking_item_id UNIQUE (booking_item_id);\n```\n\n---\n\n## Stage 5 — API\n\n### `GET /api/v1/payments/checkout/{cartId}`\n\n**Auth:** JWT `ROLE_USER`\n**Purpose:** Called immediately after cart confirm. Returns Stripe `clientSecret` for frontend Stripe Payment Element initialisation.\n\n**Response `200 OK`:**\n```json\n{\n  \"cartId\": 42,\n  \"bookingRef\": \"BK-20260304-001\",\n  \"paymentIntentId\": \"pi_3MtwBw...\",\n  \"clientSecret\": \"pi_3MtwBw..._secret_...\",\n  \"amountInSmallestUnit\": 150000,\n  \"currency\": \"inr\"\n}\n```\n\n**Errors:**\n- `404 CART_NOT_FOUND`\n- `409 PAYMENT_ALREADY_EXISTS` (idempotent — returns existing intent if already `PENDING`)\n- `400 CART_NOT_CONFIRMED` (cart not in `HARD_LOCKED` state)\n\n---\n\n### `POST /api/v1/payments/confirm`\n\n**Auth:** JWT `ROLE_USER`\n**Purpose:** Frontend calls this after `stripe.confirmPayment()` redirect with the `paymentIntentId`.\n\n**Request:**\n```json\n{ \"paymentIntentId\": \"pi_3MtwBw...\" }\n```\n\n**Response `200 OK`:**\n```json\n{\n  \"bookingRef\": \"BK-20260304-001\",\n  \"status\": \"CONFIRMED\",\n  \"totalAmount\": 150000,\n  \"currency\": \"inr\",\n  \"items\": [\n    {\n      \"bookingItemId\": 1,\n      \"seatId\": 101,\n      \"ticketClassId\": \"TC-001\",\n      \"unitPrice\": 75000\n    }\n  ]\n}\n```\n\n**Errors:**\n- `402 PAYMENT_NOT_CONFIRMED` — Stripe status ≠ `succeeded`; includes `stripeStatus` in body\n- `404 PAYMENT_INTENT_NOT_FOUND`\n- `409 BOOKING_ALREADY_CONFIRMED` (idempotent — returns existing `bookingRef`)\n\n---\n\n### `POST /api/v1/payments/failed`\n\n**Auth:** JWT `ROLE_USER`\n**Purpose:** Frontend reports payment failure so seat locks are released immediately (before webhook arrives).\n\n**Request:**\n```json\n{ \"paymentIntentId\": \"pi_3MtwBw...\" }\n```\n\n**Response `200 OK`:**\n```json\n{ \"message\": \"Seat locks released. Cart cleared.\" }\n```\n\n**Errors:** `404 PAYMENT_INTENT_NOT_FOUND`\n\n---\n\n### `GET /api/v1/bookings?page=0&size=10`\n\n**Auth:** JWT `ROLE_USER`\n**Purpose:** Paginated booking history for the calling user.\n\n**Response `200 OK`:**\n```json\n{\n  \"content\": [\n    {\n      \"bookingRef\": \"BK-20260304-001\",\n      \"slotId\": 5,\n      \"status\": \"CONFIRMED\",\n      \"totalAmount\": 150000,\n      \"currency\": \"inr\",\n      \"createdAt\": \"2026-03-04T10:00:00Z\"\n    }\n  ],\n  \"page\": 0,\n  \"size\": 10,\n  \"totalElements\": 1\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### `GET /api/v1/bookings/{bookingRef}`\n\n**Auth:** JWT `ROLE_USER`, must own booking\n**Purpose:** Full booking detail with line items and e-ticket stubs.\n\n**Response `200 OK`:**\n```json\n{\n  \"bookingRef\": \"BK-20260304-001\",\n  \"status\": \"CONFIRMED\",\n  \"slotId\": 5,\n  \"totalAmount\": 150000,\n  \"currency\": \"inr\",\n  \"stripePaymentIntentId\": \"pi_3MtwBw...\",\n  \"items\": [\n    {\n      \"bookingItemId\": 1,\n      \"seatId\": 101,\n      \"ticketClassId\": \"TC-001\",\n      \"unitPrice\": 75000,\n      \"status\": \"ACTIVE\",\n      \"eTicket\": {\n        \"ticketCode\": \"BK-20260304-001:1\",\n        \"qrCodeData\": \"QlItMjAyNjAzMDQtMDAxOjE=\",\n        \"pdfUrl\": \"/tickets/BK-20260304-001/1.pdf\",\n        \"status\": \"ACTIVE\"\n      }\n    }\n  ]\n}\n```\n\n**Errors:**\n- `404 BOOKING_NOT_FOUND` (also returned when booking belongs to different user — never reveal existence)\n- `403 FORBIDDEN` omitted in favour of `404` to prevent enumeration\n\n---\n\n### `POST /api/v1/bookings/{bookingRef}/cancel`\n\n**Auth:** JWT `ROLE_USER`, must own booking\n**Purpose:** Buyer-initiated cancellation — triggers Stripe refund.\n\n**Request:**\n```json\n{ \"reason\": \"REQUESTED_BY_CUSTOMER\" }\n```\n\n**Response `200 OK`:**\n```json\n{\n  \"bookingRef\": \"BK-20260304-001\",\n  \"status\": \"CANCELLATION_PENDING\",\n  \"refund\": {\n    \"stripeRefundId\": \"re_1Nispe...\",\n    \"amount\": 150000,\n    \"currency\": \"inr\",\n    \"status\": \"PENDING\"\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**Errors:**\n- `404 BOOKING_NOT_FOUND`\n- `409 CANCELLATION_NOT_ALLOWED` — already cancelled, wrong status, or outside window; includes `reason` and `windowClosedAt` where applicable\n\n---\n\n### `GET /api/v1/tickets/{bookingRef}`\n\n**Auth:** JWT `ROLE_USER`, must own booking\n**Purpose:** Returns all e-tickets for a booking for display and download.\n\n**Response `200 OK`:**\n```json\n{\n  \"bookingRef\": \"BK-20260304-001\",\n  \"tickets\": [\n    {\n      \"ticketCode\": \"BK-20260304-001:1\",\n"
  }
}

```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T17:23:21.432Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_caoG2ZL50zpyDhLRsbIBSLGu",
    "output": "Chunk ID: cbaba6\nWall time: 0.0507 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 3259\nOutput:\n# Modular Monolith — Canonical Folder Structure Template\n\n> **Purpose:** This document is the canonical reference for building any new feature or module\n> in this codebase. Every agent, developer, or tool that adds code MUST follow this structure\n> exactly. It enforces high cohesion, low coupling, and zero cyclic dependencies by design.\n\n---\n\n## Core Architectural Rules (Read Before Writing Any Code)\n\n### Rule 1 — Dependency Direction is One-Way and Downward Only\n```\nshared/          ← no dependencies on any module (foundational)\n    ↓\n[module-A]       ← may depend on shared only\n    ↓\n[module-B]       ← may depend on shared + module-A\n    ↓\n[module-C]       ← may depend on shared + module-A + module-B\n    ↓\nadmin/           ← thin orchestration layer, depends on all modules but owns no domain\n    ↓\napp/             ← entry point only, no business logic\n```\n**A module NEVER imports from a module below it or at the same level.**\n**If two modules need to talk, they do so through Spring Events — never direct service calls.**\n\n### Rule 2 — Three Ways Modules Communicate (Pick the Right One)\n| Communication Type | When to Use | Mechanism |\n|---|---|---|\n| Query (sync, read-only) | Module B needs data that Module A owns | Module B calls Module A's **public API endpoint** (HTTP), never imports Module A's service class directly |\n| Command (async, fire-and-forget) | Module A completed something and others should react | Module A publishes a **Spring ApplicationEvent**; interested modules register a listener |\n| Shared contract | Enums, base classes, standard DTOs used by 2+ modules | Lives in `shared/common/` only — never duplicated |\n\n### Rule 3 — The ACL is the Only Door to External Systems\nAny external API (Eventbrite, payment gateway, SMS provider, etc.) MUST be accessed\nexclusively through the `shared/` ACL package. No module ever calls an external API directly.\nIf a new external integration is needed, it goes in `shared/` first, then modules consume it\nvia the ACL facade services.\n\n### Rule 4 — A Module Owns Its Own Database Tables\nNo two modules share a JPA entity or database table. If Module B needs data from Module A's\ntable, it calls Module A's API — it does NOT import Module A's `@Entity` class or `@Repository`.\nViolating this rule creates hidden coupling at the database layer.\n\n### Rule 5 — The `admin/` Module Owns No Domain\nThe admin module only orchestrates and aggregates. It delegates all writes to the owning module.\nIt never defines its own `@Entity` or owns a database table. It is a read + orchestration layer.\n\nFor inter-module access, admin follows this rule:\n- **Reads** — admin MAY import a module's `@Service` bean directly (same JVM, no overhead)\n- **Writes** — admin MUST call the owning module's REST API (never bypass the module's own validation, events, and business rules)\n\n### Rule 6 — Mapping Logic Belongs in the Mapper Layer Only\nConverting domain objects to API response DTOs and request DTOs to domain objects must be done\nexclusively in the `mapper/` package. Controllers and services must never call `.builder()` on a\nresponse DTO directly or manually map fields.\n- **Controllers** — call service, return result. No mapping.\n- **Services** — orchestrate domain + infra. No mapping.\n- **Mappers** — the only place field translation happens. Use MapStruct.\n\n---\n\n## Project Layout\n\n```\n[project-root]/\n├── pom.xml                    ← Parent POM. All dependency versions managed here only.\n├── shared/                    ← Internal library. Never imports from any module.\n├── [module-name]/             ← One folder per bounded context.\n├── admin/                     ← Thin orchestration layer. Owns no domain, no tables.\n└── app/                       ← Spring Boot entry point. Zero business logic.\n```\n\n---\n\n## shared/ Structure\n\nCross-cutting concerns only. Every module depends on this.\n\n```\nshared/\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/shared/\n    ├── [external-system-acl]/     ← One package per external system (e.g. razorpay/, eventbrite/)\n    │   ├── client/                ← Raw HTTP calls only\n    │   ├── config/                ← WebClient bean, rate limiter config\n    │   ├── dto/request/           ← External system's raw request shapes\n    │   ├── dto/response/          ← External system's raw response shapes\n    │   ├── mapper/                ← Translates external DTOs → internal domain objects\n    │   ├── service/               ← THE ONLY THING MODULES CALL. One service per capability.\n    │   ├── webhook/               ← Receives inbound webhooks, dispatches as Spring Events\n    │   └── resilience/            ← Circuit breaker + fallback config per external system\n    │\n    ├── security/\n    │   ├── JwtTokenProvider       ← Generate + validate JWTs\n    │   ├── JwtAuthenticationFilter← Spring Security filter, registered once\n    │   └── SecurityConstants      ← Token TTLs, header names, role constants\n    │\n    ├── common/\n    │   ├── domain/\n    │   │   ├── BaseEntity         ← id, createdAt, updatedAt — all entities extend this\n    │   │   └── Money              ← Value object for all monetary amounts. Never use primitives.\n    │   ├── dto/\n    │   │   ├── ApiResponse        ← Standard success response wrapper\n    │   │   ├── PageResponse       ← Paginated list wrapper\n    │   │   └── ErrorResponse      ← Standard error shape\n    │   ├── enums/                 ← Enums shared across 2+ modules only\n    │   └── exception/\n    │       ├── GlobalExceptionHandler  ← @ControllerAdvice. One handler for the entire app.\n    │       ├── ResourceNotFoundException   ← 404\n    │       ├── BusinessRuleException       ← 422\n    │       └── IntegrationException        ← 502\n    │\n    └── config/\n        ├── RedisConfig            ← Shared Redis bean\n        ├── ElasticsearchConfig    ← Shared ES client bean\n        ├── CacheConfig            ← Cache names and TTLs\n        ├── AsyncConfig            ← Thread pool for @Async\n        ├── OpenApiConfig          ← Springdoc global config\n        ├── WebConfig              ← Global CORS + request logging. Never use @CrossOrigin on controllers.\n        └── TracingConfig          ← MDC traceId on every log line. Essential for prod debugging.\n```\n\n---\n\n## [module-name]/ Structure\n\nOne folder per bounded context. Name reflects the domain, not the tech.\n\n```\n[module-name]/\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/[module]/\n    ├── api/\n    │   ├── controller/            ← HTTP endpoints only. No business logic. Delegates to service.\n    │   └── dto/\n    │       ├── request/           ← [Action][Resource]Request\n    │       └── response/          ← [Resource]Response. Flat, serializable, no JPA proxies.\n    │\n    ├── domain/                    ← Pure business logic. No Spring, no JPA, no infrastructure.\n    │                                Entities have behavior (confirm(), cancel()) not just getters.\n    │\n    ├── service/                   ← Orchestrates domain + repositories + ACL + events. @Service beans.\n    │                                One service per capability group. Methods = use cases not CRUD.\n    │\n    ├── repository/                ← @Repository interfaces only. No other module imports these.\n    │\n    ├── mapper/                    ← Domain ↔ DTO translation only. Use MapStruct.\n    │\n    ├── event/\n    │   ├── published/             ← Events this module fires. Past tense. Primitives/IDs only.\n    │   └── listener/              ← Events this module reacts to. No logic — delegate to service.\n    │\n    ├── statemachine/              ← OPTIONAL. Only if 4+ states with conditional transition guards.\n    │   ├── [Resource]StateMachineConfig\n    │   ├── [Resource]State\n    │   ├── [Resource]Event\n    │   ├── [Resource]StateMachineService\n    │   ├── guard/\n    │   └── action/\n    │\n    └── exception/                 ← OPTIONAL. Module-specific exceptions only.\n```\n\n---\n\n## admin/ and app/\n\n```\nadmin/\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/admin/\n    ├── api/controller/            ← Admin-only endpoints (@PreAuthorize ROLE_ADMIN)\n    └── service/                   ← Aggregates reads across modules. Delegates writes to module APIs.\n\napp/\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/\n    ├── [ProjectName]Application   ← @SpringBootApplication. One class, nothing else.\n    └── resources/\n        ├── application.yml\n        ├── application-local.yml\n        ├── application-prod.yml\n        └── db/migration/          ← Flyway migrations. Global version sequence. Never modify existing files.\n```\n\n---\n\n## Anti-Patterns — Never Do These\n\n| Anti-Pattern | Why It's Wrong | Correct Approach |\n|---|---|---|\n| Module A imports `@Service` from Module B | Direct coupling — changes in B break A at compile time | Module A calls Module B's REST endpoint or listens to its Spring Event |\n| Module A imports `@Entity` or `@Repository` from Module B | Hidden DB coupling — schema changes in B break A silently | Module A calls Module B's API to get the data it needs |\n| Putting Eventbrite/Razorpay HTTP calls inside a module's service | Scattered integration — if the API changes, you hunt across modules | All external calls go through `shared/[system-acl]/service/` facades only |\n| Sharing a database table between two modules | Breaks module autonomy — both modules must coordinate schema changes | Each module owns its own tables; sync via events or API calls |\n| Putting business logic in a `@RestController` | Untestable and leaks domain logic into the HTTP layer | Controller delegates immediately to service; service owns all logic |\n| Creating an `[Entity]Service` that just wraps CRUD | Anemic domain model — no real encapsulation | Put behavior on the entity itself; service orchestrates multi-step use cases |\n| Publishing a Spring Event that contains a JPA `@Entity` | Entity may be a lazy-loaded proxy; causes serialization failures | Events carry only primitive values, IDs, or immutable value objects |\n| Adding a new enum to `shared/common/enums/` for a single-module concept | Pollutes shared space with module-specific concerns | Single-module enums stay inside that module's `domain/` package |\n| Writing business logic in `admin/` | Admin owns no domain; it becomes a second copy of truth | Admin delegates writes to the owning module's REST API; only reads and aggregates |\n| Admin calling a module's `@Service` for writes | Bypasses the module's own validation, events, and business rules | Admin must call the owning module's REST API for all write operations |\n| Mapping fields inside a `@Service` or `@RestController` | Pollutes orchestration/HTTP layers with translation logic; hard to test | All domain ↔ DTO mapping goes in `mapper/[Resource]Mapper` only |\n| Using `@CrossOrigin` on individual controllers | Scattered CORS config — inconsistent across modules | Define CORS once in `shared/config/WebConfig` |\n\n---\n\n## Event Naming Conventions\n\n```\nPublished events  →  Past tense + \"Event\" suffix\n                     [WhatHappened]Event\n                     e.g. BookingConfirmedEvent\n                          SeatReleasedEvent\n                          UserRegisteredEvent\n                          PaymentFailedEvent\n\nListeners         →  [TriggerEvent]Listener\n                     e.g. PaymentFailedListener (inside booking-inventory module)\n                          BookingConfirmedListener (inside engagement module)\n                          CartAssembledListener (inside payments module)\n\nEvent payload     →  Only primitives, IDs, enums, and value objects.\n                     NEVER include @Entity objects in events.\n                     NEVER include mutable collections.\n```\n\n---\n\n## Module pom.xml Dependency Rules\n\n```xml\n<!-- Every module pom.xml follows this exact pattern -->\n<dependencies>\n\n  <!-- 1. Always: shared internal library -->\n  <dependency>\n    <groupId>com.[org]</groupId>\n    <artifactId>shared</artifactId>\n  </dependency>\n\n  <!-- 2. Only upstream modules (modules this one reads from) -->\n  <!-- Example: booking-inventory reads from discovery-catalog -->\n  <dependency>\n    <groupId>com.[org]</groupId>\n    <artifactId>[upstream-module]</artifactId>\n  </dependency>\n\n  <!-- 3. NEVER depend on a peer or downstream module -->\n  <!-- NEVER depend on admin/ -->\n  <!-- NEVER depend on app/ -->\n\n</dependencies>\n```\n\n---\n\n## Flyway Migration Naming\n\n```\nV[auto-increment]__[verb]_[table_name].sql\n\nExamples:\n"
  }
}
```

## Entry 45
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-04T17:23:25.639Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T17:23:25.639Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_wEEflXW4nlUqD3FKYEfpmFVR",
    "output": "Chunk ID: bb745b\nWall time: 0.0507 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 1603\nOutput:\n5:**Last Updated:** March 2026 — FR5 updated: Stripe replaces Eventbrite Checkout Widget for payments and refunds\n11:⚠ **Important:** This is the single source of truth for module ownership, boundaries, inter-module communication, and the Eventbrite ACL. Every agent and developer MUST read this before touching any module.\n25:| payments-ticketing | Bookings, Payments (Stripe), E-Tickets, Cancellations, Refunds (Stripe), Wallet | Seats (reads cart only) |\n30:- `shared/` — owns all external integrations (Eventbrite ACL), JWT, base classes, common DTOs.\n33:## 2. Module Dependency Order\n48:payments-ticketing    ← depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (reads via REST)\n52:engagement            ← depends on shared + payments-ticketing + identity (reads via REST)\n83:- payments-ticketing → listens `CartAssembledListener` → initiates payment\n85:- payments-ticketing → publishes `PaymentFailedEvent`\n88:- payments-ticketing → publishes `BookingConfirmedEvent`\n97:## 4. Eventbrite ACL — Facades in `shared/`\n117:  - Retrieve order details for org-level reporting only. **NOT used in FR5 or FR6 payment flows** — Stripe owns all payment and order management. Retained only for organizer-side admin reporting and orphan detection.\n120:  - Retrieve attendee records; list attendees by event. Used in FR8 (attendance verification for reviews) only. **No longer used in FR5 payment flows** — Stripe and internal DB own all booking records.\n126:  - **Inactive for FR5/FR6.** All refunds are now processed via `StripeRefundService`. Retained in code for legacy compatibility only. Do NOT call for any new refund flow.\n131:> **Note:** `EbOrderService` is **not used in FR5 (payment) or FR6 (refund) flows**. Stripe owns all payment, order, and refund processing. `EbOrderService` is retained only for organizer-side admin reporting.\n141:All Stripe-related calls go through these three facades in `shared/stripe/service/`. No module calls Stripe HTTP directly.\n143:- **StripePaymentService** (`shared/stripe/service/`)\n148:- **StripeRefundService** (`shared/stripe/service/`)\n153:- **StripeWebhookHandler** (`shared/stripe/service/`)\n158:> **Stripe Sandbox:** Use `sk_test_...` / `pk_test_...` keys. Test card `4242 4242 4242 4242` for success, `4000 0000 0000 0002` for decline, `4000 0000 0000 7726` for async refund simulation. See `docs/FR5-reworked-v2.md` and `docs/stripe-api-reference.md` for full test card matrix.\n208:**Core Architecture:** Your internal DB is the primary source of truth. **Stripe is the payment and refund backbone** (FR5, FR6). **Eventbrite is a passive event listing sync layer** — it hosts the public event listing, tracks ticket class capacity, and fires organizer-side webhooks (`event.updated`, `event.unpublished`). Every entity is created internally first — then pushed to Eventbrite for listing purposes. The `eventbrite_event_id` returned from each push is stored in your DB and is the bridge for listing sync.\n225:- FR5 (Payment) — event listing on Eventbrite uses the `event_id`; Stripe drives the actual payment, so EB checkout widget is **not required** for payment to succeed, but EB passive sync still depends on `eventbrite_event_id` being valid\n231:Without FR2 sync: If `eventbrite_event_id` is null or orphaned, EB listing sync and capacity tracking break. However, payments (FR5) and refunds (FR6 buyer-initiated) are fully functional through Stripe regardless.\n394:**Publishes:** `ShowSlotCreatedEvent` → booking-inventory | `ShowSlotCancelledEvent` → booking-inventory + payments-ticketing\n452:  - `CartAssembledEvent` published → payments-ticketing listens\n479:**Publishes:** `CartAssembledEvent` → payments-ticketing\n481:### FR5 — Payment via Stripe → Booking Confirmation → E-Ticket Generation\n482:**Module:** payments-ticketing\n496:  - `CartAssembledEvent` received by `payments-ticketing`\n498:  - Backend calls `StripePaymentService.createPaymentIntent()` via `shared/stripe/service/`\n501:- Stripe API Call (via `shared/stripe/` ACL)\n531:- Stripe API Call (via `shared/stripe/` ACL)\n563:Register once in Stripe Dashboard (Test mode) at `POST /api/webhooks/stripe`. Handled by `StripeWebhookHandler` in `shared/stripe/service/`.\n627:**Module:** payments-ticketing\n644:  - Calls `StripeRefundService.createRefund()` via `shared/stripe/service/`\n645:- Stripe API Call (via `shared/stripe/` ACL)\n678:  - payments-ticketing iterates all `CONFIRMED` bookings for that `event_id`\n723:  - Discounted `amount` is then used when creating the Stripe `PaymentIntent` (Step 1 of FR5)\n731:  - Discounted total used as `amount` in `POST /v1/payment_intents` during FR5 Step 1\n733:- Stripe API Call: (via FR5 path) `POST /v1/payment_intents` with discounted `amount`\n758:  - REST call to payments-ticketing to verify booking status\n879:- Stripe API Call (via `shared/stripe/` ACL)\n966:| booking-inventory | `CartAssembledEvent` | payments-ticketing | Cart ready → create Stripe PaymentIntent + return `client_secret` to frontend |\n967:| payments-ticketing | `PaymentFailedEvent` | booking-inventory | Payment failed (Stripe decline/cancel) → rollback Redis seat locks |\n968:| payments-ticketing | `BookingConfirmedEvent` | engagement | Booking confirmed (Stripe succeeded) → unlock review eligibility |\n969:| payments-ticketing | `BookingConfirmedEvent` | identity | Booking confirmed (Stripe succeeded) → update user order history |\n970:| payments-ticketing | `BookingCancelledEvent` | booking-inventory | Cancellation → release Redis seat locks |\n973:| scheduling | `ShowSlotCancelledEvent` | payments-ticketing | Slot cancelled → bulk Stripe refunds + EB event cancel (passive) |\n984:| `shared/stripe/` | Stripe API | `StripePaymentService`, `StripeRefundService`, `StripeWebhookHandler` |\n1009:## 9. Hard Rules — Never Violate These\n1014:4. No module calls Eventbrite HTTP directly — only through `shared/eventbrite/service/`. No module calls Stripe HTTP directly — only through `shared/stripe/service/`.\n1020:10. `eventbrite_event_id` is never null in production for events that require EB listing sync — if null, block FR7, FR8, FR10 (EB availability check). FR5 (Stripe payments) and FR6 (Stripe refunds) operate independently of `eventbrite_event_id`.\n1045:| New Stripe API call | `shared/stripe/service/` — add to `StripePaymentService`, `StripeRefundService`, or `StripeWebhookHandler` only |\n"
  }
}
```
