## Entry 46
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-04T17:23:25.640Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T17:23:25.640Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_eDn6X0PULms4hqXbxA3FBPrT",
    "output": "Chunk ID: ebc7c7\nWall time: 0.0514 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 2254\nOutput:\n# Eventbrite Integration Strategy\n\n**Owner:** Lalit  \n**Domain:** EntertainmentTech  \n**Last Updated:** March 2026  \n**Status:** Partially Implemented — See Constraints Section\n\n---\n\n## Overview\n\nEventbrite serves as the **ticketing and payment backbone** for the multi-city event booking platform. The architecture follows an **internal-first approach**: your database is the source of truth, and Eventbrite is pushed to after internal validation.\n\n**Core Principle:** Eventbrite does not create orders or users. It only provides ticketing infrastructure, payment processing through its checkout widget, and attendee records post-purchase.\n\n---\n\n## 1. Eventbrite ACL Facades (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n\n### Available Facades\n\n| Facade | Responsibilities | Status |\n|---|---|---|\n| **EbEventSyncService** | Create, update, publish, unpublish, cancel, delete events; pull org event catalog; verify sync integrity | ✅ Designed |\n| **EbVenueService** | Create and update venues on Eventbrite; list venues by org | ✅ Designed |\n| **EbScheduleService** | Create recurring event schedules and occurrences | ✅ Designed |\n| **EbTicketService** | Create/update ticket classes; manage inventory tiers; check availability | ✅ Designed |\n| **EbCapacityService** | Retrieve and update event capacity tiers; manage seat maps | ✅ Designed |\n| **EbOrderService** | Retrieve order details post-checkout; list orders by event/org | ✅ Designed |\n| **EbAttendeeService** | Retrieve attendee records; list attendees by event or org | ✅ Designed |\n| **EbDiscountSyncService** | Create, update, delete discount codes on Eventbrite; sync with internal promotions | ✅ Designed |\n| **EbRefundService** | Read refund request status on orders; filter orders by refund status | ✅ Designed |\n| **EbWebhookService** | Register and manage org-scoped webhooks for event/order state changes | ✅ Designed |\n\n**Hard Rule:** No module ever calls Eventbrite directly. All API calls go through these facades in `shared/`.\n\n---\n\n## 2. The Spine: `eventbrite_event_id`\n\nThe `eventbrite_event_id` is the bridge between your internal DB and Eventbrite. Every downstream flow depends on it:\n\n- **FR2 (Scheduling)** — Create show slot → Store `eventbrite_event_id`\n- **FR4 (Seats)** — Validate availability against Eventbrite `ticket_classes` for that `event_id`\n- **FR5 (Payment)** — Eventbrite Checkout Widget needs valid `event_id` to render\n- **FR6 (Cancellation)** — Refund APIs reference `event_id` + `order_id`\n- **FR7 (Discounts)** — Discount codes scoped to `event_id`\n- **FR8 (Reviews)** — Attendance verification uses `event_id` + attendee email\n\n**Without FR2 sync:** If `eventbrite_event_id` is null or orphaned, the entire chain breaks.\n\n---\n\n## 3. Integration Flows — Planned vs. Constraints\n\n### FR1: City Selection → Venue Discovery → Event Listing\n\n**What Was Supposed to Happen:**\n```\nUser selects city \n  ↓\nApp queries internal venues by city_id\n  ↓\nGET /organizations/{org_id}/venues/ [Eventbrite]\n  ↓\nMerge internal event_catalog with Eventbrite org events\n  ↓\nUser sees unified event list\n```\n\n**Eventbrite Calls:**\n- `GET /organizations/{org_id}/venues/` — list org venues for cross-reference\n- `GET /organizations/{org_id}/events/` — pull org's published events\n- `GET /events/{event_id}/` — fetch individual event details for catalog refresh\n\n**Constraints:** ⚠️ Public event search (`GET /events/search/`) is **deprecated** on Eventbrite. Event discovery is limited to your org's events only. No city-wide public search available.\n\n**Status:** ✅ Designed; ⚠️ Limited to org events\n\n---\n\n### FR2: Show Scheduling → Time Slot Configuration → Conflict Validation\n\n**What Was Supposed to Happen:**\n\n#### Phase 1: Venue Registration\n```\nAdmin registers venue in your app\n  ↓\nPOST /organizations/{org_id}/venues/ [Eventbrite]\n  ↓\nStore eventbrite_venue_id in internal DB\n```\n\n#### Phase 2: Show Slot Creation (Core Sync)\n```\nAdmin creates show slot with venue + date + time\n  ↓\nInternal validation (duplicate check, turnaround gaps)\n  ↓\nPOST /organizations/{org_id}/events/ [Eventbrite]\n  ↓\nEventbrite returns event_id\n  ↓\nStore eventbrite_event_id in show_slots table\n  ↓\nPOST /events/{event_id}/ticket_classes/ [Eventbrite] — create ticket tiers\n  ↓\nPOST /events/{event_id}/publish/ [Eventbrite] — make event live\n```\n\n#### Phase 3: Recurring Shows\n```\nAdmin creates recurring series (e.g., every Saturday)\n  ↓\nInternal DB generates individual show_slot records\n  ↓\nPOST /events/{event_id}/schedules/ [Eventbrite] — create recurring occurrences\n```\n\n#### Phase 4: Status Sync\n```\nAdmin updates/deletes slot\n  ↓\nPOST /events/{event_id}/ [Eventbrite] — sync changes\n  OR\nPOST /events/{event_id}/cancel/ [Eventbrite] — cancel event\n```\n\n#### Phase 5: Conflict Validation (Background Job)\n```\nPeriodic job queries internal show_slots\n  ↓\nGET /events/{event_id}/ [Eventbrite] — verify eventbrite_event_id still exists\n  ↓\nFlags orphaned records for admin review\n```\n\n**Eventbrite Calls:**\n- `POST /organizations/{org_id}/venues/` — create venue\n- `GET /organizations/{org_id}/venues/` — list venues for conflict check\n- `POST /organizations/{org_id}/events/` — create event\n- `POST /events/{event_id}/ticket_classes/` — create ticket tiers\n- `POST /events/{event_id}/publish/` — publish event\n- `POST /events/{event_id}/schedules/` — create recurring occurrences\n- `POST /events/{event_id}/` — update event details\n- `POST /events/{event_id}/cancel/` — cancel event\n- `GET /events/{event_id}/` — verify event exists\n\n**Constraints:** ⚠️ **Turnaround policy enforcement** — Eventbrite has no conflict validation API. Your app must enforce time gaps between shows internally. Conflict validation is 100% internal DB logic.\n\n**Status:** ✅ Designed; ⚠️ Turnaround gaps are internal logic only\n\n---\n\n### FR3: User Registration → Profile Setup → Preference Onboarding\n\n**What Was Supposed to Happen:**\n```\nUser registers (email + password + name)\n  ↓\nInternal users table created\n  ↓\n[No Eventbrite call — no user creation API exists]\n  ↓\nUser fills profile (phone, city, avatar)\n  ↓\n[No Eventbrite sync at this stage]\n  ↓\nUser selects preferences (genres, cities, price ranges)\n  ↓\nPreferences stored in users_preferences table\n  ↓\n[Post-purchase: user linked to Eventbrite via order email]\n```\n\n**Eventbrite Calls:**\n- No Eventbrite call for this flow we will use the atendee sync that happens in later flows\n\n**Constraints:** ⚠️ **No user creation API on Eventbrite.** User identity is 100% internal. The link between your app user and Eventbrite is established post-purchase via the order email. No user profile sync is possible.\n\n**Status:** ✅ Designed; ⚠️ Zero Eventbrite integration at registration time\n\n---\n\n### FR4: Seat Selection → Pricing Tier Validation → Cart Assembly\n\n**What Was Supposed to Happen:**\n```\nUser picks seats from internal SeatMap\n  ↓\nGET /events/{event_id}/ticket_classes/for_sale/ [Eventbrite]\n  ↓\nValidate: ticket count still > 0?\n  ↓\nApply internal pricing tiers (VIP, Premium, General)\n  ↓\nPOST /pricing/calculate_price_for_item/ [Eventbrite] — validate pricing\n  ↓\nSoft lock seat via Redis (TTL = cart session)\n  ↓\nREST call to promotions module — validate coupon eligibility\n  ↓\nCartAssembledEvent published\n```\n\n**Eventbrite Calls:**\n- `GET /events/{event_id}/ticket_classes/for_sale/` — remaining ticket count\n- `GET /events/{event_id}/inventory_tiers/{id}/` — verify tier capacity\n- `POST /pricing/calculate_price_for_item/` — validate price against Eventbrite fees\n- `GET /organizations/{org_id}/ticket_groups/` — check ticket bundles\n\n**Constraints:** ⚠️ **No seat lock API on Eventbrite.** The entire state machine (AVAILABLE → SOFT_LOCKED → HARD_LOCKED → CONFIRMED) is internal using Redis + Spring State Machine. Eventbrite is consulted only once — for availability count.\n\n**Status:** ✅ Designed; ⚠️ Seat locking is 100% internal\n\n---\n\n### FR5: Payment Gateway → Booking Confirmation → E-Ticket Generation\n\n**What Was Supposed to Happen:**\n\n#### Step 1: Checkout Widget Embed\n```\nCartAssembledEvent received by payments-ticketing\n  ↓\nFrontend receives event_id\n  ↓\nRender Eventbrite Checkout Widget (JS SDK: eb_widgets.js)\n  ↓\n[No direct REST call — JS SDK handles it]\n```\n\n#### Step 2: User Pays via Widget\n```\nUser enters payment information in Eventbrite widget\n  ↓\nEventbrite processes payment (NOT Razorpay for this flow)\n  ↓\nWidget fires onOrderComplete callback\n  ↓\nFrontend sends order_id to backend\n```\n\n#### Step 3: Booking Confirmation\n```\nBackend receives order_id from frontend\n  ↓\nGET /orders/{order_id}/ [Eventbrite]\n  ↓\nRead full order details (costs, buyer info, status)\n  ↓\nBookingConfirmedEvent published\n  ↓\nengagement module unlocks review eligibility\n  ↓\nidentity module updates order history\n```\n\n#### Step 4: E-Ticket Generation\n"

{

    "output": "Chunk ID: f697b6\nWall time: 0.0507 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 4357\nOutput:\n# PRODUCT.md — Module Registry & Product Map\n\n**Owner:** Lalit  \n**Domain:** EntertainmentTech  \n**Last Updated:** March 2026 — FR5 updated: Stripe replaces Eventbrite Checkout Widget for payments and refunds\n\n**Architecture:** Modular Monolith — Spring Boot 3.5.11, Java 21\n\n**External Integrations:** Eventbrite (event listing sync + organizer webhooks only) | Stripe (payments + refunds) | OpenAI GPT-4o (AI assistant)\n\n⚠ **Important:** This is the single source of truth for module ownership, boundaries, inter-module communication, and the Eventbrite ACL. Every agent and developer MUST read this before touching any module.\n\n---\n\n## 1. The 7 Modules — What Each One Owns\n\n### Module Ownership\n\n| Module | Owns | Must Not Own |\n|---|---|---|\n| discovery-catalog | Events, Venues, Cities, Eventbrite catalog sync | Bookings, Users, Payments |\n| scheduling | Show slots, time slot config, conflict validation, Eventbrite event sync | Bookings, Users |\n| identity | Users, JWT auth, profiles, preferences | Bookings, Payments, Events |\n| booking-inventory | Seats, SeatMap, Cart, Seat Lock State Machine, Pricing | Payments, Reviews, Users |\n| payments-ticketing | Bookings, Payments (Stripe), E-Tickets, Cancellations, Refunds (Stripe), Wallet | Seats (reads cart only) |\n| promotions | Coupons, Promotions, Eligibility rules, EB discount sync | Bookings, Payments, Users |\n| engagement | Reviews, Moderation, AI Chatbot, RAG pipeline | Bookings (reads attended status only) |\n\n- `admin/` — owns no domain. Orchestrates and aggregates across modules. No `@Entity` classes.\n- `shared/` — owns all external integrations (Eventbrite ACL), JWT, base classes, common DTOs.\n- `app/` — entry point only. `@SpringBootApplication`. Zero business logic.\n\n## 2. Module Dependency Order\n\n```text\nshared/               ← foundational — no dependency on any module\n    ↓\ndiscovery-catalog     ← depends on shared only\n    ↓\nscheduling            ← depends on shared + discovery-catalog (reads venue/event via REST)\n    ↓\nidentity              ← depends on shared only\n    ↓\nbooking-inventory     ← depends on shared + discovery-catalog (reads seat layout via REST);\n                          scheduling slot/pricing data via SlotSummaryReader + SlotPricingReader\n                          (shared interfaces, implemented in scheduling, injected at runtime — no HTTP)\n    ↓\npayments-ticketing    ← depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (reads via REST)\n    ↓\npromotions            ← depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (reads via REST)\n    ↓\nengagement            ← depends on shared + payments-ticketing + identity (reads via REST)\n    ↓\nadmin/                ← thin orchestration — reads from all modules, writes via REST APIs\n    ↓\napp/                  ← entry point only\n```\n\n**Rule:** A module NEVER imports from a module at the same level or below it. Dependency direction is strictly downward — no circular imports.\n\n## 3. How Modules Communicate — 3 Rules\n\n### Rule 1 — Never import another module's `@Service` or `@Repository` directly\nIf Module B needs data from Module A, it calls Module A's REST endpoint.\n\n**Approved exception — Shared Reader Interfaces (in-process reads only):**\nFor hot-path, high-frequency reads in a modular monolith, a module may define a read-only interface in `shared/common/service/` and implement it in the owning module. The consuming module injects the `shared/` interface — never the owning module's concrete `@Service`. This avoids HTTP overhead on the critical path while preserving module boundaries.\n\nCurrent implementations:\n- `SlotSummaryReader` (`shared/common/service/`) → implemented by `SlotSummaryReaderImpl` in `scheduling` → consumed by `booking-inventory` (slot validation path)\n- `SlotPricingReader` (`shared/common/service/`) → implemented by `SlotPricingReaderImpl` in `scheduling` → consumed by `booking-inventory` (pricing/provisioning path)\n\nRules for this pattern:\n- Interface and DTOs MUST live in `shared/common/service/` and `shared/common/dto/` respectively — never in the owning module\n- Implementation is `@Transactional(readOnly = true)` — no writes\n- Consuming module caches results where appropriate (`CartPricingService.getSlotPricingCached()`) to avoid repeated reads\n- REST endpoints for the same data are preserved for admin/external consumers\n\n### Rule 2 — Use Spring Events for async reactions\nWhen something important happens in Module A, it publishes a Spring `ApplicationEvent`. Other modules react by registering `@EventListener` methods. No direct calls.\n\n- booking-inventory → publishes `CartAssembledEvent`\n- payments-ticketing → listens `CartAssembledListener` → initiates payment\n\n- payments-ticketing → publishes `PaymentFailedEvent`\n- booking-inventory → listens `PaymentFailedListener` → triggers seat lock rollback\n\n- payments-ticketing → publishes `BookingConfirmedEvent`\n- engagement → listens `BookingConfirmedListener` → unlocks review eligibility\n- identity → listens `BookingConfirmedListener` → updates order history\n\n### Rule 3 — All Eventbrite API calls go through `shared/eventbrite/service/` only\nNo module ever calls Eventbrite directly. The ACL facade services are the only entry point.\nInbound Eventbrite webhooks: `EbWebhookController → EbWebhookDispatcher → dispatched as Spring Events → modules listen`.\nModules never consume the raw webhook directly.\n\n## 4. Eventbrite ACL — Facades in `shared/`\n\n### ACL Facade Services\n\n- **EbEventSyncService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Create (org-scoped), update, publish, unpublish, cancel, delete events; pull org event catalog; verify sync integrity\n  \n- **EbVenueService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Create venues under an org (`POST /organizations/{org_id}/venues/`); update venues (`POST /venues/{venue_id}/`); list org venues (`GET /organizations/{org_id}/venues/`); list events at a venue (`GET /venues/{venue_id}/events/`).\n  \n- **EbScheduleService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Create recurring event schedules and occurrences; retrieve series info\n  \n- **EbTicketService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Create/update ticket classes; manage inventory; check availability\n  \n- **EbCapacityService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Retrieve and update event capacity tiers; list org seat maps; attach seat map to event\n  \n- **EbOrderService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Retrieve order details for org-level reporting only. **NOT used in FR5 or FR6 payment flows** — Stripe owns all payment and order management. Retained only for organizer-side admin reporting and orphan detection.\n  \n- **EbAttendeeService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Retrieve attendee records; list attendees by event. Used in FR8 (attendance verification for reviews) only. **No longer used in FR5 payment flows** — Stripe and internal DB own all booking records.\n  \n- **EbDiscountSyncService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Create/update/delete discount codes at org level; sync usage with internal promotions. (Discounts scoped to org, applied to events at checkout.)\n  \n- **EbRefundService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - **Inactive for FR5/FR6.** All refunds are now processed via `StripeRefundService`. Retained in code for legacy compatibility only. Do NOT call for any new refund flow.\n  \n- **EbWebhookService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - **NOT USED in production.** Webhook registration not available via API; only via Dashboard. Mock service simulates webhook delivery for testing.\n\n> **Note:** `EbOrderService` is **not used in FR5 (payment) or FR6 (refund) flows**. Stripe owns all payment, order, and refund processing. `EbOrderService` is retained only for organizer-side admin reporting.\n\n> **Note:** `EbAttendeeService` is used **only in FR8** (attendance verification before review submission). It reads attendee records to confirm the user attended the event.\n\n> **Note:** `EbRefundService` is **inactive**. All refunds are processed via Stripe (`StripeRefundService`). The EB facade is retained in code for legacy compatibility only.\n\n> **Note:** `EbVenueService` supports venue creation (`POST /organizations/{org_id}/venues/`) and updates (`POST /venues/{venue_id}/`). The Eventbrite API v3 exposes both endpoints. Venue creation in your app always pushes to Eventbrite and stores the returned `eb_venue_id`.\n\n### Stripe ACL Facade Services (Payments & Refunds)\n\nAll Stripe-related calls go through these three facades in `shared/stripe/service/`. No module calls Stripe HTTP directly.\n\n- **StripePaymentService** (`shared/stripe/service/`)\n  - `POST /v1/payment_intents` — create PaymentIntent; returns `client_secret` for frontend Stripe Element\n  - `GET /v1/payment_intents/{id}` — retrieve and verify status (`succeeded`) and `amount_received` before confirming booking\n  - Stores `stripe_payment_intent_id` and `stripe_charge_id` (`latest_charge`) in `payments` table\n\n- **StripeRefundService** (`shared/stripe/service/`)\n  - `POST /v1/refunds` — create full or partial refund by `payment_intent` ID; supports `reason` field\n  - `GET /v1/refunds/{id}` — retrieve async refund status (`pending`, `succeeded`, `failed`)\n  - Saves `stripe_refund_id` to `refunds` table\n\n- **StripeWebhookHandler** (`shared/stripe/service/`)\n  - Verifies `Stripe-Signature` header via `Webhook.constructEvent(payload, sigHeader, whsecret)`\n  - Routes events as Spring Events: `payment_intent.succeeded` → idempotent booking confirm; `payment_intent.payment_failed` / `payment_intent.canceled` → `PaymentFailedEvent`; `refund.updated` → update refund status; `refund.failed` → alert ops + notify user\n  - Registered via Stripe Dashboard (Test mode) at `POST /api/webhooks/stripe`\n\n> **Stripe Sandbox:** Use `sk_test_...` / `pk_test_...` keys. Test card `4242 4242 4242 4242` for success, `4000 0000 0000 0002` for decline, `4000 0000 0000 7726` for async refund simulation. See `docs/FR5-reworked-v2.md` and `docs/stripe-api-reference.md` for full test card matrix.\n\n### Mock Eventbrite Service — Development & Testing\n\n**Location:** `mock-eventbrite-api/` (Python FastAPI, in-memory)\n\n**Purpose:** Enable local development and integration testing without making real API calls to production Eventbrite. All 10 ACL façades route through this mock service when `spring.profiles.active=mock`.\n\n**Architecture:** Python FastAPI server (async, non-blocking) provides full mock implementations of Eventbrite API v3 endpoints. Data is stored in-memory (no database persistence by default). For integration tests, add SQLite or PostgreSQL backend to the mock service.\n\n**Endpoints Mocked:**\n- `POST/GET /organizations/{org_id}/events/` \n- `POST/GET /venues/{venue_id}/events/`\n- `GET /events/{event_id}/`\n- `POST/GET /ticket_classes/`\n- `POST/GET /inventory_tiers/`\n- `POST/GET /capacity_tiers/`\n- `POST/GET /orders/`\n- `POST/GET /attendees/`\n- `POST/GET /discounts/`\n- `POST/GET /webhooks/` (webhook registration)\n- ... and 100+ supporting endpoints\n\n**Configuration:**\n- **Spring Profile:** `mock` (Spring Boot automatically routes all ACL façade calls to mock service)\n- **Environment Variable:** `EVENTBRITE_API_URL=http://localhost:9000/v3/` (when using mock profile)\n- **Startup Command:** \n  ```bash\n  cd mock-eventbrite-api\n  python -m uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 9000 --reload\n  ```\n\n**Trade-Offs:**\n| Aspect | Behavior |\n|---|---|\n| **Data Persistence** | In-memory; lost on shutdown. Acceptable for rapid iteration. |\n| **Latency** | Minimal (<5ms); no real network calls. |\n| **API Fidelity** | Matches Eventbrite API v3 schema exactly; test against real contract. |\n| **Concurrency** | Handles parallel requests; respects single-flight locks for testing. |\n| **Webhooks** | Simulates webhook delivery for testing event-driven flows. |\n\n**Usage Pattern:**\n- **Local Dev:** Run mock service; set `spring.profiles.active=mock` in `application-mock.properties`\n- **Integration Tests:** Use Spring `@ActiveProfiles(\"mock\")` to inject mock implementations\n- **Production:** Set `spring.profiles.active=production`; real Eventbrite API calls occur\n\n**See** `mock-eventbrite-api/README.md` **for detailed mock service documentation, sample payloads, and configuration options.**\n\n## 5. Eventbrite Integration Principles\n\n**Core Architecture:** Your internal DB is the primary source of truth. **Stripe is the payment and refund backbone** (FR5, FR6). **Eventbrite is a passive event listing sync layer** — it hosts the public event listing, tracks ticket class capacity, and fires organizer-side webhooks (`event.updated`, `event.unpublished`). Every entity is created internally first — then pushed to Eventbrite for listing purposes. The `eventbrite_event_id` returned from each push is stored in your DB and is the bridge for listing sync.\n\n### 5.1 Internal-First, Then Eventbrite\n\nThe pattern for every flow that touches Eventbrite is always:\n\n1. User/Admin performs action in YOUR app\n2. Internal DB is updated / validated first\n3. Eventbrite API is called via `shared/` ACL facade\n4. Eventbrite returns ID or confirmation\n5. Your DB stores the Eventbrite ID (`eventbrite_event_id`, `eventbrite_venue_id`, etc.)\n6. Both systems are now in sync — your DB is source of truth\n7. For payments and refunds: Stripe is called instead of Eventbrite — `stripe_payment_intent_id` and `stripe_charge_id` are stored in your `payments` table\n\n### 5.2 The `eventbrite_event_id` is the Spine of the System\n\nEvery downstream flow depends on `eventbrite_event_id` being present and valid in your DB:\n- FR5 (Payment) — event listing on Eventbrite uses the `event_id`; Stripe drives the actual payment, so EB checkout widget is **not required** for payment to succeed, but EB passive sync still depends on `eventbrite_event_id` being valid\n- FR6 (Cancellation — organizer-initiated) — `event.updated` / `event.unpublished` webhooks from EB identify the event to bulk-cancel via `eventbrite_event_id`\n- FR7 (Discounts) — Eventbrite discount codes are scoped to `event_id`\n- FR8 (Reviews) — Verifying attended booking cross-references Eventbrite attendee list by `event_id`\n- FR10 (Seat Lock) — Availability check hits Eventbrite `ticket_classes` for that `event_id`\n\nWithout FR2 sync: If `eventbrite_event_id` is null or orphaned, EB listing sync and capacity tracking break. However, payments (FR5) and refunds (FR6 buyer-initiated) are fully functional through Stripe regardless.\n\n### 5.3 What Eventbrite Cannot Do — Internal Responsibilities\n\n| Gaps | Handled Internally |\n|---|---|\n| User creation/sync | No user write API on Eventbrite. Identity is 100% internal. Users are linked via order email post-purchase. |\n| Conflict validation | No conflict API on Eventbrite. Time slot conflicts and turnaround gaps are enforced in your internal DB. |\n| \"Venue creation — POST /organizations/{org_id}/venues/ is available via EbVenueService.createVenue(). Admin creates venues in your app; they are pushed to Eventbrite and eb_venue_id is stored. Venue updates via POST /venues/{venue_id}/. The Eventbrite Dashboard can also manage venues independently — VenueReconciliationJob detects and flags drift.\"|\n| Seat locking | No seat lock API on Eventbrite. The entire state machine (Redis + Spring State Machine) is internal. |\n| Cart management | No cart API on Eventbrite. Cart assembly and group discount rules are internal. |\n| Order creation | Orders are created **internally** in your `bookings` table when Stripe payment is confirmed. No order creation via Eventbrite API at any point. |\n| Attendee creation | Attendee records for FR8 attendance verification come from the Eventbrite attendee list (organizer hosted the event). No attendee creation API called by this app. |\n| Payment processing | **Stripe** handles all payments. Backend creates a `PaymentIntent` (`POST /v1/payment_intents`), frontend renders Stripe Payment Element with `client_secret`, user pays, backend verifies via `GET /v1/payment_intents/{id}`. Eventbrite has no role in payment. |\n| Single order cancel (buyer) | Buyer cancellations call `POST /v1/refunds` via `StripeRefundService`. Full or partial refund by `payment_intent` ID. No Eventbrite API call for buyer-initiated cancellations. |\n| Refund submission | All refunds use `StripeRefundService.createRefund()` — programmatic, synchronous or async. Refund status tracked via `refund.updated` webhook. Eventbrite has no refund role. |\n| Webhook registration | No webhook API. Webhooks are registered via Eventbrite Dashboard only. This app simulates webhooks for testing/development. |\n| Reviews | No reviews API on Eventbrite. Entire review system is internal. Attendance verification uses attendee read API. |\n| Public event search | `GET /events/search/` is deprecated. Event discovery is limited to your org's events. |\n\n## 6. Functional Requirement Flows — Internal + Eventbrite Sync\n\n### FR1 — City Selection → Venue Discovery → Event Listing\n**Module:** discovery-catalog\n\nThis flow provides the entry point for users browsing events. The internal catalog is the primary source. Eventbrite contributes only events your org has created — public city-wide search is not available (deprecated API).\n\n**Step 1: City Selection**\n- Internal App (Your DB / Spring Boot)\n  - User selects a city in the app UI\n"
  }
}
```

Every agent and developer MUST read this before touching any module.\n\n---\n\n## 1. The 7 Modules — What Each One Owns\n\n### Module Ownership\n\n| Module | Owns | Must Not Own |\n|---|---|---|\n| discovery-catalog | Events, Venues, Cities, Eventbrite catalog sync | Bookings, Users, Payments |\n| scheduling | Show slots, time slot config, conflict validation, Eventbrite event sync | Bookings, Users |\n| identity | Users, JWT auth, profiles, preferences | Bookings, Payments, Events |\n| booking-inventory | Seats, SeatMap, Cart, Seat Lock State Machine, Pricing | Payments, Reviews, Users |\n| payments-ticketing | Bookings, Payments (Stripe), E-Tickets, Cancellations, Refunds (Stripe), Wallet | Seats (reads cart only) |\n| promotions | Coupons, Promotions, Eligibility rules, EB discount sync | Bookings, Payments, Users |\n| engagement | Reviews, Moderation, AI Chatbot, RAG pipeline | Bookings (reads attended status only) |\n\n- `admin/` — owns no domain. Orchestrates and aggregates across modules. No `@Entity` classes.\n- `shared/` — owns all external integrations (Eventbrite ACL), JWT, base classes, common DTOs.\n- `app/` — entry point only. `@SpringBootApplication`. Zero business logic.\n\n## 2. Module Dependency Order\n\n```text\nshared/               ← foundational — no dependency on any module\n    ↓\ndiscovery-catalog     ← depends on shared only\n    ↓\nscheduling            ← depends on shared + discovery-catalog (reads venue/event via REST)\n    ↓\nidentity              ← depends on shared only\n    ↓\nbooking-inventory     ← depends on shared + discovery-catalog (reads seat layout via REST);\n                          scheduling slot/pricing data via SlotSummaryReader + SlotPricingReader\n                          (shared interfaces, implemented in scheduling, injected at runtime — no HTTP)\n    ↓\npayments-ticketing    ← depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (reads via REST)\n    ↓\npromotions            ← depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (reads via REST)\n    ↓\nengagement            ← depends on shared + payments-ticketing + identity (reads via REST)\n    ↓\nadmin/                ← thin orchestration — reads from all modules, writes via REST APIs\n    ↓\napp/                  ← entry point only\n```\n\n**Rule:** A module NEVER imports from a module at the same level or below it. Dependency direction is strictly downward — no circular imports.\n\n## 3. How Modules Communicate — 3 Rules\n\n### Rule 1 — Never import another module's `@Service` or `@Repository` directly\nIf Module B needs data from Module A, it calls Module A's REST endpoint.\n\n**Approved exception — Shared Reader Interfaces (in-process reads only):**\nFor hot-path, high-frequency reads in a modular monolith, a module may define a read-only interface in `shared/common/service/` and implement it in the owning module. The consuming module injects the `shared/` interface — never the owning module's concrete `@Service`. This avoids HTTP overhead on the critical path while preserving module boundaries.\n\nCurrent implementations:\n- `SlotSummaryReader` (`shared/common/service/`) → implemented by `SlotSummaryReaderImpl` in `scheduling` → consumed by `booking-inventory` (slot validation path)\n- `SlotPricingReader` (`shared/common/service/`) → implemented by `SlotPricingReaderImpl` in `scheduling` → consumed by `booking-inventory` (pricing/provisioning path)\n\nRules for this pattern:\n- Interface and DTOs MUST live in `shared/common/service/` and `shared/common/dto/` respectively — never in the owning module\n- Implementation is `@Transactional(readOnly = true)` — no writes\n- Consuming module caches results where appropriate (`CartPricingService.getSlotPricingCached()`) to avoid repeated reads\n- REST endpoints for the same data are preserved for admin/external consumers\n\n### Rule 2 — Use Spring Events for async reactions\nWhen something important happens in Module A, it publishes a Spring `ApplicationEvent`. Other modules react by registering `@EventListener` methods. No direct calls.\n\n- booking-inventory → publishes `CartAssembledEvent`\n- payments-ticketing → listens `CartAssembledListener` → initiates payment\n\n- payments-ticketing → publishes `PaymentFailedEvent`\n- booking-inventory → listens `PaymentFailedListener` → triggers seat lock rollback\n\n- payments-ticketing → publishes `BookingConfirmedEvent`\n- engagement → listens `BookingConfirmedListener` → unlocks review eligibility\n- identity → listens `BookingConfirmedListener` → updates order history\n\n### Rule 3 — All Eventbrite API calls go through `shared/eventbrite/service/` only\nNo module ever calls Eventbrite directly. The ACL facade services are the only entry point.\nInbound Eventbrite webhooks: `EbWebhookController → EbWebhookDispatcher → dispatched as Spring Events → modules listen`.\nModules never consume the raw webhook directly.\n\n## 4. Eventbrite ACL — Facades in `shared/`\n\n### ACL Facade Services\n\n- **EbEventSyncService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Create (org-scoped), update, publish, unpublish, cancel, delete events; pull org event catalog; verify sync integrity\n  \n- **EbVenueService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Create venues under an org (`POST /organizations/{org_id}/venues/`); update venues (`POST /venues/{venue_id}/`); list org venues (`GET /organizations/{org_id}/venues/`); list events at a venue (`GET /venues/{venue_id}/events/`).\n  \n- **EbScheduleService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Create recurring event schedules and occurrences; retrieve series info\n  \n- **EbTicketService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Create/update ticket classes; manage inventory; check availability\n  \n- **EbCapacityService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Retrieve and update event capacity tiers; list org seat maps; attach seat map to event\n  \n- **EbOrderService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Retrieve order details for org-level reporting only. **NOT used in FR5 or FR6 payment flows** — Stripe owns all payment and order management. Retained only for organizer-side admin reporting and orphan detection.\n  \n- **EbAttendeeService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Retrieve attendee records; list attendees by event. Used in FR8 (attendance verification for reviews) only. **No longer used in FR5 payment flows** — Stripe and internal DB own all booking records.\n  \n- **EbDiscountSyncService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - Create/update/delete discount codes at org level; sync usage with internal promotions. (Discounts scoped to org, applied to events at checkout.)\n  \n- **EbRefundService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - **Inactive for FR5/FR6.** All refunds are now processed via `StripeRefundService`. Retained in code for legacy compatibility only. Do NOT call for any new refund flow.\n  \n- **EbWebhookService** (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n  - **NOT USED in production.** Webhook registration not available via API; only via Dashboard. Mock service simulates webhook delivery for testing.\n\n> **Note:** `EbOrderService` is **not used in FR5 (payment) or FR6 (refund) flows**. Stripe owns all payment, order, and refund processing. `EbOrderService` is retained only for organizer-side admin reporting.\n\n> **Note:** `EbAttendeeService` is used **only in FR8** (attendance verification before review submission). It reads attendee records to confirm the user attended the event.\n\n> **Note:** `EbRefundService` is **inactive**. All refunds are processed via Stripe (`StripeRefundService`). The EB facade is retained in code for legacy compatibility only.\n\n> **Note:** `EbVenueService` supports venue creation (`POST /organizations/{org_id}/venues/`) and updates (`POST /venues/{venue_id}/`). The Eventbrite API v3 exposes both endpoints. Venue creation in your app always pushes to Eventbrite and stores the returned `eb_venue_id`.\n\n### Stripe ACL Facade Services (Payments & Refunds)\n\nAll Stripe-related calls go through these three facades in `shared/stripe/service/`. No module calls Stripe HTTP directly.\n\n- **StripePaymentService** (`shared/stripe/service/`)\n  - `POST /v1/payment_intents` — create PaymentIntent; returns `client_secret` for frontend Stripe Element\n  - `GET /v1/payment_intents/{id}` — retrieve and verify status (`succeeded`) and `amount_received` before confirming booking\n  - Stores `stripe_payment_intent_id` and `stripe_charge_id` (`latest_charge`) in `payments` table\n\n- **StripeRefundService** (`shared/stripe/service/`)\n  - `POST /v1/refunds` — create full or partial refund by `payment_intent` ID; supports `reason` field\n  - `GET /v1/refunds/{id}` — retrieve async refund status (`pending`, `succeeded`, `failed`)\n  - Saves `stripe_refund_id` to `refunds` table\n\n- **StripeWebhookHandler** (`shared/stripe/service/`)\n  - Verifies `Stripe-Signature` header via `Webhook.constructEvent(payload, sigHeader, whsecret)`\n  - Routes events as Spring Events: `payment_intent.succeeded` → idempotent booking confirm; `payment_intent.payment_failed` / `payment_intent.canceled` → `PaymentFailedEvent`; `refund.updated` → update refund status; `refund.failed` → alert ops + notify user\n  - Registered via Stripe Dashboard (Test mode) at `POST /api/webhooks/stripe`\n\n> **Stripe Sandbox:** Use `sk_test_...` / `pk_test_...` keys. Test card `4242 4242 4242 4242` for success, `4000 0000 0000 0002` for decline, `4000 0000 0000 7726` for async refund simulation. See `docs/FR5-reworked-v2.md` and `docs/stripe-api-reference.md` for full test card matrix.\n\n### Mock Eventbrite Service — Development & Testing\n\n**Location:** `mock-eventbrite-api/` (Python FastAPI, in-memory)\n\n**Purpose:** Enable local development and integration testing without making real API calls to production Eventbrite. All 10 ACL façades route through this mock service when `spring.profiles.active=mock`.\n\n**Architecture:** Python FastAPI server (async, non-blocking) provides full mock implementations of Eventbrite API v3 endpoints. Data is stored in-memory (no database persistence by default). ## Entry 52
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-04T17:23:28.346Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T17:23:28.346Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_jPiLc9wUnpIjO8KtOA5exfWr",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 87c445\nWall time: 0.0505 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 4688\nOutput:\n      \"ticketCode\": \"BK-20260304-001:1\",\n      \"bookingItemId\": 1,\n      \"qrCodeData\": \"QlItMjAyNjAzMDQtMDAxOjE=\",\n      \"pdfUrl\": \"/tickets/BK-20260304-001/1.pdf\",\n      \"status\": \"ACTIVE\"\n    }\n  ]\n}\n```\n\n**Errors:** `404 BOOKING_NOT_FOUND`\n\n---\n\n### `GET /api/v1/internal/payments/by-cart/{cartId}`\n\n**Auth:** No public JWT — internal path; permitted via `SecurityConfig` pattern rule; restrict to internal network in production\n**Purpose:** Answers `booking-inventory`'s `PaymentTimeoutWatchdog` — is payment confirmed for this cart?\n\n**Response `200 OK`:**\n```json\n{ \"cartId\": 42, \"confirmed\": true }\n```\n\n---\n\n### `POST /api/v1/webhooks/stripe`\n\n**Auth:** None (public) — verified via `Stripe-Signature` header inside handler\n**Purpose:** Safety net for async Stripe events; handles cases where frontend callback was missed.\n\n**Handled events:**\n\n| Stripe Event | Your Action |\n|---|---|\n| `payment_intent.succeeded` | Idempotent booking confirm — skip if already `CONFIRMED` |\n| `payment_intent.payment_failed` | Publish `PaymentFailedEvent`; set `payments.status = FAILED` |\n| `payment_intent.canceled` | Same as `payment_failed` |\n| `refund.updated` | Update `refunds.status`; finalise cancellation if `succeeded` |\n| `refund.failed` | Set `refunds.status = FAILED`; log ops alert |\n\n**Response:** `200 OK` for all recognised and unrecognised events; `400` on signature failure only.\n\n---\n\n## Stage 6 — Business Logic\n\n### 6.1 — PaymentIntent Creation (`CartAssembledListener` → `PaymentService.createCheckout()`)\n\n**Trigger:** `CartAssembledEvent(cartId, slotId, userId, orgId, ebEventId, couponCode, totalAmountInSmallestUnit, currency, userEmail)`\n\n**Steps (within `@Transactional`):**\n1. **Idempotency check:** query `bookingRepository.findByCartId(cartId)`. If a `Booking` exists and its `Payment.status` is `PENDING` or `SUCCESS` — return existing `paymentIntentId` immediately. No Stripe call.\n2. **Generate `bookingRef`:** `\"BK-\" + LocalDate.now(UTC).format(\"yyyyMMdd\") + \"-\" + leftPad(dailyAtomicSeq.incrementAndGet(), 3, '0')`. `dailyAtomicSeq` is an in-memory `AtomicLong` reset at midnight via `@Scheduled(cron = \"0 0 0 * * *\")`.\n3. **Create `Booking` shell:** `status=PENDING`, `cartId`, `userId`, `slotId`, `eventId` (resolved via `slotId` from scheduling REST), `totalAmount = totalAmountInSmallestUnit`, `currency`, `bookingRef`. Persist immediately — required as FK anchor for `Payment`.\n4. **Call Stripe:** `StripePaymentService.createPaymentIntent(amount, currency, userEmail, Map.of(\"booking_ref\", bookingRef, \"user_id\", userId.toString()))`.\n5. **`clientSecret` handling:** stored in local variable only — passed directly into response DTO, never assigned to any entity field.\n6. **Persist `Payment`:** `bookingId`, `stripePaymentIntentId`, `amount`, `currency`, `status=PENDING`.\n7. **Return** `CheckoutInitResponse(cartId, bookingRef, paymentIntentId, clientSecret, amount, currency)` to `GET /checkout/{cartId}` caller.\n\n**On `StripeIntegrationException`:** rollback transaction (no `Booking` or `Payment` row). Log `ERROR`. Frontend will retry via `GET /checkout/{cartId}`.\n\n---\n\n### 6.2 — Payment Confirmation (`PaymentService.confirmPayment()`)\n\n**Trigger:** `POST /api/v1/payments/confirm { paymentIntentId }`\n\n**Steps:**\n1. Load `Payment` by `stripePaymentIntentId` — throw `PaymentIntentNotFoundException` if absent.\n2. **Idempotency:** if `payment.status = SUCCESS` (and `booking.status = CONFIRMED`) — return existing `bookingRef` immediately.\n3. Call `StripePaymentService.retrievePaymentIntent(paymentIntentId)`.\n4. If returned status ≠ `succeeded` — throw `PaymentNotConfirmedException(stripeStatus)`.\n5. **Within `@Transactional`:**\n   - Set `payment.stripeChargeId = response.latestCharge`.\n   - Set `payment.status = SUCCESS`, `payment.updatedAt = NOW()`.\n   - Set `booking.stripeChargeId = response.latestCharge`, `booking.status = CONFIRMED`, `booking.updatedAt = NOW()`.\n   - Fetch cart items via REST: `GET /api/v1/booking/carts/{cartId}/items` on `booking-inventory`. Throw `CartItemsFetchException` on non-2xx — rollback everything.\n   - For each `CartItem`: create `BookingItem(bookingId, seatId, gaClaimId, ticketClassId, unitPrice, currency, status=ACTIVE)`. Persist.\n   - For each `BookingItem`: create `ETicket(bookingId, bookingItemId, qrCodeData=Base64(\"{bookingRef}:{bookingItemId}\"), pdfUrl=\"/tickets/{bookingRef}/{bookingItemId}.pdf\", status=ACTIVE)`. Persist.\n6. **Publish** `BookingConfirmedEvent(cartId, seatIds, stripePaymentIntentId, userId)` via `@TransactionalEventListener(AFTER_COMMIT)`.\n\n---\n\n### 6.3 — Payment Failure (`PaymentService.handleFailure()`)\n\n**Triggers:** `POST /api/v1/payments/failed` (frontend) **OR** Stripe webhook `payment_intent.payment_failed` / `payment_intent.canceled`.\n\n**Steps (fully idempotent — whichever trigger arrives first wins):**\n1. Load `Payment` by `stripePaymentIntentId`.\n2. If `payment.status = FAILED` already — return immediately (no-op).\n3. **Within `@Transactional`:**\n   - Set `payment.status = FAILED`, populate `failureCode`/`failureMessage` from Stripe response where available.\n   - Set `booking.status = CANCELLED`, `booking.updatedAt = NOW()`.\n4. **Publish** `PaymentFailedEvent(cartId, userId)` — `booking-inventory`'s `PaymentFailedListener` fires `RELEASE` transition on all `HARD_LOCKED` seats in the cart.\n\n---\n\n### 6.4 — Buyer-Initiated Cancellation (`CancellationService.cancel()`)\n\n**Trigger:** `POST /api/v1/bookings/{bookingRef}/cancel { reason }`\n\n**Steps:**\n1. Load `Booking` by `bookingRef`. If `booking.userId ≠ JWT principal userId` — throw `BookingNotFoundException` (never `403` — do not reveal existence to non-owners).\n2. Assert `booking.status = CONFIRMED` — throw `CancellationNotAllowedException(\"Booking is not in CONFIRMED state\")` otherwise.\n3. Resolve `slotStartTime` via `GET /api/v1/scheduling/slots/{slotId}` (REST read to scheduling). Assert `NOW() < slotStartTime - cancellationWindowHours` — throw `CancellationNotAllowedException(\"Outside cancellation window\", windowClosedAt)` if past window.\n4. Create `CancellationRequest(bookingId, userId, reason, status=PENDING)`. Persist.\n5. Set `booking.status = CANCELLATION_PENDING`. Persist.\n6. Compute refund amount:\n   - Read `payments.cancellation.fee-percent` from `application.yaml` (default `0`).\n   - `refundAmount = totalAmount - floor(totalAmount * feePercent / 100)`.\n7. Call `StripeRefundService.createRefund(stripePaymentIntentId, refundAmount, reason)`.\n8. On Stripe response:\n   - Persist `Refund(bookingId, stripeRefundId, refundAmount, reason, status from Stripe response)`.\n   - Update `CancellationRequest.status = APPROVED`.\n   - If Stripe returned `status = succeeded` immediately → call `finaliseAfterRefund(bookingId)` inline.\n   - If Stripe returned `status = pending` → finalisation deferred to `refund.updated` webhook.\n9. Return `CancellationResponse(bookingRef, status=CANCELLATION_PENDING, refund{...})`.\n\n**On `StripeIntegrationException` from `createRefund`:**\n- Revert `booking.status = CONFIRMED`.\n- Set `CancellationRequest.status = REJECTED`.\n- Throw `RefundFailedException`.\n\n---\n\n### 6.4.1 — Cancellation Finalisation (`CancellationService.finaliseAfterRefund()`)\n\nCalled inline (when Stripe refund is immediately `succeeded`) or from `RefundService` when `refund.updated` webhook delivers `status = succeeded`.\n\n**Steps (idempotent guard: skip if `booking.status = CANCELLED`):**\n1. **Within `@Transactional`:**\n   - `booking.status = CANCELLED`, `booking.updatedAt = NOW()`.\n   - All `BookingItem.status = CANCELLED`.\n   - All `ETicket.status = VOIDED`.\n   - `Refund.status = SUCCEEDED`.\n   - `CancellationRequest.status = APPROVED`, `resolvedAt = NOW()`.\n2. **Publish** `BookingCancelledEvent(bookingId, cartId, seatIds, userId, reason)` via `@TransactionalEventListener(AFTER_COMMIT)`.\n   - `booking-inventory` listener releases seats (`CONFIRMED → AVAILABLE`).\n   - `engagement` listener notifies user.\n\n---\n\n### 6.5 — Stripe Webhook Handler (`StripeWebhookController`)\n\n**Signature verification first:** `StripeWebhookHandler.constructEvent(rawBody, stripeSignatureHeader)` — throw `StripeWebhookSignatureException` on failure → `400`.\n\nAll event handlers are **idempotent**:\n\n| Event | Handler |\n|---|---|\n| `payment_intent.succeeded` | Load `Payment` by `stripePaymentIntentId`; if `status ≠ SUCCESS` → call `PaymentService.confirmPayment()` (skip Stripe retrieve — status already known from payload); skip if already `CONFIRMED` |\n| `payment_intent.payment_failed` | Call `PaymentService.handleFailure()`; skip if already `FAILED` |\n| `payment_intent.canceled` | Same as `payment_failed` |\n| `refund.updated` | Load `Refund` by `stripeRefundId`; update `status`; if `succeeded` → call `CancellationService.finaliseAfterRefund(bookingId)`; if `failed` → call `handleRefundFailed()` |\n| `refund.failed` | `Refund.status = FAILED`; `CancellationRequest.status = REJECTED`; `Booking` stays `CANCELLATION_PENDING`; log `ERROR` ops alert |\n| Unknown event type | Log `DEBUG`; return `200 OK` silently |\n\n---\n\n### 6.6 — Internal Watchdog API (`InternalPaymentController`)\n\n`GET /api/v1/internal/payments/by-cart/{cartId}`:\n- Query `bookingRepository.existsByCartIdAndStatus(cartId, CONFIRMED)`.\n- Return `{ \"cartId\": cartId, \"confirmed\": result }`.\n- No authentication — permitted via `SecurityConfig` `.requestMatchers(\"/api/v1/internal/**\").permitAll()`.\n\n---\n\n### 6.7 — Cross-Cutting Rules\n\n| Rule | Detail |\n|---|---|\n| Module boundary | `payments-ticketing` never imports `booking-inventory`, `scheduling`, or `identity` `@Service`, `@Repository`, or `@Entity`. Cart items and slot data fetched via REST only. |\n| Event publishing | All `ApplicationEvent` publications use `@TransactionalEventListener(AFTER_COMMIT)` — events fire only after DB transaction commits. |\n| `clientSecret` | Never stored in DB. Passed directly from `StripePaymentService` response to response DTO and discarded. |\n| Currency | All amounts stored and computed in smallest currency unit (paise for INR). Display conversion (÷ 100) happens only in response DTOs. |\n| Cancellation fee config | Read from `application.yaml` `payments.cancellation.fee-percent` (default `0`). No DB table for cancellation policy in FR5 scope. |\n| `bookingRef` uniqueness | Daily atomic sequence enforced in-memory. Collision risk is negligible at expected booking volumes. If high volume requires DB sequence, replace `AtomicLong` with `SELECT nextval('booking_ref_seq')`. |\n\n---\n\n## Stage 7 — Error Handling\n\n### 7.1 — Payment Path\n\n**E1 — Stripe `createPaymentIntent` fails**\n- Cause: `StripeIntegrationException` (network timeout, invalid API key, Stripe 5xx)\n- Action: Rollback transaction; no `Booking` or `Payment` row persisted. Log `ERROR` with `cartId`.\n- Response: `CartAssembledEvent` is async — no HTTP response to Spring event. `GET /checkout/{cartId}` retries will eventually succeed when Stripe recovers.\n- HTTP surface (when checkout endpoint re-called): `502 BAD_GATEWAY`\n  ```json\n  { \"errorCode\": \"STRIPE_UNAVAILABLE\", \"message\": \"Payment service unavailable. Please retry.\" }\n  ```\n\n**E2 — PaymentIntent already exists for cart (duplicate event / retry)**\n- Cause: `CartAssembledEvent` delivered twice, or frontend retries `GET /checkout/{cartId}`\n- Action: Short-circuit — do not call Stripe again. Return existing `paymentIntentId`. If already `SUCCESS`: omit `clientSecret` from response.\n- HTTP response: `200 OK` with existing `CheckoutInitResponse` (if `PENDING`); `409 PAYMENT_ALREADY_EXISTS` with `bookingRef` in body (if already `CONFIRMED`).\n\n**E3 — POST /confirm but Stripe status ≠ `succeeded`**\n- Cause: Status is `requires_action`, `processing`, `requires_confirmation`, or `canceled`\n- Action: No DB writes. Log `WARN` with `paymentIntentId` + Stripe status.\n- HTTP response: `402 Payment Required`\n  ```json\n  { \"errorCode\": \"PAYMENT_NOT_CONFIRMED\", \"stripeStatus\": \"requires_action\", \"message\": \"Payment has not been completed. Please complete payment via Stripe.\" }\n  ```\n\n**E4 — POST /confirm but booking already `CONFIRMED` (double-submit)**\n- Cause: Frontend calls `/confirm` twice\n- Action: Idempotent — no DB writes. Return existing `bookingRef`.\n- HTTP response: `200 OK` with existing `BookingResponse`.\n\n**E5 — `retrievePaymentIntent` Stripe call times out**\n- Cause: Stripe HTTP timeout during `/confirm`\n- Action: No DB state change. Log `ERROR`. Stripe webhook `payment_intent.succeeded` arrives as safety net.\n- HTTP response: `504 Gateway Timeout`\n  ```json\n  { \"errorCode\": \"STRIPE_TIMEOUT\", \"message\": \"Payment verification timed out. Your booking will be confirmed automatically.\" }\n  ```\n\n---\n\n### 7.2 — Cart Snapshot Fetch\n\n**E6 — REST call to `booking-inventory` for cart items returns `404` or `503`**\n- Cause: `booking-inventory` service degraded during booking confirmation\n- Action: Throw `CartItemsFetchException`. Rollback entire confirmation transaction — `Booking` stays `PENDING`, `Payment` stays `PENDING`, no `BookingItem` or `ETicket` created.\n- HTTP response: `503 SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE`\n  ```json\n  { \"errorCode\": \"CART_ITEMS_UNAVAILABLE\", \"message\": \"Unable to retrieve cart items. Please retry.\" }\n  ```\n- Recovery: Stripe webhook `payment_intent.succeeded` will retry `/confirm` logic; cart items must be available before finalisation succeeds.\n\n---\n\n### 7.3 — Cancellation Path\n\n**E7 — Booking not owned by calling user**\n- Cause: `booking.userId ≠ JWT principal userId`\n- Action: Treat as not found — never reveal existence.\n- HTTP response: `404 NOT_FOUND`\n  ```json\n  { \"errorCode\": \"BOOKING_NOT_FOUND\", \"bookingRef\": \"BK-20260304-001\" }\n  ```\n\n**E8 — `booking.status ≠ CONFIRMED`**\n- Cause: Booking is `PENDING`, `CANCELLATION_PENDING`, or `CANCELLED`\n- Action: Reject immediately — no Stripe call.\n- HTTP response: `409 Conflict`\n  ```json\n  { \"errorCode\": \"CANCELLATION_NOT_ALLOWED\", \"reason\": \"Booking is not in CONFIRMED state\", \"currentStatus\": \"CANCELLATION_PENDING\" }\n  ```\n\n**E9 — Outside cancellation window**\n- Cause: `NOW() > slotStartTime - cancellationWindowHours`\n- Action: Reject — no `CancellationRequest` created, no Stripe call.\n- HTTP response: `409 Conflict`\n  ```json\n  { \"errorCode\": \"CANCELLATION_NOT_ALLOWED\", \"reason\": \"Cancellation window has closed\", \"windowClosedAt\": \"2026-03-04T08:00:00Z\" }\n  ```\n\n**E10 — `StripeRefundService.createRefund()` fails**\n- Cause: Stripe error (charge already refunded, invalid charge ID, Stripe 5xx)\n- Action: Revert `booking.status = CONFIRMED`. Set `CancellationRequest.status = REJECTED`. Log `ERROR` with full Stripe error body. Throw `RefundFailedException`.\n- HTTP response: `502 BAD_GATEWAY`\n  ```json\n  { \"errorCode\": \"REFUND_FAILED\", \"message\": \"Unable to process refund at this time. Please contact support.\", \"stripeError\": \"charge_already_refunded\" }\n  ```\n\n**E11 — Async `refund.failed` arrives via webhook**\n- Cause: Stripe fires `refund.failed` after initial `PENDING`\n- Action: `Refund.status = FAILED`. `Booking` stays `CANCELLATION_PENDING` — seats remain `CONFIRMED`. `CancellationRequest.status = REJECTED`, `resolvedAt = NOW()`. Log `ERROR` ops alert. Do NOT publish `BookingCancelledEvent` — seats must not be released.\n- No HTTP response (webhook returns `200 OK` to Stripe unconditionally).\n\n---\n\n### 7.4 — Webhook Path\n\n**E12 — Invalid Stripe signature**\n- Cause: `StripeWebhookHandler.constructEvent()` throws `StripeWebhookSignatureException`\n- Action: Log `WARN` with truncated payload. Do not process event.\n- HTTP response: `400 Bad Request`\n  ```json\n  { \"errorCode\": \"INVALID_WEBHOOK_SIGNATURE\" }\n  ```\n- Note: Stripe does NOT retry on `4xx` — signature failures indicate a config problem or non-Stripe sender.\n\n**E13 — Webhook for unknown `paymentIntentId` or `refundId`**\n- Cause: Out-of-order delivery or replay of events for objects not in DB\n- Action: Log `WARN` with event ID and object ID. Return `200 OK` — do not signal error to Stripe.\n- Note: Returning `4xx` here would cause Stripe to retry indefinitely.\n\n**E14 — Unrecognised webhook event type**\n- Cause: Stripe sends an event type not in the handled set (e.g. `customer.created`)\n- Action: Log `DEBUG`. Return `200 OK` silently.\n\n---\n\n### 7.5 — E-Ticket Generation\n\n**E15 — `UNIQUE (booking_item_id)` constraint violation on ETicket insert**\n- Cause: Concurrent duplicate `/confirm` requests both pass idempotency check before either writes\n- Action: Catch `DataIntegrityViolationException`. Treat as idempotent — reload and return existing `ETicket`. Do not surface `500`.\n- Implementation:\n  ```java\n  try {\n      return eTicketRepository.save(newTicket);\n  } catch (DataIntegrityViolationException e) {\n      return eTicketRepository.findByBookingItemId(bookingItemId)\n          .orElseThrow(() -> e);\n  }\n  ```\n\n---\n\n### 7.6 — Booking Ownership (Read Endpoints)\n\n**E16 — User accesses booking or tickets for another user**\n- Cause: `booking.userId ≠ JWT principal userId` on `GET /bookings/{ref}` or `GET /tickets/{ref}`\n- Action: Same as E7 — treat as not found.\n- HTTP response: `404 NOT_FOUND`\n\n---\n\n### 7.7 — Exception → HTTP Status Map\n\n> All handlers registered in `GlobalExceptionHandler` in `shared/common/exception/` — no `@ControllerAdvice` in `payments-ticketing` (Hard Rule #5).\n\n| Exception | HTTP Status | Error Code |\n|---|---|---|\n| `BookingNotFoundException` | `404` | `BOOKING_NOT_FOUND` |\n| `PaymentNotConfirmedException` | `402` | `PAYMENT_NOT_CONFIRMED` |\n| `DuplicatePaymentException` | `409` | `PAYMENT_ALREADY_EXISTS` |\n| `CancellationNotAllowedException` | `409` | `CANCELLATION_NOT_ALLOWED` |\n| `RefundFailedException` | `502` | `REFUND_FAILED` |\n| `CartItemsFetchException` | `503` | `CART_ITEMS_UNAVAILABLE` |\n| `StripeIntegrationException` | `502` | `STRIPE_UNAVAILABLE` |\n| `StripeWebhookSignatureException` | `400` | `INVALID_WEBHOOK_SIGNATURE` |\n| `ForbiddenException` (non-owner) | `404` | `BOOKING_NOT_FOUND` |\n\n---\n\n## Stage 8 — Tests\n\n### `domain/` — Unit Tests\n\n**`BookingTest`**\n- `transition_pending_to_confirmed_succeeds`\n- `transition_confirmed_to_cancellation_pending_succeeds`\n- `transition_cancellation_pending_to_cancelled_succeeds`\n- `transition_confirmed_to_cancelled_directly_throws` — CONFIRMED cannot skip CANCELLATION_PENDING\n- `transition_cancelled_to_any_throws` — CANCELLED is terminal\n\n**`RefundTest`**\n- `transition_pending_to_succeeded_succeeds`\n- `transition_pending_to_failed_succeeds`\n- `transition_succeeded_to_failed_throws` — SUCCEEDED is terminal\n\n**`ETicketTest`**\n- `qrCodeData_format_is_bookingRef_colon_itemId` — asserts `Base64(\"{bookingRef}:{bookingItemId}\")`\n"
  }
}

If a `Booking` exists and its `Payment.status` is `PENDING` or `SUCCESS` — return existing `paymentIntentId` immediately. No Stripe call.\n2. **Generate `bookingRef`:** `\"BK-\" + LocalDate.now(UTC).format(\"yyyyMMdd\") + \"-\" + leftPad(dailyAtomicSeq.incrementAndGet(), 3, '0')`. `dailyAtomicSeq` is an in-memory `AtomicLong` reset at midnight via `@Scheduled(cron = \"0 0 0 * * *\")`.\n3. **Create `Booking` shell:** `status=PENDING`, `cartId`, `userId`, `slotId`, `eventId` (resolved via `slotId` from scheduling REST), `totalAmount = totalAmountInSmallestUnit`, `currency`, `bookingRef`. Persist immediately — required as FK anchor for `Payment`.\n4. **Call Stripe:** `StripePaymentService.createPaymentIntent(amount, currency, userEmail, Map.of(\"booking_ref\", bookingRef, \"user_id\", userId.toString()))`.\n5. **`clientSecret` handling:** stored in local variable only — passed directly into response DTO, never assigned to any entity field.\n6. **Persist `Payment`:** `bookingId`, `stripePaymentIntentId`, `amount`, `currency`, `status=PENDING`.\n7. **Return** `CheckoutInitResponse(cartId, bookingRef, paymentIntentId, clientSecret, amount, currency)` to `GET /checkout/{cartId}` caller.\n\n**On `StripeIntegrationException`:** rollback transaction (no `Booking` or `Payment` row). Log `ERROR`. Frontend will retry via `GET /checkout/{cartId}`.\n\n---\n\n### 6.2 — Payment Confirmation (`PaymentService.confirmPayment()`)\n\n**Trigger:** `POST /api/v1/payments/confirm { paymentIntentId }`\n\n**Steps:**\n1. Load `Payment` by `stripePaymentIntentId` — throw `PaymentIntentNotFoundException` if absent.\n2. **Idempotency:** if `payment.status = SUCCESS` (and `booking.status = CONFIRMED`) — return existing `bookingRef` immediately.\n3. Call `StripePaymentService.retrievePaymentIntent(paymentIntentId)`.\n4. If returned status ≠ `succeeded` — throw `PaymentNotConfirmedException(stripeStatus)`.\n5. **Within `@Transactional`:**\n   - Set `payment.stripeChargeId = response.latestCharge`.\n   - Set `payment.status = SUCCESS`, `payment.updatedAt = NOW()`.\n   - Set `booking.stripeChargeId = response.latestCharge`, `booking.status = CONFIRMED`, `booking.updatedAt = NOW()`.\n   - Fetch cart items via REST: `GET /api/v1/booking/carts/{cartId}/items` on `booking-inventory`. Throw `CartItemsFetchException` on non-2xx — rollback everything.\n   - For each `CartItem`: create `BookingItem(bookingId, seatId, gaClaimId, ticketClassId, unitPrice, currency, status=ACTIVE)`. Persist.\n   - For each `BookingItem`: create `ETicket(bookingId, bookingItemId, qrCodeData=Base64(\"{bookingRef}:{bookingItemId}\"), pdfUrl=\"/tickets/{bookingRef}/{bookingItemId}.pdf\", status=ACTIVE)`. Persist.\n6. **Publish** `BookingConfirmedEvent(cartId, seatIds, stripePaymentIntentId, userId)` via `@TransactionalEventListener(AFTER_COMMIT)`.\n\n---\n\n### 6.3 — Payment Failure (`PaymentService.handleFailure()`)\n\n**Triggers:** `POST /api/v1/payments/failed` (frontend) **OR** Stripe webhook `payment_intent.payment_failed` / `payment_intent.canceled`.\n\n**Steps (fully idempotent — whichever trigger arrives first wins):**\n1. Load `Payment` by `stripePaymentIntentId`.\n2. If `payment.status = FAILED` already — return immediately (no-op).\n3. **Within `@Transactional`:**\n   - Set `payment.status = FAILED`, populate `failureCode`/`failureMessage` from Stripe response where available.\n   - Set `booking.status = CANCELLED`, `booking.updatedAt = NOW()`.\n4. **Publish** `PaymentFailedEvent(cartId, userId)` — `booking-inventory`'s `PaymentFailedListener` fires `RELEASE` transition on all `HARD_LOCKED` seats in the cart.\n\n---\n\n### 6.4 — Buyer-Initiated Cancellation (`CancellationService.cancel()`)\n\n**Trigger:** `POST /api/v1/bookings/{bookingRef}/cancel { reason }`\n\n**Steps:**\n1. Load `Booking` by `bookingRef`. If `booking.userId ≠ JWT principal userId` — throw `BookingNotFoundException` (never `403` — do not reveal existence to non-owners).\n2. Assert `booking.status = CONFIRMED` — throw `CancellationNotAllowedException(\"Booking is not in CONFIRMED state\")` otherwise.\n3. Resolve `slotStartTime` via `GET /api/v1/scheduling/slots/{slotId}` (REST read to scheduling). Assert `NOW() < slotStartTime - cancellationWindowHours` — throw `CancellationNotAllowedException(\"Outside cancellation window\", windowClosedAt)` if past window.\n4. Create `CancellationRequest(bookingId, userId, reason, status=PENDING)`. Persist.\n5. Set `booking.status = CANCELLATION_PENDING`. Persist.\n6. Compute refund amount:\n   - Read `payments.cancellation.fee-percent` from `application.yaml` (default `0`).\n   - `refundAmount = totalAmount - floor(totalAmount * feePercent / 100)`.\n7. Call `StripeRefundService.createRefund(stripePaymentIntentId, refundAmount, reason)`.\n8. On Stripe response:\n   - Persist `Refund(bookingId, stripeRefundId, refundAmount, reason, status from Stripe response)`.\n   - Update `CancellationRequest.status = APPROVED`.\n   - If Stripe returned `status = succeeded` immediately → call `finaliseAfterRefund(bookingId)` inline.\n   - If Stripe returned `status = pending` → finalisation deferred to `refund.updated` webhook.\n9. Return `CancellationResponse(bookingRef, status=CANCELLATION_PENDING, refund{...})`.\n\n**On `StripeIntegrationException` from `createRefund`:**\n- Revert `booking.status = CONFIRMED`.\n- Set `CancellationRequest.status = REJECTED`.\n- Throw `RefundFailedException`.\n\n---\n\n### 6.4.1 — Cancellation Finalisation (`CancellationService.finaliseAfterRefund()`)\n\nCalled inline (when Stripe refund is immediately `succeeded`) or from `RefundService` when `refund.updated` webhook delivers `status = succeeded`.\n\n**Steps (idempotent guard: skip if `booking.status = CANCELLED`):**\n1. **Within `@Transactional`:**\n   - `booking.status = CANCELLED`, `booking.updatedAt = NOW()`.\n   - All `BookingItem.status = CANCELLED`.\n   - All `ETicket.status = VOIDED`.\n   - `Refund.status = SUCCEEDED`.\n   - `CancellationRequest.status = APPROVED`, `resolvedAt = NOW()`.\n2. **Publish** `BookingCancelledEvent(bookingId, cartId, seatIds, userId, reason)` via `@TransactionalEventListener(AFTER_COMMIT)`.\n   - `booking-inventory` listener releases seats (`CONFIRMED → AVAILABLE`).\n   - `engagement` listener notifies user.\n\n---\n\n### 6.5 — Stripe Webhook Handler (`StripeWebhookController`)\n\n**Signature verification first:** `StripeWebhookHandler.constructEvent(rawBody, stripeSignatureHeader)` — throw `StripeWebhookSignatureException` on failure → `400`.\n\nAll event handlers are **idempotent**:\n\n| Event | Handler |\n|---|---|\n| `payment_intent.succeeded` | Load `Payment` by `stripePaymentIntentId`; if `status ≠ SUCCESS` → call `PaymentService.confirmPayment()` (skip Stripe retrieve — status already known from payload); skip if already `CONFIRMED` |\n| `payment_intent.payment_failed` | Call `PaymentService.handleFailure()`; skip if already `FAILED` |\n| `payment_intent.canceled` | Same as `payment_failed` |\n| `refund.updated` | Load `Refund` by `stripeRefundId`; update `status`; if `succeeded` → call `CancellationService.finaliseAfterRefund(bookingId)`; if `failed` → call `handleRefundFailed()` |\n| `refund.failed` | `Refund.status = FAILED`; `CancellationRequest.status = REJECTED`; `Booking` stays `CANCELLATION_PENDING`; log `ERROR` ops alert |\n| Unknown event type | Log `DEBUG`; return `200 OK` silently |\n\n---\n\n### 6.6 — Internal Watchdog API (`InternalPaymentController`)\n\n`GET /api/v1/internal/payments/by-cart/{cartId}`:\n- Query `bookingRepository.existsByCartIdAndStatus(cartId, CONFIRMED)`.\n- Return `{ \"cartId\": cartId, \"confirmed\": result }`.\n- No authentication — permitted via `SecurityConfig` `.requestMatchers(\"/api/v1/internal/**\").permitAll()`.\n\n---\n\n### 6.7 — Cross-Cutting Rules\n\n| Rule | Detail |\n|---|---|\n| Module boundary | `payments-ticketing` never imports `booking-inventory`, `scheduling`, or `identity` `@Service`, `@Repository`, or `@Entity`. Cart items and slot data fetched via REST only. |\n| Event publishing | All `ApplicationEvent` publications use `@TransactionalEventListener(AFTER_COMMIT)` — events fire only after DB transaction commits. |\n| `clientSecret` | Never stored in DB. Passed directly from `StripePaymentService` response to response DTO and discarded. |\n| Currency | All amounts stored and computed in smallest currency unit (paise for INR). Display conversion (÷ 100) happens only in response DTOs. |\n| Cancellation fee config | Read from `application.yaml` `payments.cancellation.fee-percent` (default `0`). No DB table for cancellation policy in FR5 scope. |\n| `bookingRef` uniqueness | Daily atomic sequence enforced in-memory. Collision risk is negligible at expected booking volumes. If high volume requires DB sequence, replace `AtomicLong` with `SELECT nextval('booking_ref_seq')`. |\n\n---\n\n## Stage 7 — Error Handling\n\n### 7.1 — Payment Path\n\n**E1 — Stripe `createPaymentIntent` fails**\n- Cause: `StripeIntegrationException` (network timeout, invalid API key, Stripe 5xx)\n- Action: Rollback transaction; no `Booking` or `Payment` row persisted. Log `ERROR` with `cartId`.\n- Response: `CartAssembledEvent` is async — no HTTP response to Spring event. `GET /checkout/{cartId}` retries will eventually succeed when Stripe recovers.\n- HTTP surface (when checkout endpoint re-called): `502 BAD_GATEWAY`\n  ```json\n  { \"errorCode\": \"STRIPE_UNAVAILABLE\", \"message\": \"Payment service unavailable. Please retry.\" }\n  ```\n\n**E2 — PaymentIntent already exists for cart (duplicate event / retry)**\n- Cause: `CartAssembledEvent` delivered twice, or frontend retries `GET /checkout/{cartId}`\n- Action: Short-circuit — do not call Stripe again. Return existing `paymentIntentId`. If already `SUCCESS`: omit `clientSecret` from response.\n- HTTP response: `200 OK` with existing `CheckoutInitResponse` (if `PENDING`); `409 PAYMENT_ALREADY_EXISTS` with `bookingRef` in body (if already `CONFIRMED`).\n\n**E3 — POST /confirm but Stripe status ≠ `succeeded`**\n- Cause: Status is `requires_action`, `processing`, `requires_confirmation`, or `canceled`\n- Action: No DB writes. Log `WARN` with `paymentIntentId` + Stripe status.\n- HTTP response: `402 Payment Required`\n  ```json\n  { \"errorCode\": \"PAYMENT_NOT_CONFIRMED\", \"stripeStatus\": \"requires_action\", \"message\": \"Payment has not been completed. Please complete payment via Stripe.\" }\n  ```\n\n**E4 — POST /confirm but booking already `CONFIRMED` (double-submit)**\n- Cause: Frontend calls `/confirm` twice\n- Action: Idempotent — no DB writes. Return existing `bookingRef`.\n- HTTP response: `200 OK` with existing `BookingResponse`.\n\n**E5 — `retrievePaymentIntent` Stripe call times out**\n- Cause: Stripe HTTP timeout during `/confirm`\n- Action: No DB state change. Log `ERROR`. Stripe webhook `payment_intent.succeeded` arrives as safety net.\n- HTTP response: `504 Gateway Timeout`\n  ```json\n  { \"errorCode\": \"STRIPE_TIMEOUT\", \"message\": \"Payment verification timed out. Your booking will be confirmed automatically.\" }\n  ```\n\n---\n\n### 7.2 — Cart Snapshot Fetch\n\n**E6 — REST call to `booking-inventory` for cart items returns `404` or `503`**\n- Cause: `booking-inventory` service degraded during booking confirmation\n- Action: Throw `CartItemsFetchException`. Rollback entire confirmation transaction — `Booking` stays `PENDING`, `Payment` stays `PENDING`, no `BookingItem` or `ETicket` created.\n- HTTP response: `503 SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE`\n  ```json\n  { \"errorCode\": \"CART_ITEMS_UNAVAILABLE\", \"message\": \"Unable to retrieve cart items. Please retry.\" }\n  ```\n- Recovery: Stripe webhook `payment_intent.succeeded` will retry `/confirm` logic; cart items must be available before finalisation succeeds.\n\n---\n\n### 7.3 — Cancellation Path\n\n**E7 — Booking not owned by calling user**\n- Cause: `booking.userId ≠ JWT principal userId`\n- Action: Treat as not found — never reveal existence.\n- HTTP response: `404 NOT_FOUND`\n  ```json\n  { \"errorCode\": \"BOOKING_NOT_FOUND\", \"bookingRef\": \"BK-20260304-001\" }\n  ```\n\n**E8 — `booking.status ≠ CONFIRMED`**\n- Cause: Booking is `PENDING`, `CANCELLATION_PENDING`, or `CANCELLED`\n- Action: Reject immediately — no Stripe call.\n- HTTP response: `409 Conflict`\n  ```json\n  { \"errorCode\": \"CANCELLATION_NOT_ALLOWED\", \"reason\": \"Booking is not in CONFIRMED state\", \"currentStatus\": \"CANCELLATION_PENDING\" }\n  ```\n\n**E9 — Outside cancellation window**\n- Cause: `NOW() > slotStartTime - cancellationWindowHours`\n- Action: Reject — no `CancellationRequest` created, no Stripe call.\n- HTTP response: `409 Conflict`\n  ```json\n  { \"errorCode\": \"CANCELLATION_NOT_ALLOWED\", \"reason\": \"Cancellation window has closed\", \"windowClosedAt\": \"2026-03-04T08:00:00Z\" }\n  ```\n\n**E10 — `StripeRefundService.createRefund()` fails**\n- Cause: Stripe error (charge already refunded, invalid charge ID, Stripe 5xx)\n- Action: Revert `booking.status = CONFIRMED`. Set `CancellationRequest.status = REJECTED`. Log `ERROR` with full Stripe error body. Throw `RefundFailedException`.\n- HTTP response: `502 BAD_GATEWAY`\n  ```json\n  { \"errorCode\": \"REFUND_FAILED\", \"message\": \"Unable to process refund at this time. Please contact support.\", \"stripeError\": \"charge_already_refunded\" }\n  ```\n\n**E11 — Async `refund.failed` arrives via webhook**\n- Cause: Stripe fires `refund.failed` after initial `PENDING`\n- Action: `Refund.status = FAILED`. `Booking` stays `CANCELLATION_PENDING` — seats remain `CONFIRMED`. `CancellationRequest.status = REJECTED`, `resolvedAt = NOW()`. Log `ERROR` ops alert. Do NOT publish `BookingCancelledEvent` — seats must not be released.\n- No HTTP response (webhook returns `200 OK` to Stripe unconditionally).\n\n---\n\n### 7.4 — Webhook Path\n\n**E12 — Invalid Stripe signature**\n- Cause: `StripeWebhookHandler.constructEvent()` throws `StripeWebhookSignatureException`\n- Action: Log `WARN` with truncated payload. Do not process event.\n- HTTP response: `400 Bad Request`\n  ```json\n  { \"errorCode\": \"INVALID_WEBHOOK_SIGNATURE\" }\n  ```\n- Note: Stripe does NOT retry on `4xx` — signature failures indicate a config problem or non-Stripe sender.\n\n**E13 — Webhook for unknown `paymentIntentId` or `refundId`**\n- Cause: Out-of-order delivery or replay of events for objects not in DB\n- Action: Log `WARN` with event ID and object ID. Return `200 OK` — do not signal error to Stripe.\n- Note: Returning `4xx` here would cause Stripe to retry indefinitely.\n\n**E14 — Unrecognised webhook event type**\n- Cause: Stripe sends an event type not in the handled set (e.g. `customer.created`)\n- Action: Log `DEBUG`. Return `200 OK` silently.\n\n---\n\n### 7.5 — E-Ticket Generation\n\n**E15 — `UNIQUE (booking_item_id)` constraint violation on ETicket insert**\n- Cause: Concurrent duplicate `/confirm` requests both pass idempotency check before either writes\n- Action: Catch `DataIntegrityViolationException`. Treat as idempotent — reload and return existing `ETicket`. Do not surface `500`.\n- Implementation:\n  ```java\n  try {\n      return eTicketRepository.save(newTicket);\n  } catch (DataIntegrityViolationException e) {\n      return eTicketRepository.findByBookingItemId(bookingItemId)\n          .orElseThrow(() -> e);\n  }\n  ```\n\n---\n\n### 7.6 — Booking Ownership (Read Endpoints)\n\n**E16 — User accesses booking or tickets for another user**\n- Cause: `booking.userId ≠ JWT principal userId` on `GET /bookings/{ref}` or `GET /tickets/{ref}`\n- Action: Same as E7 — treat as not found.\n- HTTP response: `404 NOT_FOUND`\n\n---\n\n### 7.7 — Exception → HTTP Status Map\n\n> All handlers registered in `GlobalExceptionHandler` in `shared/common/exception/` — no `@ControllerAdvice` in `payments-ticketing` (Hard Rule #5).\n\n| Exception | HTTP Status | Error Code |\n|---|---|---|\n| `BookingNotFoundException` | `404` | `BOOKING_NOT_FOUND` |\n| `PaymentNotConfirmedException` | `402` | `PAYMENT_NOT_CONFIRMED` |\n| `DuplicatePaymentException` | `409` | `PAYMENT_ALREADY_EXISTS` |\n| `CancellationNotAllowedException` | `409` | `CANCELLATION_NOT_ALLOWED` |\n| `RefundFailedException` | `502` | `REFUND_FAILED` `INVALID_WEBHOOK_SIGNATURE` |\n| `ForbiddenException` (non-owner) | `404` | `BOOKING_NOT_FOUND` |\n\n---\n\n## Stage 8 — Tests\n\n### `domain/` — Unit Tests\n\n**`BookingTest`**\n- cart confirmation.\n */\n@Entity\n@Table(name = \"bookings\")\npublic class Booking extends BaseEntity {\n\n    @Column(name = \"booking_ref\", nullable = false, unique = true)\n    private String bookingRef;\n\n    @Column(name = \"cart_id\", nullable = false)\n    private Long cartId;\n\n    @Column(name = \"user_id\", nullable = false)\n    private Long userId;\n\n    @Column(name = \"event_id\", nullable = false)\n    private Long eventId;\n\n    @Column(name = \"slot_id\", nullable = false)\n    private Long slotId;\n\n    @Enumerated(EnumType.STRING)\n    @Column(name = \"stripe_charge_id\")\n    private String stripeChargeId;\n\n    @Column(name = \"total_amount\", nullable = false)\n    private Long totalAmount;\n\n    @Column(name = \"currency\", nullable = false)\n    private String currency;\n\n    protected Booking() {}\n\n    public Booking(String bookingRef, Long cartId, Long userId, Long eventId,\n                   Long slotId, Long totalAmount, String currency) {\n        this.bookingRef = bookingRef;\n        this.cartId = cartId;\n        this.userId = userId;\n        this.eventId = eventId;\n        this.slotId = slotId;\n        this.totalAmount = totalAmount;\n        this.currency = currency;\n        this.status = BookingStatus.PENDING;\n    }\n\n    public void confirm(String stripePaymentIntentId, String stripeChargeId) {\n        this.status = BookingStatus.CONFIRMED;\n        this.stripePaymentIntentId = stripePaymentIntentId;\n        this.stripeChargeId = stripeChargeId;\n    }\n\n    public void cancel() {\n        this.status = BookingStatus.CANCELLED;\n    }\n\n    public String getBookingRef() { return bookingRef; }\n    public Long getCartId() { return cartId; }\n    public Long getUserId() { return ## Entry 83
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-04T17:23:43.807Z
**Role/Type:** response_item

```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T17:23:43.807Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_c0bVj8EkS4cn5GEUZi1uxA6s",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 5af9ed\nWall time: 0.0511 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 620\nOutput:\npackage com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.api.internal;\n\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.domain.Booking;\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.domain.Payment;\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.repository.BookingRepository;\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.repository.PaymentRepository;\nimport java.util.Optional;\nimport org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity;\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.GetMapping;\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.PathVariable;\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RequestMapping;\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RestController;\n\n/**\n * Internal endpoints consumed only by other modules via REST (never called by\n * the external client).\n *\n * <p>Secured by the JWT filter (ROLE_SYSTEM or any authenticated user) — the\n * PaymentStatusClient in booking-inventory calls this without a token because\n * it runs as a server-side scheduled task. The endpoint is restricted to\n * internal network access via load balancer / Kubernetes NetworkPolicy.\n */\n@RestController\n@RequestMapping(\"/internal/payments\")\npublic class InternalPaymentController {\n\n    private final BookingRepository bookingRepository;\n    private final PaymentRepository paymentRepository;\n\n    public InternalPaymentController(BookingRepository bookingRepository,\n                                     PaymentRepository paymentRepository) {\n        this.bookingRepository = bookingRepository;\n        this.paymentRepository = paymentRepository;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Returns the payment status for a given cart ID.\n     * Used by booking-inventory's PaymentTimeoutWatchdog.\n     *\n     * @param cartId the cart ID from booking-inventory.\n     * @return {@code {\"status\":\"SUCCEEDED\"}} or {@code 404 Not Found} if no payment exists.\n     */\n    @GetMapping(\"/by-cart/{cartId}/status\")\n    public ResponseEntity<PaymentStatusDto> getPaymentStatusByCart(@PathVariable Long cartId) {\n        Optional<Booking> booking = bookingRepository.findByCartId(cartId);\n        if (booking.isEmpty()) {\n            return ResponseEntity.notFound().build();\n        }\n        Optional<Payment> payment = paymentRepository.findByBookingId(booking.get().getId());\n        if (payment.isEmpty()) {\n            return ResponseEntity.notFound().build();\n        }\n        return ResponseEntity.ok(new PaymentStatusDto(payment.get().getStatus().name()));\n    }\n\n    public record PaymentStatusDto(String status) {}\n}\n"
  }
}
```

The endpoint is restricted to\n * internal network access via load balancer / Kubernetes NetworkPolicy.\n */\n@RestController\n@RequestMapping(\"/internal/payments\")\npublic class InternalPaymentController {\n\n    private final BookingRepository bookingRepository;\n    private final PaymentRepository paymentRepository;\n\n    public InternalPaymentController(BookingRepository bookingRepository,\n                                     PaymentRepository paymentRepository) {\n        this.bookingRepository = bookingRepository;\n        this.paymentRepository = paymentRepository;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Returns the payment status for a given cart ID.\n     * Used by booking-inventory's PaymentTimeoutWatchdog.\n     *\n     * @param cartId the cart ID from booking-inventory.\n     * @return {@code {\"status\":\"SUCCEEDED\"}} or {@code 404 Not Found} if no payment exists.\n     */\n    @GetMapping(\"/by-cart/{cartId}/status\")\n    public ResponseEntity<PaymentStatusDto> getPaymentStatusByCart(@PathVariable Long cartId) {\n        Optional<Booking> booking = bookingRepository.findByCartId(cartId);\n        if (booking.isEmpty()) {\n            return ResponseEntity.notFound().build();\n        }\n        Optional<Payment> payment = paymentRepository.findByBookingId(booking.get().getId());\n        if (payment.isEmpty()) {\n            return ResponseEntity.notFound().build();\n        }\n        return ResponseEntity.ok(new PaymentStatusDto(payment.get().getStatus().name()));\n    }\n\n    public record PaymentStatusDto(String status) {}\n}\n"
