BookingController {\\n\\n    private final BookingService bookingService;\\n    private final CancellationService cancellationService;\\n\\n    public BookingController(BookingService bookingService, CancellationService cancellationService) {\\n        this.bookingService = bookingService;\\n        this.cancellationService = cancellationService;\\n    }\\n\\n    @GetMapping\\n    @PreAuthorize(\\\"hasRole('\\\" + Roles.USER + \\\"')\\\")\\n    public Page<BookingSummaryResponse> listBookings(\\n        Authentication authentication,\\n        @PageableDefault(size = 10) Pageable pageable\\n    ) {\\n        AuthenticatedUser user = (AuthenticatedUser) authentication.getPrincipal();\\n        return bookingService.listBookings(user.userId(), pageable);\\n    }\\n\\n    @GetMapping(\\\"/{bookingRef}\\\")\\n    @PreAuthorize(\\\"hasRole('\\\" + Roles.USER + \\\"')\\\")\\n    public BookingResponse getBooking(Authentication authentication, @PathVariable String bookingRef) {\\n        AuthenticatedUser user = (AuthenticatedUser) authentication.getPrincipal();\\n        return bookingService.getBooking(user.userId(), bookingRef);\\n    }\\n\\n    @PostMapping(\\\"/{bookingRef}/cancel\\\")\\n    @PreAuthorize(\\\"hasRole('\\\" + Roles.USER + \\\"')\\\")\\n    public CancellationResponse cancel(\\n        Authentication authentication,\\n        @PathVariable String bookingRef,\\n        @Valid @RequestBody CancellationRequest request\\n    ) {\\n        AuthenticatedUser user = (AuthenticatedUser) authentication.getPrincipal();\\n        return cancellationService.cancel(user.userId(), bookingRef, request.reason());\\n    }\\n}\\nEOF\\n\\ncat > payments-ticketing/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/paymentsticketing/api/controller/ETicketController.java <<'EOF'\\npackage com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.api.controller;\\n\\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.api.dto.response.TicketsByBookingResponse;\\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.service.ETicketService;\\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.security.AuthenticatedUser;\\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.security.Roles;\\nimport org.springframework.security.access.prepost.PreAuthorize;\\nimport org.springframework.security.core.Authentication;\\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.GetMapping;\\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.PathVariable;\\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RequestMapping;\\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RestController;\\n\\n@RestController\\n@RequestMapping(\\\"/api/v1/tickets\\\")\\npublic class ETicketController {\\n\\n    private final ETicketService eTicketService;\\n\\n    public ETicketController(ETicketService eTicketService) {\\n        this.eTicketService = eTicketService;\\n    }\\n\\n    @GetMapping(\\\"/{bookingRef}\\\")\\n    @PreAuthorize(\\\"hasRole('\\\" + Roles.USER + \\\"')\\\")\\n    public TicketsByBookingResponse getTickets(Authentication authentication, @PathVariable String bookingRef) {\\n        AuthenticatedUser user = (AuthenticatedUser) authentication.getPrincipal();\\n        return eTicketService.getTickets(user.userId(), bookingRef);\\n    }\\n}\\nEOF\\n\\ncat > payments-ticketing/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/paymentsticketing/api/controller/StripeWebhookController.java <<'EOF'\\npackage com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.api.controller;\\n\\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.service.PaymentService;\\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.service.RefundService;\\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.stripe.dto.StripeWebhookEvent;\\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.stripe.webhook.StripeWebhookHandler;\\nimport com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.JsonNode;\\nimport com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.ObjectMapper;\\nimport java.util.Map;\\nimport org.slf4j.Logger;\\nimport org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;\\nimport org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity;\\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.PostMapping;\\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RequestBody;\\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RequestHeader;\\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RequestMapping;\\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RestController;\\n\\n@RestController\\n@RequestMapping(\\\"/api/v1/webhooks/stripe\\\")\\npublic class StripeWebhookController {\\n\\n    private static final Logger log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(StripeWebhookController.class);\\n\\n    private final StripeWebhookHandler stripeWebhookHandler;\\n    private final PaymentService paymentService;\\n    private final RefundService refundService;\\n    private final ObjectMapper objectMapper;\\n\\n    public StripeWebhookController(\\n        StripeWebhookHandler stripeWebhookHandler,\\n        PaymentService paymentService,\\n        RefundService refundService,\\n        ObjectMapper objectMapper\\n    ) {\\n        this.stripeWebhookHandler = stripeWebhookHandler;\\n        this.paymentService = paymentService;\\n        this.refundService = refundService;\\n        this.objectMapper = objectMapper;\\n    }\\n\\n    @PostMapping\\n    public ResponseEntity<Map<String, String>> handleWebhook(\\n        @RequestBody String payload,\\n        @RequestHeader(\\\"Stripe-Signature\\\") String signature\\n    ) {\\n        StripeWebhookEvent event = stripeWebhookHandler.parse(payload, signature);\\n\\n        switch (event.type()) {\\n            case \\\"payment_intent.succeeded\\\" -> paymentService.confirmPaymentFromWebhook(event.objectId());\\n            case \\\"payment_intent.payment_failed\\\", \\\"payment_intent.canceled\\\" -> {\\n                JsonNode root = readJson(event.rawJson());\\n                JsonNode objectNode = root.path(\\\"data\\\").path(\\\"object\\\");\\n                String failureCode = objectNode.path(\\\"last_payment_error\\\").path(\\\"code\\\").asText(null);\\n                String failureMessage = objectNode.path(\\\"last_payment_error\\\").path(\\\"message\\\").asText(null);\\n                paymentService.handleFailure(event.objectId(), failureCode, failureMessage);\\n            }\\n            case \\\"refund.updated\\\" -> refundService.updateRefundStatus(event.objectId());\\n            case \\\"refund.failed\\\" -> refundService.updateRefundStatus(event.objectId());\\n            default -> log.debug(\\\"Ignoring unsupported Stripe webhook event type={} eventId={}\\\", event.type(), event.id());\\n        }\\n\\n        return ResponseEntity.ok(Map.of(\\\"status\\\", \\\"ok\\\"));\\n    }\\n\\n    private JsonNode readJson(String payload) {\\n        try {\\n            return objectMapper.readTree(payload);\\n        } catch (Exception ex) {\\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\\\"Unable to parse Stripe webhook payload\\\", ex);\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\nEOF\"}",
    "call_id": "call_E8NSi5Y57z4HvQtj0ZBo3c6a"
  }
}

@PreAuthorize(\\\"hasRole('\\\" + Roles.USER + \\\"')\\\")\\n    public Page<BookingSummaryResponse> listBookings(\\n        Authentication authentication,\\n        @PageableDefault(size = 10) Pageable pageable\\n    ) {\\n        AuthenticatedUser user = (AuthenticatedUser) authentication.getPrincipal();\\n        return bookingService.listBookings(user.userId(), pageable);\\n    }\\n\\n    @GetMapping(\\\"/{bookingRef}\\\")\\n    @PreAuthorize(\\\"hasRole('\\\" + Roles.USER + \\\"')\\\")\\n    public BookingResponse getBooking(Authentication authentication, @PathVariable String bookingRef) {\\n        AuthenticatedUser user = (AuthenticatedUser) authentication.getPrincipal();\\n        return bookingService.getBooking(user.userId(), bookingRef);\\n    }\\n\\n    @PostMapping(\\\"/{bookingRef}/cancel\\\")\\n    @PreAuthorize(\\\"hasRole('\\\" + Roles.USER + \\\"')\\\")\\n    public CancellationResponse cancel(\\n        Authentication authentication,\\n        @PathVariable String bookingRef,\\n        @Valid @RequestBody CancellationRequest request\\n    ) {\\n        AuthenticatedUser user = (AuthenticatedUser) authentication.getPrincipal();\\n        return cancellationService.cancel(user.userId(), bookingRef, request.reason());\\n    }\\n}\\nEOF\\n\\ncat > payments-ticketing/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/paymentsticketing/api/controller/ETicketController.java <<'EOF'\\npackage com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.api.controller;\\n\\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.api.dto.response.TicketsByBookingResponse;\\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.service.ETicketService;\\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.security.AuthenticatedUser;\\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.security.Roles;\\nimport org.springframework.security.access.prepost.PreAuthorize;\\nimport org.springframework.security.core.Authentication;\\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.GetMapping;\\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.PathVariable;\\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RequestMapping;\\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RestController;\\n\\n@RestController\\n@RequestMapping(\\\"/api/v1/tickets\\\")\\npublic class ETicketController {\\n\\n    private final ETicketService eTicketService;\\n\\n    public ETicketController(ETicketService eTicketService) {\\n        this.eTicketService = eTicketService;\\n    }\\n\\n    @GetMapping(\\\"/{bookingRef}\\\")\\n    @PreAuthorize(\\\"hasRole('\\\" + Roles.USER + \\\"')\\\")\\n    public TicketsByBookingResponse getTickets(Authentication authentication, @PathVariable String bookingRef) {\\n        AuthenticatedUser user = (AuthenticatedUser) authentication.getPrincipal();\\n        return eTicketService.getTickets(user.userId(), bookingRef);\\n    }\\n}\\nEOF\\n\\ncat > payments-ticketing/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/paymentsticketing/api/controller/StripeWebhookController.java <<'EOF'\\npackage com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.api.controller;\\n\\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.service.PaymentService;\\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.service.RefundService;\\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.stripe.dto.StripeWebhookEvent;\\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.stripe.webhook.StripeWebhookHandler;\\nimport com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.JsonNode;\\nimport com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.ObjectMapper;\\nimport java.util.Map;\\nimport org.slf4j.Logger;\\nimport org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;\\nimport org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity;\\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.PostMapping;\\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RequestBody;\\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RequestHeader;\\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RequestMapping;\\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RestController;\\n\\n@RestController\\n@RequestMapping(\\\"/api/v1/webhooks/stripe\\\")\\npublic class StripeWebhookController {\\n\\n    private static final Logger log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(StripeWebhookController.class);\\n\\n    private final StripeWebhookHandler stripeWebhookHandler;\\n    private final PaymentService paymentService;\\n    private final RefundService refundService;\\n    private final ObjectMapper objectMapper;\\n\\n    public StripeWebhookController(\\n        StripeWebhookHandler stripeWebhookHandler,\\n        PaymentService paymentService,\\n        RefundService refundService,\\n        ObjectMapper objectMapper\\n    ) {\\n        this.stripeWebhookHandler = stripeWebhookHandler;\\n        this.paymentService = paymentService;\\n        this.refundService = refundService;\\n        this.objectMapper = objectMapper;\\n    }\\n\\n    @PostMapping\\n    public ResponseEntity<Map<String, String>> handleWebhook(\\n        @RequestBody String payload,\\n        @RequestHeader(\\\"Stripe-Signature\\\") String signature\\n    ) {\\n        StripeWebhookEvent event = stripeWebhookHandler.parse(payload, signature);\\n\\n        switch (event.type()) {\\n            case \\\"payment_intent.succeeded\\\" -> paymentService.confirmPaymentFromWebhook(event.objectId());\\n            case \\\"payment_intent.payment_failed\\\", \\\"payment_intent.canceled\\\" -> {\\n                JsonNode root = readJson(event.rawJson());\\n                JsonNode objectNode = root.path(\\\"data\\\").path(\\\"object\\\");\\n                String failureCode = objectNode.path(\\\"last_payment_error\\\").path(\\\"code\\\").asText(null);\\n                String failureMessage = objectNode.path(\\\"last_payment_error\\\").path(\\\"message\\\").asText(null);\\n                paymentService.handleFailure(event.objectId(), failureCode, failureMessage);\\n            }\\n            case \\\"refund.updated\\\" -> refundService.updateRefundStatus(event.objectId());\\n            case \\\"refund.failed\\\" -> refundService.updateRefundStatus(event.objectId());\\n            default -> log.debug(\\\"Ignoring unsupported Stripe webhook event type={} eventId={}\\\", event.type(), event.id());\\n        }\\n\\n        return ResponseEntity.ok(Map.of(\\\"status\\\", \\\"ok\\\"));\\n    }\\n\\n    private JsonNode readJson(String payload) {\\n        try {\\n            return objectMapper.readTree(payload);\\n        } catch (Exception ex) {\\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\\\"Unable to parse Stripe webhook payload\\\", ex);\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\nEOF\"}",
    "call_id": "call_E8NSi5Y57z4HvQtj0ZBo3c6a"
  }
}

{

  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call",
    "name": "exec_command",
    "arguments": "{\"cmd\":\"cat > payments-ticketing/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/paymentsticketing/event/listener/CartAssembledListener.java <<'EOF'\\npackage com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.event.listener;\\n\\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.service.PaymentService;\\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.common.event.published.CartAssembledEvent;\\nimport org.springframework.context.event.EventListener;\\nimport org.springframework.stereotype.Component;\\n\\n@Component\\npublic class CartAssembledListener {\\n\\n    private final PaymentService paymentService;\\n\\n    public CartAssembledListener(PaymentService paymentService) {\\n        this.paymentService = paymentService;\\n    }\\n\\n    @EventListener\\n    public void onCartAssembled(CartAssembledEvent event) {\\n        paymentService.createCheckout(event);\\n    }\\n}\\nEOF\"}",
    "call_id": "call_1K0fwNGDAAlrgaQf2XvD6RzR"
  }
}
```

## Entry 425
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-04T17:33:00.602Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T17:33:00.602Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "custom_tool_call",
    "status": "completed",
    "call_id": "call_t1YBxh6um8X7JP6M1GgoPp5Y",
    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: shared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/stripe/service/StripePaymentService.java\n@@\n             PaymentIntentCreateParams params = PaymentIntentCreateParams.builder()\n                 .setAmount(request.amount())\n                 .setCurrency(request.currency())\n                 .setReceiptEmail(request.receiptEmail())\n                 .setDescription(request.description())\n                 .setCaptureMethod(PaymentIntentCreateParams.CaptureMethod.valueOf(\n                     stripeConfig.getCaptureMethod().toUpperCase().replace(\"-\", \"_\")))\n+                .putAllMetadata(request.metadata() == null ? java.util.Map.of() : request.metadata())\n                 .build();\n@@\n         return new StripePaymentIntentResponse(\n             intent.getId(),\n             intent.getClientSecret(),\n             intent.getStatus(),\n             intent.getAmount(),\n-            intent.getCurrency()\n+            intent.getCurrency(),\n+            intent.getLatestCharge() == null ? null : intent.getLatestCharge()\n         );\n     }\n }\n*** End Patch\n"
  }
}
```

void should_return_response_when_createPaymentIntent_succeeds() throws Exception {\n        PaymentIntent mockIntent = mock(PaymentIntent.class);\n        when(mockIntent.getId()).thenReturn(\"pi_test_123\");\n        when(mockIntent.getClientSecret()).thenReturn(\"pi_test_123_secret\");\n        when(mockIntent.getStatus()).thenReturn(\"requires_payment_method\");\n        when(mockIntent.getAmount()).thenReturn(50000L);\n        when(mockIntent.getCurrency()).thenReturn(\"inr\");\n\n assertThat(response.paymentIntentId()).isEqualTo(\"pi_test_123\");\n            assertThat(response.clientSecret()).isEqualTo(\"pi_test_123_secret\");\n            assertThat(response.status()).isEqualTo(\"requires_payment_method\");\n            assertThat(response.amount()).isEqualTo(50000L);\n            assertThat(response.currency()).isEqualTo(\"inr\");\n mockStatic(PaymentIntent.class)) {\n            staticMock.when(() -> PaymentIntent.create(\n                any(PaymentIntentCreateParams.class),\n                any(RequestOptions.class)\n            )).thenThrow(new ApiException(\"Stripe error\", \"req_1\", null, 400, null));\n\n            StripePaymentIntentRequest request = new StripePaymentIntentRequest(\n                1000L, \"inr\", \"user@test.com\", \"Test\", \"idem-key-2\"\n            );\n\n            assertThatThrownBy(() -> service.createPaymentIntent(request))\n                .isInstanceOf(StripeIntegrationException.class)\n                .hasMessageContaining(\"Failed to create PaymentIntent\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_return_response_when_getPaymentIntent_succeeds() throws Exception {\n        PaymentIntent mockIntent = mock(PaymentIntent.class);\n        when(mockIntent.getId()).thenReturn(\"pi_retrieve_456\");\n        when(mockIntent.getClientSecret()).thenReturn(\"pi_retrieve_456_secret\");\n        when(mockIntent.getStatus()).thenReturn(\"succeeded\");\n        when(mockIntent.getAmount()).thenReturn(20000L);\n        when(mockIntent.getCurrency()).thenReturn(\"inr\");\n\n        try (MockedStatic<PaymentIntent> staticMock = mockStatic(PaymentIntent.class)) {\n            staticMock.when(() -> PaymentIntent.retrieve(\"pi_retrieve_456\"))\n                .thenReturn(mockIntent);\n\n            StripePaymentIntentResponse response = service.getPaymentIntent(\"pi_retrieve_456\");\n\n            assertThat(response.paymentIntentId()).isEqualTo(\"pi_retrieve_456\");\n            assertThat(response.status()).isEqualTo(\"succeeded\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_throw_StripeIntegrationException_when_getPaymentIntent_fails() throws Exception {\n        try (MockedStatic<PaymentIntent> staticMock = mockStatic(PaymentIntent.class)) {\n            staticMock.when(() -> PaymentIntent.retrieve(\"pi_missing\"))\n                .thenThrow(new ApiException(\"Not found\", \"req_2\", null, 404, null));\n\n            assertThatThrownBy(() -> service.getPaymentIntent(\"pi_missing\"))\n                .isInstanceOf(StripeIntegrationException.class)\n                .hasMessageContaining(\"pi_missing\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_throw_StripeIntegrationException_when_cancelPaymentIntent_fails() throws Exception {\n        PaymentIntent mockIntent = mock(PaymentIntent.class);\n\n        try (MockedStatic<PaymentIntent> staticMock = mockStatic(PaymentIntent.class)) {\n            staticMock.when(() -> PaymentIntent.retrieve(\"pi_cancel_fail\"))\n                .thenReturn(mockIntent);\n assertThatThrownBy(() -> service.cancelPaymentIntent(\"pi_cancel_fail\"))\n                .isInstanceOf(StripeIntegrationException.class)\n                .hasMessageContaining(\"pi_cancel_fail\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_successfully_cancel_payment_intent() throws Exception {\n        PaymentIntent mockIntent = mock(PaymentIntent.class);\n        PaymentIntent cancelledIntent = mock(PaymentIntent.class);\n        when(cancelledIntent.getStatus()).thenReturn(\"canceled\");\n\n        try (MockedStatic<PaymentIntent> staticMock = mockStatic(PaymentIntent.class)) {\n            staticMock.when(() -> PaymentIntent.retrieve(\"pi_cancel_ok\"))\n                .thenReturn(mockIntent);\n            when(mockIntent.cancel(any(PaymentIntentCancelParams.class)))\n                .thenReturn(cancelledIntent);\n\n            // Should not throw\n            service.cancelPaymentIntent(\"pi_cancel_ok\");\n        }\n    }\n}\n"
  }

{

  "payload": {

org.assertj.core.api.Assertions.assertThat;\nimport static org.assertj.core.api.Assertions.assertThatThrownBy;\nimport static org.mockito.Mockito.mock;\nimport static org.mockito.Mockito.mockStatic;\nimport static org.mockito.Mockito.when;\n\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.stripe.config.StripeConfig;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.stripe.dto.StripeWebhookEvent;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.stripe.exception.StripeWebhookSignatureException;\nimport com.stripe.exception.SignatureVerificationException;\nimport com.stripe.model.Event;\nimport com.stripe.model.EventDataObjectDeserializer;\nimport com.stripe.model.StripeObject;\nimport com.stripe.net.Webhook;\nimport java.util.Optional;\nimport org.junit.jupiter.api.BeforeEach;\nimport org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;\nimport org.mockito.MockedStatic;\n\nclass StripeWebhookHandlerTest {\n\n    private StripeConfig stripeConfig;\n    private StripeWebhookHandler handler;\n\n    private static final String WEBHOOK_SECRET = \"whsec_test_secret\";\n    private static final String PAYLOAD = \"{\\\"id\\\":\\\"evt_test_1\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"payment_intent.succeeded\\\"}\";\n    private static final String SIG_HEADER = \"t=12345,v1=abc123\";\n\n    @BeforeEach\n    void setUp() {\n        stripeConfig = mock(StripeConfig.class);\n        when(stripeConfig.getWebhookSecret()).thenReturn(WEBHOOK_SECRET);\n        handler = new StripeWebhookHandler(stripeConfig);\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_parse_valid_webhook_and_return_event() throws Exception {\n        Event mockEvent = mock(Event.class);\n        EventDataObjectDeserializer deserializer = mock(EventDataObjectDeserializer.class);\n        when(mockEvent.getId()).thenReturn(\"evt_test_1\");\n        when(mockEvent.getType()).thenReturn(\"payment_intent.succeeded\");\n        when(mockEvent.getDataObjectDeserializer()).thenReturn(deserializer);\n        when(deserializer.getObject()).thenReturn(Optional.empty());\n\n        try (MockedStatic<Webhook> staticMock = mockStatic(Webhook.class)) {\n            staticMock.when(() -> Webhook.constructEvent(PAYLOAD, SIG_HEADER, WEBHOOK_SECRET))\n                .thenReturn(mockEvent);\n\n            StripeWebhookEvent result = handler.parse(PAYLOAD, SIG_HEADER);\n\n            assertThat(result.id()).isEqualTo(\"evt_test_1\");\n            assertThat(result.type()).isEqualTo(\"payment_intent.succeeded\");\n            assertThat(result.rawJson()).isEqualTo(PAYLOAD);\n            assertThat(result.objectId()).isNull();\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_extract_objectId_when_stripe_object_has_getId() throws Exception {\n        StripeObject stripeObj = mock(StripeObject.class);\n        // StripeObject implements getId() at runtime on subtypes, but here we stub via a concrete mock\n        // We use reflection in the handler; since mock returns null by default for getId(), we test with null objectId\n        Event mockEvent = mock(Event.class);\n        EventDataObjectDeserializer deserializer = mock(EventDataObjectDeserializer.class);\n        when(mockEvent.getId()).thenReturn(\"evt_test_2\");\n        when(mockEvent.getType()).thenReturn(\"charge.succeeded\");\n        when(mockEvent.getDataObjectDeserializer()).thenReturn(deserializer);\n        when(deserializer.getObject()).thenReturn(Optional.of(stripeObj));\n\n        try (MockedStatic<Webhook> staticMock = mockStatic(Webhook.class)) {\n            staticMock.when(() -> Webhook.constructEvent(PAYLOAD, SIG_HEADER, WEBHOOK_SECRET))\n                .thenReturn(mockEvent);\n\n            StripeWebhookEvent result = handler.parse(PAYLOAD, SIG_HEADER);\n\n            assertThat(result.id()).isEqualTo(\"evt_test_2\");\n            assertThat(result.type()).isEqualTo(\"charge.succeeded\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_throw_StripeWebhookSignatureException_when_signature_invalid() throws Exception {\n        try (MockedStatic<Webhook> staticMock = mockStatic(Webhook.class)) {\n            staticMock.when(() -> Webhook.constructEvent(PAYLOAD, \"bad_signature\", WEBHOOK_SECRET))\n                .thenThrow(new SignatureVerificationException(\"Invalid signature\", \"bad_signature\"));\n\n            assertThatThrownBy(() -> handler.parse(PAYLOAD, \"bad_signature\"))\n                .isInstanceOf(StripeWebhookSignatureException.class)\n                .hasMessageContaining(\"Invalid Stripe webhook signature\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_throw_StripeWebhookSignatureException_when_payload_tampered() throws Exception {\n        String tamperedPayload = PAYLOAD + \"TAMPERED\";\n        try (MockedStatic<Webhook> staticMock = mockStatic(Webhook.class)) {\n            staticMock.when(() -> Webhook.constructEvent(tamperedPayload, SIG_HEADER, WEBHOOK_SECRET))\n                .thenThrow(new SignatureVerificationException(\"Mismatch\", SIG_HEADER));\n\n            assertThatThrownBy(() -> handler.parse(tamperedPayload, SIG_HEADER))\n                .isInstanceOf(StripeWebhookSignatureException.class);\n        }\n    }\n}\n"
  }
}
```

org.assertj.core.api.Assertions.assertThat;\nimport static org.assertj.core.api.Assertions.assertThatThrownBy;\nimport static org.mockito.Mockito.mock;\nimport static org.mockito.Mockito.mockStatic;\nimport static org.mockito.Mockito.when;\n\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.stripe.config.StripeConfig;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.stripe.dto.StripeWebhookEvent;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.stripe.exception.StripeWebhookSignatureException;\nimport com.stripe.exception.SignatureVerificationException;\nimport com.stripe.model.Event;\nimport com.stripe.model.EventDataObjectDeserializer;\nimport com.stripe.model.StripeObject;\nimport com.stripe.net.Webhook;\nimport java.util.Optional;\nimport org.junit.jupiter.api.BeforeEach;\nimport org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;\nimport org.mockito.MockedStatic;\n\nclass StripeWebhookHandlerTest {\n\n    private StripeConfig stripeConfig;\n    private StripeWebhookHandler handler;\n\n    private static final String WEBHOOK_SECRET = \"whsec_test_secret\";\n    private static final String PAYLOAD = \"{\\\"id\\\":\\\"evt_test_1\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"payment_intent.succeeded\\\"}\";\n    private static final String SIG_HEADER = \"t=12345,v1=abc123\";\n\n    @BeforeEach\n    void setUp() {\n        stripeConfig = mock(StripeConfig.class);\n        when(stripeConfig.getWebhookSecret()).thenReturn(WEBHOOK_SECRET);\n        handler = new StripeWebhookHandler(stripeConfig);\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_parse_valid_webhook_and_return_event() throws Exception {\n        Event mockEvent = mock(Event.class);\n        EventDataObjectDeserializer deserializer = mock(EventDataObjectDeserializer.class);\n        when(mockEvent.getId()).thenReturn(\"evt_test_1\");\n        when(mockEvent.getType()).thenReturn(\"payment_intent.succeeded\");\n        when(mockEvent.getDataObjectDeserializer()).thenReturn(deserializer);\n        when(deserializer.getObject()).thenReturn(Optional.empty());\n\n        try (MockedStatic<Webhook> staticMock = mockStatic(Webhook.class)) {\n            staticMock.when(() -> Webhook.constructEvent(PAYLOAD, SIG_HEADER, WEBHOOK_SECRET))\n                .thenReturn(mockEvent);\n\n            StripeWebhookEvent result = handler.parse(PAYLOAD, SIG_HEADER);\n\n            assertThat(result.id()).isEqualTo(\"evt_test_1\");\n            assertThat(result.type()).isEqualTo(\"payment_intent.succeeded\");\n            assertThat(result.rawJson()).isEqualTo(PAYLOAD);\n            assertThat(result.objectId()).isNull();\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_extract_objectId_when_stripe_object_has_getId() throws Exception {\n        StripeObject stripeObj = mock(StripeObject.class);\n        // StripeObject implements getId() at runtime on subtypes, but here we stub via a concrete mock\n        // We use reflection in the handler; since mock returns null by default for getId(), we test with null objectId\n        Event mockEvent = mock(Event.class);\n        EventDataObjectDeserializer deserializer = mock(EventDataObjectDeserializer.class);\n        when(mockEvent.getId()).thenReturn(\"evt_test_2\");\n        when(mockEvent.getType()).thenReturn(\"charge.succeeded\");\n        when(mockEvent.getDataObjectDeserializer()).thenReturn(deserializer);\n        when(deserializer.getObject()).thenReturn(Optional.of(stripeObj));\n\n        try (MockedStatic<Webhook> staticMock = mockStatic(Webhook.class)) {\n            staticMock.when(() -> Webhook.constructEvent(PAYLOAD, SIG_HEADER, WEBHOOK_SECRET))\n                .thenReturn(mockEvent);\n\n            StripeWebhookEvent result = handler.parse(PAYLOAD, SIG_HEADER);\n\n            assertThat(result.id()).isEqualTo(\"evt_test_2\");\n            assertThat(result.type()).isEqualTo(\"charge.succeeded\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_throw_StripeWebhookSignatureException_when_signature_invalid() throws Exception {\n        try (MockedStatic<Webhook> staticMock = mockStatic(Webhook.class)) {\n            staticMock.when(() -> Webhook.constructEvent(PAYLOAD, \"bad_signature\", WEBHOOK_SECRET))\n                .thenThrow(new SignatureVerificationException(\"Invalid signature\", \"bad_signature\"));\n\n            assertThatThrownBy(() -> handler.parse(PAYLOAD, \"bad_signature\"))\n                .isInstanceOf(StripeWebhookSignatureException.class)\n                .hasMessageContaining(\"Invalid Stripe webhook signature\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_throw_StripeWebhookSignatureException_when_payload_tampered() throws Exception {\n        String tamperedPayload = PAYLOAD + \"TAMPERED\";\n        try (MockedStatic<Webhook> staticMock = mockStatic(Webhook.class)) {\n            staticMock.when(() -> Webhook.constructEvent(tamperedPayload, SIG_HEADER, WEBHOOK_SECRET))\n                .thenThrow(new SignatureVerificationException(\"Mismatch\", SIG_HEADER));\n\n            assertThatThrownBy(() -> handler.parse(tamperedPayload, SIG_HEADER))\n                .isInstanceOf(StripeWebhookSignatureException.class);\n        }\n    }\n}\n"
  }
}

    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: shared/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/shared/stripe/service/StripePaymentServiceTest.java\n@@\n         when(mockIntent.getStatus()).thenReturn(\"requires_payment_method\");\n         when(mockIntent.getAmount()).thenReturn(50000L);\n         when(mockIntent.getCurrency()).thenReturn(\"inr\");\n+        when(mockIntent.getLatestCharge()).thenReturn(\"ch_test_latest\");\n@@\n             StripePaymentIntentRequest request = new StripePaymentIntentRequest(\n-                50000L, \"inr\", \"user@test.com\", \"Test booking\", \"idem-key-1\"\n+                50000L, \"inr\", \"user@test.com\", \"Test booking\", \"idem-key-1\", java.util.Map.of(\"booking_ref\", \"BK-1\")\n             );\n@@\n             assertThat(response.status()).isEqualTo(\"requires_payment_method\");\n             assertThat(response.amount()).isEqualTo(50000L);\n             assertThat(response.currency()).isEqualTo(\"inr\");\n+            assertThat(response.latestCharge()).isEqualTo(\"ch_test_latest\");\n@@\n             StripePaymentIntentRequest request = new StripePaymentIntentRequest(\n-                1000L, \"inr\", \"user@test.com\", \"Test\", \"idem-key-2\"\n+                1000L, \"inr\", \"user@test.com\", \"Test\", \"idem-key-2\", java.util.Map.of()\n             );\n@@\n         when(mockIntent.getStatus()).thenReturn(\"succeeded\");\n         when(mockIntent.getAmount()).thenReturn(20000L);\n         when(mockIntent.getCurrency()).thenReturn(\"inr\");\n+        when(mockIntent.getLatestCharge()).thenReturn(\"ch_123\");\n*** End Patch\n"
  }
}

## Entry 456
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-04T17:33:31.429Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T17:33:31.429Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call",
    "name": "exec_command",
    "arguments": "{\"cmd\":\"cat > booking-inventory/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/api/internal/InternalCartQueryController.java <<'EOF'\\npackage com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.api.internal;\\n\\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.domain.CartItem;\\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.repository.CartItemRepository;\\nimport java.util.List;\\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.GetMapping;\\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.PathVariable;\\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RequestMapping;\\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RestController;\\n\\n@RestController\\n@RequestMapping(\\\"/internal/booking/carts\\\")\\npublic class InternalCartQueryController {\\n\\n    private final CartItemRepository cartItemRepository;\\n\\n    public InternalCartQueryController(CartItemRepository cartItemRepository) {\\n        this.cartItemRepository = cartItemRepository;\\n    }\\n\\n    @GetMapping(\\\"/{cartId}/items\\\")\\n    public List<CartItemSnapshotResponse> getCartItems(@PathVariable Long cartId) {\\n        return cartItemRepository.findByCartId(cartId).stream()\\n            .map(this::toResponse)\\n            .toList();\\n    }\\n\\n    private CartItemSnapshotResponse toResponse(CartItem item) {\\n        return new CartItemSnapshotResponse(\\n            item.getId(),\\n            item.getSeatId(),\\n            item.getGaClaimId(),\\n            item.getEbTicketClassId(),\\n            item.getBasePrice().amount().longValue(),\\n            item.getBasePrice().currency().toLowerCase(),\\n            item.getQuantity()\\n        );\\n    }\\n\\n    public record CartItemSnapshotResponse(\\n        Long itemId,\\n        Long seatId,\\n        Long gaClaimId,\\n        String ticketClassId,\\n        Long unitPrice,\\n        String currency,\\n        Integer quantity\\n    ) {\\n    }\\n}\\nEOF\"}",
    "call_id": "call_3gdSxfLTv8q27HarlauF1Dde"
  }
}
```
**Metadata:**

    "arguments": "{\"cmd\":\"cat > booking-inventory/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/api/internal/InternalCartQueryController.java <<'EOF'\\npackage com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.api.internal;\\n\\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.domain.CartItem;\\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.repository.CartItemRepository;\\nimport java.util.List;\\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.GetMapping;\\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.PathVariable;\\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RequestMapping;\\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RestController;\\n\\n@RestController\\n@RequestMapping(\\\"/internal/booking/carts\\\")\\npublic class InternalCartQueryController {\\n\\n    private final CartItemRepository cartItemRepository;\\n\\n    public InternalCartQueryController(CartItemRepository cartItemRepository) {\\n        this.cartItemRepository = cartItemRepository;\\n    }\\n\\n    @GetMapping(\\\"/{cartId}/items\\\")\\n    public List<CartItemSnapshotResponse> getCartItems(@PathVariable Long cartId) {\\n        return cartItemRepository.findByCartId(cartId).stream()\\n            .map(this::toResponse)\\n            .toList();\\n    }\\n\\n    private CartItemSnapshotResponse toResponse(CartItem item) {\\n        return new CartItemSnapshotResponse(\\n            item.getId(),\\n            item.getSeatId(),\\n            item.getGaClaimId(),\\n            item.getEbTicketClassId(),\\n            item.getBasePrice().amount().longValue(),\\n            item.getBasePrice().currency().toLowerCase(),\\n            item.getQuantity()\\n        );\\n    }\\n\\n    public record CartItemSnapshotResponse(\\n        Long itemId,\\n        Long seatId,\\n        Long gaClaimId,\\n        String ticketClassId,\\n        Long unitPrice,\\n        String currency,\\n        Integer quantity\\n    ) {\\n    }\\n}\\nEOF\"}",

## Entry 461
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-04T17:33:38.509Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T17:33:38.509Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call",
    "name": "exec_command",
    "arguments": "{\"cmd\":\"mkdir -p scheduling/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/scheduling/api/internal && cat > scheduling/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/scheduling/api/internal/InternalSchedulingController.java <<'EOF'\\npackage com.eventplatform.scheduling.api.internal;\\n\\nimport com.eventplatform.scheduling.domain.ShowSlot;\\nimport com.eventplatform.scheduling.service.ShowSlotService;\\nimport java.time.Instant;\\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.GetMapping;\\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.PathVariable;\\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RequestMapping;\\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RestController;\\n\\n@RestController\\n@RequestMapping(\\\"/internal/scheduling/slots\\\")\\npublic class InternalSchedulingController {\\n\\n    private final ShowSlotService showSlotService;\\n\\n    public InternalSchedulingController(ShowSlotService showSlotService) {\\n        this.showSlotService = showSlotService;\\n    }\\n\\n    @GetMapping(\\\"/{slotId}/timing\\\")\\n    public SlotTimingResponse getSlotTiming(@PathVariable Long slotId) {\\n        ShowSlot slot = showSlotService.getSlot(slotId);\\n        return new SlotTimingResponse(slotId, slot.getStartTime().toInstant());\\n    }\\n\\n    public record SlotTimingResponse(Long slotId, Instant startTime) {\\n    }\\n}\\nEOF\"}",
    "call_id": "call_l1Ls4Ss6k4S9p8kk0qdQUnSC"
  }
}
```

    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: booking-inventory/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/service/client/PaymentStatusClient.java\n@@\n         try {\n             PaymentStatusResponse response = restClient.get()\n-                .uri(\"/internal/payments/by-cart/{cartId}/status\", cartId)\n+                .uri(\"/api/v1/internal/payments/by-cart/{cartId}\", cartId)\n                 .retrieve()\n                 .body(PaymentStatusResponse.class);\n-            return response != null && \"SUCCEEDED\".equalsIgnoreCase(response.status());\n+            return response != null && response.confirmed();\n         } catch (RestClientException ex) {\n             // payments-ticketing unavailable or cart has no payment record → safe to release\n             return false;\n         }\n     }\n \n     // DTO for the internal endpoint response\n-    public record PaymentStatusResponse(String status) {}\n+    public record PaymentStatusResponse(Long cartId, boolean confirmed) {}\n }\n*** End Patch\n"
  }
}

com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.domain.Booking;\n-import com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.domain.Payment;\n+import com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.domain.enums.BookingStatus;\n import com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.repository.BookingRepository;\n-import com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.repository.PaymentRepository;\n import com.eventplatform.shared.common.exception.GlobalExceptionHandler;\n import com.eventplatform.shared.security.JwtAuthenticationFilter;\n import com.eventplatform.shared.security.JwtTokenProvider;\n@@\n         BookingRepository bookingRepository() {\n             return mock(BookingRepository.class);\n         }\n-\n-        @Bean\n-        PaymentRepository paymentRepository() {\n-            return mock(PaymentRepository.class);\n-        }\n@@\n     @Autowired\n     private BookingRepository bookingRepository;\n \n-    @Autowired\n-    private PaymentRepository paymentRepository;\n-\n     @Test\n-    void should_return_payment_status_SUCCEEDED_when_payment_confirmed() throws Exception {\n+    void should_return_confirmed_true_when_booking_confirmed() throws Exception {\n         Booking booking = new Booking(\"B-001\", 100L, 1L, 10L, 20L, 50000L, \"inr\");\n-        ReflectionTestUtils.setField(booking, \"id\", 50L);\n-\n-        Payment payment = new Payment(50L, \"pi_test_abc\", 50000L, \"inr\");\n-        payment.markSucceeded(\"ch_test_abc\");\n-\n-        when(bookingRepository.findByCartId(100L)).thenReturn(Optional.of(booking));\n-        when(paymentRepository.findByBookingId(50L)).thenReturn(Optional.of(payment));\n-\n-        mockMvc.perform(get(\"/internal/payments/by-cart/100/status\"))\n+        booking.confirm(\"pi_test_abc\", \"ch_test_abc\");\n+        when(bookingRepository.existsByCartIdAndStatus(100L, BookingStatus.CONFIRMED)).thenReturn(true);\n+        mockMvc.perform(get(\"/api/v1/internal/payments/by-cart/100\"))\n             .andExpect(status().isOk())\n-            .andExpect(jsonPath(\"$.status\").value(\"SUCCEEDED\"));\n+            .andExpect(jsonPath(\"$.cartId\").value(100))\n+            .andExpect(jsonPath(\"$.confirmed\").value(true));\n     }\n \n     @Test\n-    void should_return_404_when_no_booking_found_for_cart() throws Exception {\n-        when(bookingRepository.findByCartId(999L)).thenReturn(Optional.empty());\n-\n-        mockMvc.perform(get(\"/internal/payments/by-cart/999/status\"))\n-            .andExpect(status().isNotFound());\n-    }\n-\n-    @Test\n-    void should_return_404_when_booking_exists_but_no_payment() throws Exception {\n-        Booking booking = new Booking(\"B-002\", 200L, 2L, 11L, 21L, 30000L, \"inr\");\n-        ReflectionTestUtils.setField(booking, \"id\", 60L);\n-\n-        when(bookingRepository.findByCartId(200L)).thenReturn(Optional.of(booking));\n-        when(paymentRepository.findByBookingId(60L)).thenReturn(Optional.empty());\n-\n-        mockMvc.perform(get(\"/internal/payments/by-cart/200/status\"))\n-            .andExpect(status().isNotFound());\n-    }\n-\n-    @Test\n-    void should_return_PENDING_status_when_payment_not_yet_confirmed() throws Exception {\n-        Booking booking = new Booking(\"B-003\", 300L, 3L, 12L, 22L, 40000L, \"inr\");\n-        ReflectionTestUtils.setField(booking, \"id\", 70L);\n-\n-        Payment payment = new Payment(70L, \"pi_pending_xyz\", 40000L, \"inr\");\n-\n-        when(bookingRepository.findByCartId(300L)).thenReturn(Optional.of(booking));\n-        when(paymentRepository.findByBookingId(70L)).thenReturn(Optional.of(payment));\n-\n-        mockMvc.perform(get(\"/internal/payments/by-cart/300/status\"))\n+    void should_return_confirmed_false_when_not_confirmed() throws Exception {\n+        when(bookingRepository.existsByCartIdAndStatus(300L, BookingStatus.CONFIRMED)).thenReturn(false);\n+        mockMvc.perform(get(\"/api/v1/internal/payments/by-cart/300\"))\n             .andExpect(status().isOk())\n-            .andExpect(jsonPath(\"$.status\").value(\"PENDING\"));\n+            .andExpect(jsonPath(\"$.cartId\").value(300))\n+            .andExpect(jsonPath(\"$.confirmed\").value(false));\n     }\n }\n*** End Patch\n"
  }
}

org.springframework.test.web.servlet.result.MockMvcResultMatchers.jsonPath;\nimport static org.springframework.test.web.servlet.result.MockMvcResultMatchers.status;\n\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.domain.Booking;\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.domain.enums.BookingStatus;\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.PaymentsTicketingTestApplication.class)\n@Import({GlobalExceptionHandler.class, SecurityConfig.class, InternalPaymentControllerTest.MockBeans.class})\nclass mock(BookingRepository.class);\n        }\n\n        @Bean\n        JwtTokenProvider jwtTokenProvider() {\n            return mock(JwtTokenProvider.class);\n        }\n\n        @Bean\n booking = new Booking(\"B-001\", 100L, 1L, 10L, 20L, 50000L, \"inr\");\n        booking.confirm(\"pi_test_abc\", \"ch_test_abc\");\n        when(bookingRepository.existsByCartIdAndStatus(100L, BookingStatus.CONFIRMED)).thenReturn(true);\n        mockMvc.perform(get(\"/api/v1/internal/payments/by-cart/100\"))\n            .andExpect(status().isOk())\n            .andExpect(jsonPath(\"$.cartId\").value(100))\n            .andExpect(jsonPath(\"$.confirmed\").value(true));\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_return_confirmed_false_when_not_confirmed() throws     .andExpect(status().isOk())\n            .andExpect(jsonPath(\"$.cartId\").value(300))\n            .andExpect(jsonPath(\"$.confirmed\").value(false));\n    }\n}\n"
  }

    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: payments-ticketing/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/paymentsticketing/service/PaymentService.java\n@@\n     @Transactional(readOnly = true)\n     public CheckoutInitResponse getCheckout(Long cartId, Long userId) {\n@@\n         );\n     }\n+\n+    @Transactional(readOnly = true)\n+    public BookingResponse getBookingResponse(Long userId, String bookingRef) {\n+        Booking booking = bookingRepository.findByBookingRef(bookingRef)\n+            .orElseThrow(() -> new BookingNotFoundException(bookingRef));\n+        if (!Objects.equals(booking.getUserId(), userId)) {\n+            throw new BookingNotFoundException(bookingRef);\n+        }\n+        return buildBookingResponse(booking);\n+    }\n@@\n-    private BookingResponse buildBookingResponse(Booking booking) {\n+    private BookingResponse buildBookingResponse(Booking booking) {\n*** End Patch\n"
  }
}

{

  "type": "response_item",

    "type": "custom_tool_call",
    "status": "completed",
    "call_id": "call_aTLkrS4J074mSZ8ihShK4RCL",
    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: payments-ticketing/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/paymentsticketing/service/BookingService.java\n@@\n     @Transactional(readOnly = true)\n     public BookingResponse getBooking(Long userId, String bookingRef) {\n-        Booking booking = bookingRepository.findByBookingRef(bookingRef)\n-            .orElseThrow(() -> new BookingNotFoundException(bookingRef));\n-        if (!booking.getUserId().equals(userId)) {\n-            throw new BookingNotFoundException(bookingRef);\n-        }\n-\n-        return paymentService.confirmPayment(booking.getUserId(), booking.getStripePaymentIntentId());\n+        return paymentService.getBookingResponse(userId, bookingRef);\n     }\n*** End Patch\n"
  }
}
```

{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T17:34:44.137Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_4TBCCLzGzWOjrJTRRw2toAei",
    "output": "Chunk ID: ba922e\nWall time: 0.0510 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 872\nOutput:\npackage com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.service.client;\n\nimport static org.assertj.core.api.Assertions.assertThat;\nimport static org.mockito.ArgumentMatchers.any;\nimport static org.mockito.ArgumentMatchers.anyString;\nimport static org.mockito.Mockito.doReturn;\nimport static org.mockito.Mockito.doThrow;\nimport static org.mockito.Mockito.mock;\nimport static org.mockito.Mockito.when;\n\nimport org.junit.jupiter.api.BeforeEach;\nimport org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;\nimport org.springframework.test.util.ReflectionTestUtils;\nimport org.springframework.web.client.ResourceAccessException;\nimport org.springframework.web.client.RestClient;\n\n@SuppressWarnings({\"unchecked\", \"rawtypes\"})\nclass PaymentStatusClientTest {\n\n    private PaymentStatusClient client;\n    private RestClient mockRestClient;\n    private RestClient.RequestHeadersUriSpec uriSpec;\n    private RestClient.RequestHeadersSpec headerSpec;\n    private RestClient.ResponseSpec responseSpec;\n\n    @BeforeEach\n    void setUp() {\n        client = new PaymentStatusClient(\"http://localhost:8080\");\n\n        mockRestClient = mock(RestClient.class);\n        uriSpec = mock(RestClient.RequestHeadersUriSpec.class);\n        headerSpec = mock(RestClient.RequestHeadersSpec.class);\n        responseSpec = mock(RestClient.ResponseSpec.class);\n\n        when(mockRestClient.get()).thenReturn(uriSpec);\n        doReturn(headerSpec).when(uriSpec).uri(anyString(), any(Object.class));\n        when(headerSpec.retrieve()).thenReturn(responseSpec);\n\n        ReflectionTestUtils.setField(client, \"restClient\", mockRestClient);\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_return_true_when_payment_status_is_SUCCEEDED() {\n        doReturn(new PaymentStatusClient.PaymentStatusResponse(\"SUCCEEDED\"))\n            .when(responseSpec).body(PaymentStatusClient.PaymentStatusResponse.class);\n\n        assertThat(client.isPaymentConfirmed(123L)).isTrue();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_return_false_when_payment_status_is_PENDING() {\n        doReturn(new PaymentStatusClient.PaymentStatusResponse(\"PENDING\"))\n            .when(responseSpec).body(PaymentStatusClient.PaymentStatusResponse.class);\n\n        assertThat(client.isPaymentConfirmed(456L)).isFalse();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_return_false_when_payment_status_is_FAILED() {\n        doReturn(new PaymentStatusClient.PaymentStatusResponse(\"FAILED\"))\n            .when(responseSpec).body(PaymentStatusClient.PaymentStatusResponse.class);\n\n        assertThat(client.isPaymentConfirmed(789L)).isFalse();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_return_false_when_response_body_is_null() {\n        doReturn(null)\n            .when(responseSpec).body(PaymentStatusClient.PaymentStatusResponse.class);\n\n        assertThat(client.isPaymentConfirmed(100L)).isFalse();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_return_false_when_RestClientException_thrown() {\n        doThrow(new ResourceAccessException(\"Connection refused\"))\n            .when(responseSpec).body(PaymentStatusClient.PaymentStatusResponse.class);\n\n        assertThat(client.isPaymentConfirmed(200L)).isFalse();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_return_false_when_cartId_is_null() {\n        assertThat(client.isPaymentConfirmed(null)).isFalse();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_return_true_for_SUCCEEDED_case_insensitive() {\n        doReturn(new PaymentStatusClient.PaymentStatusResponse(\"succeeded\"))\n            .when(responseSpec).body(PaymentStatusClient.PaymentStatusResponse.class);\n\n        assertThat(client.isPaymentConfirmed(300L)).isTrue();\n    }\n}\n"
  }
}
```

## Entry 560
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-04T17:37:33.818Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T17:37:33.818Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "custom_tool_call",
    "status": "completed",
    "call_id": "call_yZHN5xpNHqwFtmJsiCK5VVwM",
    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: payments-ticketing/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/paymentsticketing/domain/Booking.java\n@@\n     public void cancel() {\n         if (status != BookingStatus.CANCELLATION_PENDING) {\n             throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Booking cannot be cancelled from state \" + status, \"INVALID_BOOKING_STATE\");\n         }\n         this.status = BookingStatus.CANCELLED;\n     }\n+\n+    public void cancelDueToPaymentFailure() {\n+        if (status == BookingStatus.CANCELLED) {\n+            return;\n+        }\n+        if (status == BookingStatus.PENDING || status == BookingStatus.CANCELLATION_PENDING || status == BookingStatus.CONFIRMED) {\n+            this.status = BookingStatus.CANCELLED;\n+            return;\n+        }\n+        throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Booking cannot be cancelled from state \" + status, \"INVALID_BOOKING_STATE\");\n+    }\n*** End Patch\n"
  }
}
```

}

## Entry 586
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-04T17:39:09.884Z
**Role/Type:** turn_context
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T17:39:09.884Z",
  "type": "turn_context",
  "payload": {
    "turn_id": "019cb9ee-a1ba-7363-ae58-09a32053418f",
    "cwd": "/home/dell/Multi-City-Event-Booking",
    "current_date": "2026-03-04",
    "timezone": "Asia/Kolkata",
    "approval_policy": "on-request",
    "sandbox_policy": {
      "type": "workspace-write",
      "network_access": false,
      "exclude_tmpdir_env_var": false,
      "exclude_slash_tmp": false
    },
    "model": "gpt-5.3-codex",
    "personality": "pragmatic",
    "collaboration_mode": {
      "mode": "default",
      "settings": {
        "model": "gpt-5.3-codex",
        "reasoning_effort": "medium",
        "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\n\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\n\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\n\n## request_user_input availability\n\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\n\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\n"
      }
    },
    "effort": "medium",
    "summary": "none",
    "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md — Reference Index\n\n> Every agent working in this codebase must read the files listed below before producing\n> any output. Each file governs a specific concern. Read the ones that apply to your task.\n\n---\n\n## Reference Files\n\n| File | Governs |\n|---|---|\n| `PRODUCT.md` | The 7 modules, what each one owns, dependency order, inter-module event map, Eventbrite ACL facade names, hard architectural rules, and where new code goes. Read this before touching any module. |\n| `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` | Eventbrite integration architecture: ACL facades, all FR flows (planned vs. implemented), critical constraints (no user/order creation, no single-order cancel, no seat locking), workarounds. Read before designing any Eventbrite feature. |\n| `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md` | Canonical folder and package layout for every module. The exact location of every layer: `api/controller/`, `api/dto/`, `domain/`, `service/`, `repository/`, `mapper/`, `event/published/`, `event/listener/`, `statemachine/`, `exception/`. Anti-patterns list. Read this before writing any class. |\n| `agents/SPEC_GENERATOR.md` | How to produce a `SPEC.md` for any feature. Eight confirmation-gated stages with module context and Eventbrite constraints built-in. Requirements → Domain Model → Architecture & File Structure → DB Schema → API → Business Logic → Error Handling → Tests. Read this before speccing any feature. |\n| `agents/CODING_AGENT.md` | Spring Boot 3.5.11 / Java 21 coding standards. Domain behaviour, service orchestration, mapper rules, controller rules, event rules, 10 Eventbrite ACL facades, inter-module communication (REST + Spring Events), admin/ special rules, state machine, Spring AI. Read this before writing any production code. |\n| `agents/TEST_AGENT.md` | Test agent responsibilities and best practices. Builds tests from `SPEC_*.md` Stage 8, executes tests, fixes test/code issues caused by behavior mismatches, and writes timestamped logs under `test_result_folder/`. Read this before writing or running tests. |\n| `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md` | Four-layer test structure: `domain/` (unit), `service/` (Mockito), `api/` (@WebMvcTest), `arch/` (ArchUnit enforcing 10 hard rules + Eventbrite constraints). All rule examples, happy path, negative path, exception patterns. Read before writing any test. |\n| `mock-eventbrite-api/README.md` | **Mock Eventbrite Service:** Python FastAPI in-memory mock of Eventbrite API v3. Used for local development and testing. Exposes all 10 ACL facade endpoints without hitting production Eventbrite. See setup instructions below. |\n\n---\n\n## Module Quick Reference\n\nFull detail for each module is in `PRODUCT.md`. This table is a lookup only.\n\n| Module | Owns | Eventbrite ACL Facades Used |\n|---|---|---|\n| `discovery-catalog` | Events, Venues, Cities, catalog sync | `EbEventSyncService`, `EbTicketService` |\n| `scheduling` | Show slots, conflict validation | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `identity` | Users, JWT, profiles, preferences, wallet | `EbAttendeeService` |\n| `booking-inventory` | Seats, SeatMap, Cart, Pricing, Seat Lock State Machine | `EbTicketService` |\n| `payments-ticketing` | Bookings, Payments, E-Tickets, Cancellations, Refunds, Wallet | `EbOrderService`, `EbAttendeeService`, `EbRefundService` |\n| `promotions` | Coupons, Promotions, Eligibility rules | `EbDiscountSyncService` |\n| `engagement` | Reviews, Moderation, AI Chatbot (`service/chatbot/`), RAG pipeline | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `admin/` | No domain ownership. Orchestration and aggregation only. | — |\n| `shared/` | All external ACL facades, JWT, base classes, common DTOs, configs | — |\n\n---\n\n## Hard Rules — Never Violate\n\nCanonical numbering and wording are owned by `PRODUCT.md` Section 9. These shorthand rules apply to every task without exception.\n\n1. No module imports another module's `@Service`, `@Entity`, or `@Repository`.\n  - Exception: `admin/` MAY import module `@Service` beans for READ operations only; all writes MUST go through the owning module REST API.\n2. No module calls Eventbrite, Razorpay, or OpenAI HTTP directly — only through `shared/` ACL facades.\n3. ACL facade names are exactly as listed below — never invent new ones.\n4. Spring Events carry only primitives, IDs, enums, and value objects — never `@Entity` objects.\n5. One `GlobalExceptionHandler` in `shared/common/exception/` — never `@ControllerAdvice` in a module.\n6. All field mapping goes in `mapper/` using MapStruct — never inside a service or controller.\n7. `admin/` owns no `@Entity` and no database table.\n8. `shared/` has no dependency on any module.\n9. `app/` has zero business logic.\n10. Dependency direction is strictly downward — no circular imports between modules.\n\n---\n\n## Eventbrite ACL Facade Names (HARD RULE #3)\nRead the docs/eventbriteapiv3public.apib for detail API Endpoint with schema, Response, Request and errors\n\nAll 10 facades are in `shared/eventbrite/service/`. These are the ONLY things modules call for Eventbrite:\n\n1. **EbEventSyncService** — Create, update, publish, cancel events; pull org event catalog\n2. **EbVenueService** — Create and update venues; list org venues\n3. **EbScheduleService** — Create recurring event schedules and occurrences\n4. **EbTicketService** — Create/update ticket classes; manage inventory; check availability\n5. **EbCapacityService** — Retrieve and update event capacity tiers\n6. **EbOrderService** — Read orders post-checkout (NOT creation — JS SDK widget only)\n7. **EbAttendeeService** — List attendees; verify attendance for reviews\n8. **EbDiscountSyncService** — Create, update, delete discount codes\n9. **EbRefundService** — Read refund status only (NO submission API — workaround required)\n10. **EbWebhookService** — Mock/testing only; not used in production registration flow\n\n**See `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` for which module uses which facade and what gaps exist.**\n\n---\n\n## Module Dependency Order (HARD RULE #10)\n\n```\nshared/               <- foundational; no dependencies\n  |\n  v\ndiscovery-catalog    <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nscheduling           <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\nidentity             <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nbooking-inventory    <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\n  |\n  v\npayments-ticketing   <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\npromotions           <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nengagement           <- depends on shared + payments-ticketing + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nadmin/               <- thin orchestration; reads from all, writes via REST APIs\n  |\n  v\napp/                 <- entry point only; zero business logic\n```\n\n**Golden Rule:** Module at level N can depend on level N-1 or lower (or `shared/`) only. Never import from sibling or downstream modules.\n\n**See `PRODUCT.md` Section 2 for detailed rationale.**\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",
    "truncation_policy": {
      "mode": "tokens",
      "limit": 10000
    }
  }
}
```

Read the ones that apply to your task.\n\n---\n\n## Reference Files\n\n| File | Governs |\n|---|---|\n| `PRODUCT.md` | The 7 modules, what each one owns, dependency order, inter-module event map, Eventbrite ACL facade names, hard architectural rules, and where new code goes. Read this before touching any module. |\n| `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` | Eventbrite integration architecture: ACL facades, all FR flows (planned vs. implemented), critical constraints (no user/order creation, no single-order cancel, no seat locking), workarounds. Read before designing any Eventbrite feature. |\n| `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md` | Canonical folder and package layout for every module. The exact location of every layer: `api/controller/`, `api/dto/`, `domain/`, `service/`, `repository/`, `mapper/`, `event/published/`, `event/listener/`, `statemachine/`, `exception/`. Anti-patterns list. Read this before writing any class. |\n| `agents/SPEC_GENERATOR.md` | How to produce a `SPEC.md` for any feature. Eight confirmation-gated stages with module context and Eventbrite constraints built-in. Requirements → Domain Model → Architecture & File Structure → DB Schema → API → Business Logic → Error Handling → Tests. Read this before speccing any feature. |\n| `agents/CODING_AGENT.md` | Spring Boot 3.5.11 / Java 21 coding standards. Domain behaviour, service orchestration, mapper rules, controller rules, event rules, 10 Eventbrite ACL facades, inter-module communication (REST + Spring Events), admin/ special rules, state machine, Spring AI. Read this before writing any production code. |\n| `agents/TEST_AGENT.md` | Test agent responsibilities and best practices. Builds tests from `SPEC_*.md` Stage 8, executes tests, fixes test/code issues caused by behavior mismatches, and writes timestamped logs under `test_result_folder/`. Read this before writing or running tests. |\n| `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md` | Four-layer test structure: `domain/` (unit), `service/` (Mockito), `api/` (@WebMvcTest), `arch/` (ArchUnit enforcing 10 hard rules + Eventbrite constraints). All rule examples, happy path, negative path, exception patterns. Read before writing any test. |\n| `mock-eventbrite-api/README.md` | **Mock Eventbrite Service:** Python FastAPI in-memory mock of Eventbrite API v3. Used for local development and testing. Exposes all 10 ACL facade endpoints without hitting production Eventbrite. See setup instructions below. |\n\n---\n\n## Module Quick Reference\n\nFull detail for each module is in `PRODUCT.md`. This table is a lookup only.\n\n| Module | Owns | Eventbrite ACL Facades Used |\n|---|---|---|\n| `discovery-catalog` | Events, Venues, Cities, catalog sync | `EbEventSyncService`, `EbTicketService` |\n| `scheduling` | Show slots, conflict validation | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `identity` | Users, JWT, profiles, preferences, wallet | `EbAttendeeService` |\n| `booking-inventory` | Seats, SeatMap, Cart, Pricing, Seat Lock State Machine | `EbTicketService` |\n| `payments-ticketing` | Bookings, Payments, E-Tickets, Cancellations, Refunds, Wallet | `EbOrderService`, `EbAttendeeService`, `EbRefundService` |\n| `promotions` | Coupons, Promotions, Eligibility rules | `EbDiscountSyncService` |\n| `engagement` | Reviews, Moderation, AI Chatbot (`service/chatbot/`), RAG pipeline | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `admin/` | No domain ownership. Orchestration and aggregation only. | — |\n| `shared/` | All external ACL facades, JWT, base classes, common DTOs, configs | — |\n\n---\n\n## Hard Rules — Never Violate\n\nCanonical numbering and wording are owned by `PRODUCT.md` Section 9. These shorthand rules apply to every task without exception.\n\n1. No module imports another module's `@Service`, `@Entity`, or `@Repository`.\n  - Exception: `admin/` MAY import module `@Service` beans for READ operations only; all writes MUST go through the owning module REST API.\n2. No module calls Eventbrite, Razorpay, or OpenAI HTTP directly — only through `shared/` ACL facades.\n3. ACL facade names are exactly as listed below — never invent new ones.\n4. Spring Events carry only primitives, IDs, enums, and value objects — never `@Entity` objects.\n5. One `GlobalExceptionHandler` in `shared/common/exception/` — never `@ControllerAdvice` in a module.\n6. All field mapping goes in `mapper/` using MapStruct — never inside a service or controller.\n7. `admin/` owns no `@Entity` and no database table.\n8. `shared/` has no dependency on any module.\n9. `app/` has zero business logic.\n10. Dependency direction is strictly downward — no circular imports between modules.\n\n---\n\n## Eventbrite ACL Facade Names (HARD RULE #3)\nRead the docs/eventbriteapiv3public.apib for detail API Endpoint with schema, Response, Request and errors\n\nAll 10 facades are in `shared/eventbrite/service/`. These are the ONLY things modules call for Eventbrite:\n\n1. **EbEventSyncService** — Create, update, publish, cancel events; pull org event catalog\n2. **EbVenueService** — Create and update venues; list org venues\n3. **EbScheduleService** — Create recurring event schedules and occurrences\n4. **EbTicketService** — Create/update ticket classes; manage inventory; check availability\n5. **EbCapacityService** — Retrieve and update event capacity tiers\n6. **EbOrderService** — Read orders post-checkout (NOT creation — JS SDK widget only)\n7. **EbAttendeeService** — List attendees; verify attendance for reviews\n8. **EbDiscountSyncService** — Create, update, delete discount codes\n9. **EbRefundService** — Read refund status only (NO submission API — workaround required)\n10. **EbWebhookService** — Mock/testing only; not used in production registration flow\n\n**See `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` for which module uses which facade and what gaps exist.**\n\n---\n\n## Module Dependency Order (HARD RULE #10)\n\n```\nshared/               <- foundational; no dependencies\n  |\n  v\ndiscovery-catalog    <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nscheduling           <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\nidentity             <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nbooking-inventory    <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\n  |\n  v\npayments-ticketing   <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\npromotions           <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nengagement           <- depends on shared + payments-ticketing + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nadmin/               <- thin orchestration; reads from all, writes via REST APIs\n  |\n  v\napp/                 <- entry point only; zero business logic\n```\n\n**Golden Rule:** Module at level N can depend on level N-1 or lower (or `shared/`) only. Never import from sibling or downstream modules.\n\n**See `PRODUCT.md` Section 2 for detailed rationale.**\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",
    "truncation_policy": {
      "mode": "tokens",
      "limit": 10000
    }
  }
}
```

## Entry 598
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-04T17:39:24.815Z
**Role/Type:** event_msg
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T17:39:24.815Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "agent_message",
    "message": "Yes. I finished the code implementation for `POQ/Payments/SPEC_5.md` and stopped test execution.\n\nImplemented scope includes:\n\n1. Full FR5 code in `payments-ticketing`:\n- New domain/entities: booking items, e-tickets, refunds, cancellation requests\n- New enums, repositories, exceptions, DTOs, mappers\n- Services for checkout creation, payment confirm/failure, booking history, e-ticket fetch, cancellation/refund flow, webhook refund updates\n- Controllers for:\n  - `/api/v1/payments/checkout/{cartId}`\n  - `/api/v1/payments/confirm`\n  - `/api/v1/payments/failed`\n  - `/api/v1/bookings`\n  - `/api/v1/bookings/{bookingRef}`\n  - `/api/v1/bookings/{bookingRef}/cancel`\n  - `/api/v1/tickets/{bookingRef}`\n  - `/api/v1/webhooks/stripe`\n  - `/api/v1/internal/payments/by-cart/{cartId}`\n- Event listener for `CartAssembledEvent`\n\n2. Shared/infra updates required by the spec:\n- Stripe PaymentIntent request/response extended for metadata + latest charge\n- Security allowlist updated for `/api/v1/webhooks/stripe` and `/api/v1/internal/**`\n- Flyway migration added: [V32__fix_e_tickets_status_default_and_unique_item.sql](/home/dell/Multi-City-Event-Booking/app/src/main/resources/db/migration/V32__fix_e_tickets_status_default_and_unique_item.sql)\n- App config extended with cancellation fee/window config in [application.yaml](/home/dell/Multi-City-Event-Booking/app/src/main/resources/application.yaml)\n\n3. Cross-module internal APIs for FR5 orchestration:\n- Internal cart items endpoint in booking-inventory\n- Internal slot timing endpoint in scheduling\n- Booking-inventory payment-status client updated to new internal payments API contract\n\nIf you want, I can now do a final cleanup pass to remove the two test-file edits I made (`PaymentStatusClientTest`, `StripePaymentServiceTest`) so only production-code/spec-required files remain.",
    "phase": "final_answer"
  }
}
```

Shared/infra updates required by the spec:\n- Stripe PaymentIntent request/response extended for metadata + latest charge\n- Security allowlist updated for `/api/v1/webhooks/stripe` and `/api/v1/internal/**`\n- Flyway migration added: [V32__fix_e_tickets_status_default_and_unique_item.sql](/home/dell/Multi-City-Event-Booking/app/src/main/resources/db/migration/V32__fix_e_tickets_status_default_and_unique_item.sql)\n- App config extended with cancellation fee/window config in [application.yaml](/home/dell/Multi-City-Event-Booking/app/src/main/resources/application.yaml)\n\n3.
