    "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md — Reference Index\n\n> Every agent working in this codebase must read the files listed below before producing\n> any output. Each file governs a specific concern. Read the ones that apply to your task.\n\n---\n\n## Reference Files\n\n| File | Governs |\n|---|---|\n| `PRODUCT.md` | The 7 modules, what each one owns, dependency order, inter-module event map, Eventbrite ACL facade names, hard architectural rules, and where new code goes. Read this before touching any module. |\n| `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` | Eventbrite integration architecture: ACL facades, all FR flows (planned vs. implemented), critical constraints (no user/order creation, no single-order cancel, no seat locking), workarounds. Read before designing any Eventbrite feature. |\n| `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md` | Canonical folder and package layout for every module. The exact location of every layer: `api/controller/`, `api/dto/`, `domain/`, `service/`, `repository/`, `mapper/`, `event/published/`, `event/listener/`, `statemachine/`, `exception/`. Anti-patterns list. Read this before writing any class. |\n| `agents/SPEC_GENERATOR.md` | How to produce a `SPEC.md` for any feature. Eight confirmation-gated stages with module context and Eventbrite constraints built-in. Requirements → Domain Model → Architecture & File Structure → DB Schema → API → Business Logic → Error Handling → Tests. Read this before speccing any feature. |\n| `agents/CODING_AGENT.md` | Spring Boot 3.5.11 / Java 21 coding standards. Domain behaviour, service orchestration, mapper rules, controller rules, event rules, 10 Eventbrite ACL facades, inter-module communication (REST + Spring Events), admin/ special rules, state machine, Spring AI. Read this before writing any production code. |\n| `agents/TEST_AGENT.md` | Test agent responsibilities and best practices. Builds tests from `SPEC_*.md` Stage 8, executes tests, fixes test/code issues caused by behavior mismatches, and writes timestamped logs under `test_result_folder/`. Read this before writing or running tests. |\n| `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md` | Four-layer test structure: `domain/` (unit), `service/` (Mockito), `api/` (@WebMvcTest), `arch/` (ArchUnit enforcing 10 hard rules + Eventbrite constraints). All rule examples, happy path, negative path, exception patterns. Read before writing any test. |\n| `mock-eventbrite-api/README.md` | **Mock Eventbrite Service:** Python FastAPI in-memory mock of Eventbrite API v3. Used for local development and testing. Exposes all 10 ACL facade endpoints without hitting production Eventbrite. See setup instructions below. |\n\n---\n\n## Module Quick Reference\n\nFull detail for each module is in `PRODUCT.md`. This table is a lookup only.\n\n| Module | Owns | Eventbrite ACL Facades Used |\n|---|---|---|\n| `discovery-catalog` | Events, Venues, Cities, catalog sync | `EbEventSyncService`, `EbTicketService` |\n| `scheduling` | Show slots, conflict validation | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `identity` | Users, JWT, profiles, preferences, wallet | `EbAttendeeService` |\n| `booking-inventory` | Seats, SeatMap, Cart, Pricing, Seat Lock State Machine | `EbTicketService` |\n| `payments-ticketing` | Bookings, Payments, E-Tickets, Cancellations, Refunds, Wallet | `EbOrderService`, `EbAttendeeService`, `EbRefundService` |\n| `promotions` | Coupons, Promotions, Eligibility rules | `EbDiscountSyncService` |\n| `engagement` | Reviews, Moderation, AI Chatbot (`service/chatbot/`), RAG pipeline | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `admin/` | No domain ownership. Orchestration and aggregation only. | — |\n| `shared/` | All external ACL facades, JWT, base classes, common DTOs, configs | — |\n\n---\n\n## Hard Rules — Never Violate\n\nCanonical numbering and wording are owned by `PRODUCT.md` Section 9. These shorthand rules apply to every task without exception.\n\n1. No module imports another module's `@Service`, `@Entity`, or `@Repository`.\n  - Exception: `admin/` MAY import module `@Service` beans for READ operations only; all writes MUST go through the owning module REST API.\n2. No module calls Eventbrite, Razorpay, or OpenAI HTTP directly — only through `shared/` ACL facades.\n3. ACL facade names are exactly as listed below — never invent new ones.\n4. Spring Events carry only primitives, IDs, enums, and value objects — never `@Entity` objects.\n5. One `GlobalExceptionHandler` in `shared/common/exception/` — never `@ControllerAdvice` in a module.\n6. All field mapping goes in `mapper/` using MapStruct — never inside a service or controller.\n7. `admin/` owns no `@Entity` and no database table.\n8. `shared/` has no dependency on any module.\n9. `app/` has zero business logic.\n10. Dependency direction is strictly downward — no circular imports between modules.\n\n---\n\n## Eventbrite ACL Facade Names (HARD RULE #3)\nRead the docs/eventbriteapiv3public.apib for detail API Endpoint with schema, Response, Request and errors\n\nAll 10 facades are in `shared/eventbrite/service/`. These are the ONLY things modules call for Eventbrite:\n\n1. **EbEventSyncService** — Create, update, publish, cancel events; pull org event catalog\n2. **EbVenueService** — Create and update venues; list org venues\n3. **EbScheduleService** — Create recurring event schedules and occurrences\n4. **EbTicketService** — Create/update ticket classes; manage inventory; check availability\n5. **EbCapacityService** — Retrieve and update event capacity tiers\n6. **EbOrderService** — Read orders post-checkout (NOT creation — JS SDK widget only)\n7. **EbAttendeeService** — List attendees; verify attendance for reviews\n8. **EbDiscountSyncService** — Create, update, delete discount codes\n9. **EbRefundService** — Read refund status only (NO submission API — workaround required)\n10. **EbWebhookService** — Mock/testing only; not used in production registration flow\n\n**See `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` for which module uses which facade and what gaps exist.**\n\n---\n\n## Module Dependency Order (HARD RULE #10)\n\n```\nshared/               <- foundational; no dependencies\n  |\n  v\ndiscovery-catalog    <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nscheduling           <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\nidentity             <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nbooking-inventory    <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\n  |\n  v\npayments-ticketing   <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\npromotions           <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nengagement           <- depends on shared + payments-ticketing + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nadmin/               <- thin orchestration; reads from all, writes via REST APIs\n  |\n  v\napp/                 <- entry point only; zero business logic\n```\n\n**Golden Rule:** Module at level N can depend on level N-1 or lower (or `shared/`) only. Never import from sibling or downstream modules.\n\n**See `PRODUCT.md` Section 2 for detailed rationale.**\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",

## Entry 636
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-04T17:46:35.417Z
**Role/Type:** event_msg
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T17:46:35.417Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "agent_message",
    "message": "1. `payments-ticketing` confirm flow -> cart snapshot fetch  \n- Current call: [PaymentService.java](/home/dell/Multi-City-Event-Booking/payments-ticketing/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/paymentsticketing/service/PaymentService.java) -> `GET /internal/booking/carts/{cartId}/items`  \n- Why hot: this sits directly in the success path of `POST /api/v1/payments/confirm`; every successful payment waits on it before booking items + tickets are created.  \n- Bottleneck risk: internal HTTP overhead on a latency-critical, user-facing transaction.\n\n2. `booking-inventory` watchdog -> payment confirmation check  \n- Current call: [PaymentStatusClient.java](/home/dell/Multi-City-Event-Booking/booking-inventory/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/service/client/PaymentStatusClient.java) called by [PaymentTimeoutWatchdog.java](/home/dell/Multi-City-Event-Booking/booking-inventory/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/bookinginventory/scheduler/PaymentTimeoutWatchdog.java).  \n- Why hot: runs repeatedly on schedule and can fan out to many cart/seat checks.  \n- Bottleneck risk: repeated cross-module HTTP calls can create avoidable load and timing jitter.\n\nLower-priority path (not hot):  \n- cancellation window slot timing lookup in [CancellationService.java](/home/dell/Multi-City-Event-Booking/payments-ticketing/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/paymentsticketing/service/CancellationService.java).  \n- It is user-triggered and much less frequent than confirm/watchdog traffic.",
    "phase": "final_answer"
  }
}
```

## Entry 641
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-04T17:48:58.236Z
**Role/Type:** turn_context
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T17:48:58.236Z",
  "type": "turn_context",
  "payload": {
    "turn_id": "019cb9f7-9bfa-73c1-bddd-abc166905717",
    "cwd": "/home/dell/Multi-City-Event-Booking",
    "current_date": "2026-03-04",
    "timezone": "Asia/Kolkata",
    "approval_policy": "on-request",
    "sandbox_policy": {
      "type": "workspace-write",
      "network_access": false,
      "exclude_tmpdir_env_var": false,
      "exclude_slash_tmp": false
    },
    "model": "gpt-5.3-codex",
    "personality": "pragmatic",
    "collaboration_mode": {
      "mode": "plan",
      "settings": {
        "model": "gpt-5.3-codex",
        "reasoning_effort": "medium",
        "developer_instructions": "# Plan Mode (Conversational)\n\nYou work in 3 phases, and you should *chat your way* to a great plan before finalizing it. A great plan is very detailed—intent- and implementation-wise—so that it can be handed to another engineer or agent to be implemented right away. It must be **decision complete**, where the implementer does not need to make any decisions.\n\n## Mode rules (strict)\n\nYou are in **Plan Mode** until a developer message explicitly ends it.\n\nPlan Mode is not changed by user intent, tone, or imperative language. If a user asks for execution while still in Plan Mode, treat it as a request to **plan the execution**, not perform it.\n\n## Plan Mode vs update_plan tool\n\nPlan Mode is a collaboration mode that can involve requesting user input and eventually issuing a `<proposed_plan>` block.\n\nSeparately, `update_plan` is a checklist/progress/TODOs tool; it does not enter or exit Plan Mode. Do not confuse it with Plan mode or try to use it while in Plan mode. If you try to use `update_plan` in Plan mode, it will return an error.\n\n## Execution vs. mutation in Plan Mode\n\nYou may explore and execute **non-mutating** actions that improve the plan. You must not perform **mutating** actions.\n\n### Allowed (non-mutating, plan-improving)\n\nActions that gather truth, reduce ambiguity, or validate feasibility without changing repo-tracked state. Examples:\n\n* Reading or searching files, configs, schemas, types, manifests, and docs\n* Static analysis, inspection, and repo exploration\n* Dry-run style commands when they do not edit repo-tracked files\n* Tests, builds, or checks that may write to caches or build artifacts (for example, `target/`, `.cache/`, or snapshots) so long as they do not edit repo-tracked files\n\n### Not allowed (mutating, plan-executing)\n\nActions that implement the plan or change repo-tracked state. Examples:\n\n* Editing or writing files\n* Running formatters or linters that rewrite files\n* Applying patches, migrations, or codegen that updates repo-tracked files\n* Side-effectful commands whose purpose is to carry out the plan rather than refine it\n\nWhen in doubt: if the action would reasonably be described as \"doing the work\" rather than \"planning the work,\" do not do it.\n\n## PHASE 1 — Ground in the environment (explore first, ask second)\n\nBegin by grounding yourself in the actual environment. Eliminate unknowns in the prompt by discovering facts, not by asking the user. Resolve all questions that can be answered through exploration or inspection. Identify missing or ambiguous details only if they cannot be derived from the environment. Silent exploration between turns is allowed and encouraged.\n\nBefore asking the user any question, perform at least one targeted non-mutating exploration pass (for example: search relevant files, inspect likely entrypoints/configs, confirm current implementation shape), unless no local environment/repo is available.\n\nException: you may ask clarifying questions about the user's prompt before exploring, ONLY if there are obvious ambiguities or contradictions in the prompt itself. However, if ambiguity might be resolved by exploring, always prefer exploring first.\n\nDo not ask questions that can be answered from the repo or system (for example, \"where is this struct?\" or \"which UI component should we use?\" when exploration can make it clear). Only ask once you have exhausted reasonable non-mutating exploration.\n\n## PHASE 2 — Intent chat (what they actually want)\n\n* Keep asking until you can clearly state: goal + success criteria, audience, in/out of scope, constraints, current state, and the key preferences/tradeoffs.\n* Bias toward questions over guessing: if any high-impact ambiguity remains, do NOT plan yet—ask.\n\n## PHASE 3 — Implementation chat (what/how we’ll build)\n\n* Once intent is stable, keep asking until the spec is decision complete: approach, interfaces (APIs/schemas/I/O), data flow, edge cases/failure modes, testing + acceptance criteria, rollout/monitoring, and any migrations/compat constraints.\n\n## Asking questions\n\nCritical rules:\n\n* Strongly prefer using the `request_user_input` tool to ask any questions.\n* Offer only meaningful multiple‑choice options; don’t include filler choices that are obviously wrong or irrelevant.\n* In rare cases where an unavoidable, important question can’t be expressed with reasonable multiple‑choice options (due to extreme ambiguity), you may ask it directly without the tool.\n\nYou SHOULD ask many questions, but each question must:\n\n* materially change the spec/plan, OR\n* confirm/lock an assumption, OR\n* choose between meaningful tradeoffs.\n* not be answerable by non-mutating commands.\n\nUse the `request_user_input` tool only for decisions that materially change the plan, for confirming important assumptions, or for information that cannot be discovered via non-mutating exploration.\n\n## Two kinds of unknowns (treat differently)\n\n1. **Discoverable facts** (repo/system truth): explore first.\n\n   * Before asking, run targeted searches and check likely sources of truth (configs/manifests/entrypoints/schemas/types/constants).\n   * Ask only if: multiple plausible candidates; nothing found but you need a missing identifier/context; or ambiguity is actually product intent.\n   * If asking, present concrete candidates (paths/service names) + recommend one.\n   * Never ask questions you can answer from your environment (e.g., “where is this struct”).\n\n2. **Preferences/tradeoffs** (not discoverable): ask early.\n\n   * These are intent or implementation preferences that cannot be derived from exploration.\n   * Provide 2–4 mutually exclusive options + a recommended default.\n   * If unanswered, proceed with the recommended option and record it as an assumption in the final plan.\n\n## Finalization rule\n\nOnly output the final plan when it is decision complete and leaves no decisions to the implementer.\n\nWhen you present the official plan, wrap it in a `<proposed_plan>` block so the client can render it specially:\n\n1) The opening tag must be on its own line.\n2) Start the plan content on the next line (no text on the same line as the tag).\n3) The closing tag must be on its own line.\n4) Use Markdown inside the block.\n5) Keep the tags exactly as `<proposed_plan>` and `</proposed_plan>` (do not translate or rename them), even if the plan content is in another language.\n\nExample:\n\n<proposed_plan>\nplan content\n</proposed_plan>\n\nplan content should be human and agent digestible. The final plan must be plan-only and include:\n\n* A clear title\n* A brief summary section\n* Important changes or additions to public APIs/interfaces/types\n* Test cases and scenarios\n* Explicit assumptions and defaults chosen where needed\n\nDo not ask \"should I proceed?\" in the final output. The user can easily switch out of Plan mode and request implementation if you have included a `<proposed_plan>` block in your response. Alternatively, they can decide to stay in Plan mode and continue refining the plan.\n\nOnly produce at most one `<proposed_plan>` block per turn, and only when you are presenting a complete spec.\n\nIf the user stays in Plan mode and asks for revisions after a prior `<proposed_plan>`, any new `<proposed_plan>` must be a complete replacement.\n"
      }
    },
    "effort": "medium",
    "summary": "none",
    "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md — Reference Index\n\n> Every agent working in this codebase must read the files listed below before producing\n> any output. Each file governs a specific concern. Read the ones that apply to your task.\n\n---\n\n## Reference Files\n\n| File | Governs |\n|---|---|\n| `PRODUCT.md` | The 7 modules, what each one owns, dependency order, inter-module event map, Eventbrite ACL facade names, hard architectural rules, and where new code goes. Read this before touching any module. |\n| `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` | Eventbrite integration architecture: ACL facades, all FR flows (planned vs. implemented), critical constraints (no user/order creation, no single-order cancel, no seat locking), workarounds. Read before designing any Eventbrite feature. |\n| `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md` | Canonical folder and package layout for every module. The exact location of every layer: `api/controller/`, `api/dto/`, `domain/`, `service/`, `repository/`, `mapper/`, `event/published/`, `event/listener/`, `statemachine/`, `exception/`. Anti-patterns list. Read this before writing any class. |\n| `agents/SPEC_GENERATOR.md` | How to produce a `SPEC.md` for any feature. Eight confirmation-gated stages with module context and Eventbrite constraints built-in. Requirements → Domain Model → Architecture & File Structure → DB Schema → API → Business Logic → Error Handling → Tests. Read this before speccing any feature. |\n| `agents/CODING_AGENT.md` | Spring Boot 3.5.11 / Java 21 coding standards. Domain behaviour, service orchestration, mapper rules, controller rules, event rules, 10 Eventbrite ACL facades, inter-module communication (REST + Spring Events), admin/ special rules, state machine, Spring AI. Read this before writing any production code. |\n| `agents/TEST_AGENT.md` | Test agent responsibilities and best practices. Builds tests from `SPEC_*.md` Stage 8, executes tests, fixes test/code issues caused by behavior mismatches, and writes timestamped logs under `test_result_folder/`. Read this before writing or running tests. |\n| `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md` | Four-layer test structure: `domain/` (unit), `service/` (Mockito), `api/` (@WebMvcTest), `arch/` (ArchUnit enforcing 10 hard rules + Eventbrite constraints). All rule examples, happy path, negative path, exception patterns. Read before writing any test. |\n| `mock-eventbrite-api/README.md` | **Mock Eventbrite Service:** Python FastAPI in-memory mock of Eventbrite API v3. Used for local development and testing. Exposes all 10 ACL facade endpoints without hitting production Eventbrite. See setup instructions below. |\n\n---\n\n## Module Quick Reference\n\nFull detail for each module is in `PRODUCT.md`. This table is a lookup only.\n\n| Module | Owns | Eventbrite ACL Facades Used |\n|---|---|---|\n| `discovery-catalog` | Events, Venues, Cities, catalog sync | `EbEventSyncService`, `EbTicketService` |\n| `scheduling` | Show slots, conflict validation | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `identity` | Users, JWT, profiles, preferences, wallet | `EbAttendeeService` |\n| `booking-inventory` | Seats, SeatMap, Cart, Pricing, Seat Lock State Machine | `EbTicketService` |\n| `payments-ticketing` | Bookings, Payments, E-Tickets, Cancellations, Refunds, Wallet | `EbOrderService`, `EbAttendeeService`, `EbRefundService` |\n| `promotions` | Coupons, Promotions, Eligibility rules | `EbDiscountSyncService` |\n| `engagement` | Reviews, Moderation, AI Chatbot (`service/chatbot/`), RAG pipeline | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `admin/` | No domain ownership. Orchestration and aggregation only. | — |\n| `shared/` | All external ACL facades, JWT, base classes, common DTOs, configs | — |\n\n---\n\n## Hard Rules — Never Violate\n\nCanonical numbering and wording are owned by `PRODUCT.md` Section 9. These shorthand rules apply to every task without exception.\n\n1. No module imports another module's `@Service`, `@Entity`, or `@Repository`.\n  - Exception: `admin/` MAY import module `@Service` beans for READ operations only; all writes MUST go through the owning module REST API.\n2. No module calls Eventbrite, Razorpay, or OpenAI HTTP directly — only through `shared/` ACL facades.\n3. ACL facade names are exactly as listed below — never invent new ones.\n4. Spring Events carry only primitives, IDs, enums, and value objects — never `@Entity` objects.\n5. One `GlobalExceptionHandler` in `shared/common/exception/` — never `@ControllerAdvice` in a module.\n6. All field mapping goes in `mapper/` using MapStruct — never inside a service or controller.\n7. `admin/` owns no `@Entity` and no database table.\n8. `shared/` has no dependency on any module.\n9. `app/` has zero business logic.\n10. Dependency direction is strictly downward — no circular imports between modules.\n\n---\n\n## Eventbrite ACL Facade Names (HARD RULE #3)\nRead the docs/eventbriteapiv3public.apib for detail API Endpoint with schema, Response, Request and errors\n\nAll 10 facades are in `shared/eventbrite/service/`. These are the ONLY things modules call for Eventbrite:\n\n1. **EbEventSyncService** — Create, update, publish, cancel events; pull org event catalog\n2. **EbVenueService** — Create and update venues; list org venues\n3. **EbScheduleService** — Create recurring event schedules and occurrences\n4. **EbTicketService** — Create/update ticket classes; manage inventory; check availability\n5. **EbCapacityService** — Retrieve and update event capacity tiers\n6. **EbOrderService** — Read orders post-checkout (NOT creation — JS SDK widget only)\n7. **EbAttendeeService** — List attendees; verify attendance for reviews\n8. **EbDiscountSyncService** — Create, update, delete discount codes\n9. **EbRefundService** — Read refund status only (NO submission API — workaround required)\n10. **EbWebhookService** — Mock/testing only; not used in production registration flow\n\n**See `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` for which module uses which facade and what gaps exist.**\n\n---\n\n## Module Dependency Order (HARD RULE #10)\n\n```\nshared/               <- foundational; no dependencies\n  |\n  v\ndiscovery-catalog    <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nscheduling           <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\nidentity             <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nbooking-inventory    <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\n  |\n  v\npayments-ticketing   <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\npromotions           <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nengagement           <- depends on shared + payments-ticketing + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nadmin/               <- thin orchestration; reads from all, writes via REST APIs\n  |\n  v\napp/                 <- entry point only; zero business logic\n```\n\n**Golden Rule:** Module at level N can depend on level N-1 or lower (or `shared/`) only. Never import from sibling or downstream modules.\n\n**See `PRODUCT.md` Section 2 for detailed rationale.**\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",

        "developer_instructions": "# Plan Mode (Conversational)\n\nYou work in 3 phases, and you should *chat your way* to a great plan before finalizing it. A great plan is very detailed—intent- and implementation-wise—so that it can be handed to another engineer or agent to be implemented right away. It must be **decision complete**, where the implementer does not need to make any decisions.\n\n## Mode rules (strict)\n\nYou are in **Plan Mode** until a developer message explicitly ends it.\n\nPlan Mode is not changed by user intent, tone, or imperative language. If a user asks for execution while still in Plan Mode, treat it as a request to **plan the execution**, not perform it.\n\n## Plan Mode vs update_plan tool\n\nPlan Mode is a collaboration mode that can involve requesting user input and eventually issuing a `<proposed_plan>` block.\n\nSeparately, `update_plan` is a checklist/progress/TODOs tool; it does not enter or exit Plan Mode. Do not confuse it with Plan mode or try to use it while in Plan mode. If you try to use `update_plan` in Plan mode, it will return an error.\n\n## Execution vs. mutation in Plan Mode\n\nYou may explore and execute **non-mutating** actions that improve the plan. You must not perform **mutating** actions.\n\n### Allowed (non-mutating, plan-improving)\n\nActions that gather truth, reduce ambiguity, or validate feasibility without changing repo-tracked state. Examples:\n\n* Reading or searching files, configs, schemas, types, manifests, and docs\n* Static analysis, inspection, and repo exploration\n* Dry-run style commands when they do not edit repo-tracked files\n* Tests, builds, or checks that may write to caches or build artifacts (for example, `target/`, `.cache/`, or snapshots) so long as they do not edit repo-tracked files\n\n### Not allowed (mutating, plan-executing)\n\nActions that implement the plan or change repo-tracked state. Examples:\n\n* Editing or writing files\n* Running formatters or linters that rewrite files\n* Applying patches, migrations, or codegen that updates repo-tracked files\n* Side-effectful commands whose purpose is to carry out the plan rather than refine it\n\nWhen in doubt: if the action would reasonably be described as \"doing the work\" rather than \"planning the work,\" do not do it.\n\n## PHASE 1 — Ground in the environment (explore first, ask second)\n\nBegin by grounding yourself in the actual environment. Eliminate unknowns in the prompt by discovering facts, not by asking the user. Resolve all questions that can be answered through exploration or inspection. Identify missing or ambiguous details only if they cannot be derived from the environment. Silent exploration between turns is allowed and encouraged.\n\nBefore asking the user any question, perform at least one targeted non-mutating exploration pass (for example: search relevant files, inspect likely entrypoints/configs, confirm current implementation shape), unless no local environment/repo is available.\n\nException: you may ask clarifying questions about the user's prompt before exploring, ONLY if there are obvious ambiguities or contradictions in the prompt itself. However, if ambiguity might be resolved by exploring, always prefer exploring first.\n\nDo not ask questions that can be answered from the repo or system (for example, \"where is this struct?\" or \"which UI component should we use?\" when exploration can make it clear). Only ask once you have exhausted reasonable non-mutating exploration.\n\n## PHASE 2 — Intent chat (what they actually want)\n\n* Keep asking until you can clearly state: goal + success criteria, audience, in/out of scope, constraints, current state, and the key preferences/tradeoffs.\n* Bias toward questions over guessing: if any high-impact ambiguity remains, do NOT plan yet—ask.\n\n## PHASE 3 — Implementation chat (what/how we’ll build)\n\n* Once intent is stable, keep asking until the spec is decision complete: approach, interfaces (APIs/schemas/I/O), data flow, edge cases/failure modes, testing + acceptance criteria, rollout/monitoring, and any migrations/compat constraints.\n\n## Asking questions\n\nCritical rules:\n\n* Strongly prefer using the `request_user_input` tool to ask any questions.\n* Offer only meaningful multiple‑choice options; don’t include filler choices that are obviously wrong or irrelevant.\n* In rare cases where an unavoidable, important question can’t be expressed with reasonable multiple‑choice options (due to extreme ambiguity), you may ask it directly without the tool.\n\nYou SHOULD ask many questions, but each question must:\n\n* materially change the spec/plan, OR\n* confirm/lock an assumption, OR\n* choose between meaningful tradeoffs.\n* not be answerable by non-mutating commands.\n\nUse the `request_user_input` tool only for decisions that materially change the plan, for confirming important assumptions, or for information that cannot be discovered via non-mutating exploration.\n\n## Two kinds of unknowns (treat differently)\n\n1. **Discoverable facts** (repo/system truth): explore first.\n\n   * Before asking, run targeted searches and check likely sources of truth (configs/manifests/entrypoints/schemas/types/constants).\n   * Ask only if: multiple plausible candidates; nothing found but you need a missing identifier/context; or ambiguity is actually product intent.\n   * If asking, present concrete candidates (paths/service names) + recommend one.\n   * Never ask questions you can answer from your environment (e.g., “where is this struct”).\n\n2. **Preferences/tradeoffs** (not discoverable): ask early.\n\n   * These are intent or implementation preferences that cannot be derived from exploration.\n   * Provide 2–4 mutually exclusive options + a recommended default.\n   * If unanswered, proceed with the recommended option and record it as an assumption in the final plan.\n\n## Finalization rule\n\nOnly output the final plan when it is decision complete and leaves no decisions to the implementer.\n\nWhen you present the official plan, wrap it in a `<proposed_plan>` block so the client can render it specially:\n\n1) The opening tag must be on its own line.\n2) Start the plan content on the next line (no text on the same line as the tag).\n3) The closing tag must be on its own line.\n4) Use Markdown inside the block.\n5) Keep the tags exactly as `<proposed_plan>` and `</proposed_plan>` (do not translate or rename them), even if the plan content is in another language.\n\nExample:\n\n<proposed_plan>\nplan content\n</proposed_plan>\n\nplan content should be human and agent digestible. The final plan must be plan-only and include:\n\n* A clear title\n* A brief summary section\n* Important changes or additions to public APIs/interfaces/types\n* Test cases and scenarios\n* Explicit assumptions and defaults chosen where needed\n\nDo not ask \"should I proceed?\" in the final output. The user can easily switch out of Plan mode and request implementation if you have included a `<proposed_plan>` block in your response. Alternatively, they can decide to stay in Plan mode and continue refining the plan.\n\nOnly produce at most one `<proposed_plan>` block per turn, and only when you are presenting a complete spec.\n\nIf the user stays in Plan mode and asks for revisions after a prior `<proposed_plan>`, any new `<proposed_plan>` must be a complete replacement.\n"

com.eventplatform.shared.common.event.published.CartAssembledEvent;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.common.event.published.PaymentFailedEvent;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.common.service.SlotSummaryReader;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.stripe.dto.StripePaymentIntentRequest;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.stripe.dto.StripePaymentIntentResponse;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.stripe.service.StripePaymentService;\nimport java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets;\nimport java.util.ArrayList;\nimport java.util.Base64;\nimport java.util.HashMap;\nimport java.util.List;\nimport java.util.Map;\nimport java.util.Objects;\nimport org.springframework.beans.factory.annotation.Value;\nimport org.springframework.core.ParameterizedTypeReference;\nimport org.springframework.http.HttpStatusCode;\nimport org.springframework.stereotype.Service;\nimport org.springframework.transaction.annotation.Transactional;\nimport org.springframework.web.client.RestClient;\n\n@Service\npublic class PaymentService {\n\n    private final BookingRepository bookingRepository;\n    private final PaymentRepository paymentRepository;\n    private final BookingItemRepository bookingItemRepository;\n    private final ETicketRepository eTicketRepository;\n    private final StripePaymentService stripePaymentService;\n    private final BookingMapper bookingMapper;\n    private final ETicketMapper eTicketMapper;\n    private final AfterCommitEventPublisher afterCommitEventPublisher;\n    private final BookingRefGenerator bookingRefGenerator;\n    private final SlotSummaryReader slotSummaryReader;\n    private final RestClient restClient;\n\n    public PaymentService(\n        BookingRepository bookingRepository,\n        PaymentRepository paymentRepository,\n        BookingItemRepository bookingItemRepository,\n        ETicketRepository eTicketRepository,\n        StripePaymentService stripePaymentService,\n        BookingMapper bookingMapper,\n        ETicketMapper eTicketMapper,\n        AfterCommitEventPublisher afterCommitEventPublisher,\n        BookingRefGenerator bookingRefGenerator,\n        SlotSummaryReader slotSummaryReader,\n        @Value(\"${app.internal-base-url:http://localhost:8080}\") String internalBaseUrl\n    ) {\n        this.bookingRepository = bookingRepository;\n        this.paymentRepository = paymentRepository;\n        this.bookingItemRepository = bookingItemRepository;\n        this.eTicketRepository = eTicketRepository;\n        this.stripePaymentService = stripePaymentService;\n        this.bookingMapper = bookingMapper;\n        this.eTicketMapper = eTicketMapper;\n        this.afterCommitEventPublisher = afterCommitEventPublisher;\n        this.bookingRefGenerator = bookingRefGenerator;\n        this.slotSummaryReader = slotSummaryReader;\n        this.restClient = RestClient.builder().baseUrl(internalBaseUrl).build();\n    }\n\n    @Transactional\n    public CheckoutInitResponse createCheckout(CartAssembledEvent event) {\n        Booking existingBooking = bookingRepository.findByCartId(event.cartId()).orElse(null);\n        if (existingBooking != null) {\n            Payment existingPayment = paymentRepository.findByBookingId(existingBooking.getId()).orElse(null);\n            if (existingPayment != null && (existingPayment.getStatus() == PaymentStatus.PENDING || existingPayment.getStatus() == PaymentStatus.SUCCESS)) {\n                StripePaymentIntentResponse paymentIntent = stripePaymentService.getPaymentIntent(existingPayment.getStripePaymentIntentId());\n                return new CheckoutInitResponse(\n                    event.cartId(),\n                    existingBooking.getBookingRef(),\n                    existingPayment.getStripePaymentIntentId(),\n                    paymentIntent.clientSecret(),\n                    existingPayment.getAmount(),\n                    existingPayment.getCurrency()\n                );\n            }\n        }\n\n        String bookingRef = bookingRefGenerator.nextRef();\n        Long eventId = event.slotId();\n        if (slotSummaryReader.getSlotSummary(event.slotId()) == null) {\n            throw new CartItemsFetchException(\"Unable to resolve slot details for cart=\" + event.cartId());\n        }\n\n        Booking booking = bookingRepository.save(new Booking(\n            bookingRef,\n            event.cartId(),\n            event.userId(),\n            eventId,\n            event.slotId(),\n {

    "output": "Chunk ID: 917d59\nWall time: 0.0507 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 544\nOutput:\npackage com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.service.client;\n\nimport org.springframework.beans.factory.annotation.Value;\nimport org.springframework.stereotype.Service;\nimport org.springframework.web.client.RestClient;\nimport org.springframework.web.client.RestClientException;\n\n/**\n * Internal REST client to check payment status in the payments-ticketing module.\n *\n * <p>Called by the PaymentTimeoutWatchdog to verify whether a cart has a confirmed\n * Stripe payment before releasing a timed-out seat lock.\n *\n * <p>Per PRODUCT.md Hard Rule #1: booking-inventory cannot import payments-ticketing\n * @Service / @Repository beans directly; inter-module calls must go via REST.\n */\n@Service\npublic class PaymentStatusClient {\n\n    private final RestClient restClient;\n\n    public PaymentStatusClient(\n        @Value(\"${app.internal-base-url:http://localhost:8080}\") String baseUrl\n    ) {\n        this.restClient = RestClient.builder().baseUrl(baseUrl).build();\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Returns {@code true} if the cart has a confirmed payment in payments-ticketing.\n     *\n     * <p>Returns {@code false} on any error (connection refused, 404, etc.) so that\n     * the watchdog defaults to releasing the lock — correct because a confirmed payment\n     * would have already transitioned the seat to CONFIRMED state.\n     *\n     * @param cartId the booking-inventory cart ID.\n     * @return {@code true} if payment is confirmed; {@code false} otherwise.\n     */\n    public boolean isPaymentConfirmed(Long cartId) {\n        if (cartId == null) {\n            return false;\n        }\n        try {\n            PaymentStatusResponse response = restClient.get()\n                .uri(\"/api/v1/internal/payments/by-cart/{cartId}\", cartId)\n                .retrieve()\n                .body(PaymentStatusResponse.class);\n            return response != null && response.confirmed();\n        } catch (RestClientException ex) {\n            // payments-ticketing unavailable or cart has no payment record → safe to release\n            return false;\n        }\n    }\n\n    // DTO for the internal endpoint response\n    public record PaymentStatusResponse(Long cartId, boolean confirmed) {}\n}\n"
  }
}

    "output": "Chunk ID: a63887\nWall time: 0.0506 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 1188\nOutput:\npackage com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.scheduler;\n\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.domain.Seat;\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.domain.SeatLockAuditLog;\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.domain.enums.LockReleaseReason;\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.domain.enums.SeatLockEvent;\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.domain.enums.SeatLockState;\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.repository.CartItemRepository;\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.repository.SeatLockAuditLogRepository;\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.repository.SeatRepository;\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.service.CartService;\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.service.client.PaymentStatusClient;\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.service.redis.SeatLockRedisService;\nimport java.time.Instant;\nimport java.util.List;\nimport lombok.extern.slf4j.Slf4j;\nimport org.springframework.scheduling.annotation.Scheduled;\nimport org.springframework.stereotype.Component;\nimport org.springframework.transaction.annotation.Transactional;\n\n/**\n * Scheduled watchdog that releases HARD_LOCKED / PAYMENT_PENDING seat locks\n * whose TTL has expired without a confirmed Stripe payment.\n *\n * <p>Payment confirmation check delegates to {@link PaymentStatusClient} which\n * calls {@code GET /internal/payments/by-cart/{cartId}/status} in payments-ticketing.\n * If the call fails (module not yet live, network issue), the default is {@code false}\n * — safe because confirmed payments always transition the seat to CONFIRMED state first.\n */\n@Slf4j\n@Component\npublic class PaymentTimeoutWatchdog {\n\n    private final SeatRepository seatRepository;\n    private final CartItemRepository cartItemRepository;\n    private final SeatLockAuditLogRepository auditRepository;\n    private final SeatLockRedisService seatLockRedisService;\n    private final PaymentStatusClient paymentStatusClient;\n    private final CartService cartService;\n\n    public PaymentTimeoutWatchdog(\n        SeatRepository seatRepository,\n        CartItemRepository cartItemRepository,\n        SeatLockAuditLogRepository auditRepository,\n        SeatLockRedisService seatLockRedisService,\n        PaymentStatusClient paymentStatusClient,\n        CartService cartService\n    ) {\n        this.seatRepository = seatRepository;\n        this.cartItemRepository = cartItemRepository;\n        this.auditRepository = auditRepository;\n        this.seatLockRedisService = seatLockRedisService;\n        this.paymentStatusClient = paymentStatusClient;\n        this.cartService = cartService;\n    }\n\n    @Scheduled(fixedDelay = 60000)\n    @Transactional\n    public void releaseTimedOutPaymentLocks() {\n        List<Seat> candidates = seatRepository.findAll().stream()\n            .filter(seat -> (seat.getLockState() == SeatLockState.HARD_LOCKED ||\n                seat.getLockState() == SeatLockState.PAYMENT_PENDING)\n                && seat.getLockedUntil() != null\n                && seat.getLockedUntil().isBefore(Instant.now()))\n            .toList();\n\n        for (Seat seat : candidates) {\n            try {\n                if (isPaymentConfirmed(seat)) {\n                    continue;\n                }\n                SeatLockState from = seat.getLockState();\n                Long userId = seat.getLockedByUserId();\n                seat.release(LockReleaseReason.PAYMENT_TIMEOUT);\n                seatRepository.save(seat);\n                if (userId != null) {\n                    seatLockRedisService.release(seat.getId(), userId);\n                }\n                auditRepository.save(new SeatLockAuditLog(\n                    seat.getId(),\n                    null,\n                    seat.getShowSlotId(),\n                    userId,\n                    from,\n                    SeatLockState.AVAILABLE,\n                    SeatLockEvent.RELEASE,\n                    LockReleaseReason.PAYMENT_TIMEOUT.name(),\n                    null\n                ));\n                if (userId != null) {\n                    cartService.handlePaymentTimeout(null, List.of(seat.getId()), userId);\n                }\n            } catch (Exception ex) {\n                log.warn(\"Payment timeout watchdog skip seatId={} reason={}\", seat.getId(), ex.getMessage());\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Checks whether a confirmed payment exists for the cart that contains this seat.\n     * Uses PaymentStatusClient (REST call to payments-ticketing internal API).\n     */\n    private boolean isPaymentConfirmed(Seat seat) {\n        Long cartId = cartItemRepository.findFirstBySeatId(seat.getId())\n            .map(item -> item.getCart().getId())\n            .orElse(null);\n        return paymentStatusClient.isPaymentConfirmed(cartId);\n    }\n}\n"
  }
}

    "output": "Chunk ID: a63887\nWall time: 0.0506 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 1188\nOutput:\npackage com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.scheduler;\n\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.domain.Seat;\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.domain.SeatLockAuditLog;\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.domain.enums.LockReleaseReason;\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.domain.enums.SeatLockEvent;\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.domain.enums.SeatLockState;\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.repository.CartItemRepository;\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.repository.SeatLockAuditLogRepository;\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.repository.SeatRepository;\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.service.CartService;\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.service.client.PaymentStatusClient;\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.service.redis.SeatLockRedisService;\nimport java.time.Instant;\nimport java.util.List;\nimport lombok.extern.slf4j.Slf4j;\nimport org.springframework.scheduling.annotation.Scheduled;\nimport org.springframework.stereotype.Component;\nimport org.springframework.transaction.annotation.Transactional;\n\n/**\n * Scheduled watchdog that releases HARD_LOCKED / PAYMENT_PENDING seat locks\n * whose TTL has expired without a confirmed Stripe payment.\n *\n * <p>Payment confirmation check delegates to {@link PaymentStatusClient} which\n * calls {@code GET /internal/payments/by-cart/{cartId}/status} in payments-ticketing.\n * If the call fails (module not yet live, network issue), the default is {@code false}\n * — safe because confirmed payments always transition the seat to CONFIRMED state first.\n */\n@Slf4j\n@Component\npublic class PaymentTimeoutWatchdog {\n\n    private final SeatRepository seatRepository;\n    private final CartItemRepository cartItemRepository;\n    private final SeatLockAuditLogRepository auditRepository;\n    private final SeatLockRedisService seatLockRedisService;\n    private final PaymentStatusClient paymentStatusClient;\n    private final CartService cartService;\n\n    public PaymentTimeoutWatchdog(\n        SeatRepository seatRepository,\n        CartItemRepository cartItemRepository,\n        SeatLockAuditLogRepository auditRepository,\n        SeatLockRedisService seatLockRedisService,\n        PaymentStatusClient paymentStatusClient,\n        CartService cartService\n    ) {\n        this.seatRepository = seatRepository;\n        this.cartItemRepository = cartItemRepository;\n        this.auditRepository = auditRepository;\n        this.seatLockRedisService = seatLockRedisService;\n        this.paymentStatusClient = paymentStatusClient;\n        this.cartService = cartService;\n    }\n\n    @Scheduled(fixedDelay = 60000)\n    @Transactional\n    public void releaseTimedOutPaymentLocks() {\n        List<Seat> candidates = seatRepository.findAll().stream()\n            .filter(seat -> (seat.getLockState() == SeatLockState.HARD_LOCKED ||\n                seat.getLockState() == SeatLockState.PAYMENT_PENDING)\n                && seat.getLockedUntil() != null\n                && seat.getLockedUntil().isBefore(Instant.now()))\n            .toList();\n\n        for (Seat seat : candidates) {\n            try {\n                if (isPaymentConfirmed(seat)) {\n                    continue;\n                }\n                SeatLockState from = seat.getLockState();\n                Long userId = seat.getLockedByUserId();\n                seat.release(LockReleaseReason.PAYMENT_TIMEOUT);\n                seatRepository.save(seat);\n                if (userId != null) {\n                    seatLockRedisService.release(seat.getId(), userId);\n                }\n                auditRepository.save(new SeatLockAuditLog(\n                    seat.getId(),\n                    null,\n                    seat.getShowSlotId(),\n                    userId,\n                    from,\n                    SeatLockState.AVAILABLE,\n                    SeatLockEvent.RELEASE,\n                    LockReleaseReason.PAYMENT_TIMEOUT.name(),\n                    null\n                ));\n                if (userId != null) {\n                    cartService.handlePaymentTimeout(null, List.of(seat.getId()), userId);\n                }\n            } catch (Exception ex) {\n                log.warn(\"Payment timeout watchdog skip seatId={} reason={}\", seat.getId(), ex.getMessage());\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Checks whether a confirmed payment exists for the cart that contains this seat.\n     * Uses PaymentStatusClient (REST call to payments-ticketing internal API).\n     */\n    private boolean isPaymentConfirmed(Seat seat) {\n        Long cartId = cartItemRepository.findFirstBySeatId(seat.getId())\n            .map(item -> item.getCart().getId())\n            .orElse(null);\n        return paymentStatusClient.isPaymentConfirmed(cartId);\n    }\n}\n"
  }
}

    "call_id": "call_rBBLQdQcCy9gClk6GjG8dty2",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 81cf7a\nWall time: 0.0510 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 410\nOutput:\npackage com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.api.internal;\n\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.domain.CartItem;\nimport com.eventplatform.bookinginventory.repository.CartItemRepository;\nimport java.util.List;\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.GetMapping;\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.PathVariable;\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RequestMapping;\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RestController;\n\n@RestController\n@RequestMapping(\"/internal/booking/carts\")\npublic class InternalCartQueryController {\n\n    private final CartItemRepository cartItemRepository;\n\n    public InternalCartQueryController(CartItemRepository cartItemRepository) {\n        this.cartItemRepository = cartItemRepository;\n    }\n\n    @GetMapping(\"/{cartId}/items\")\n    public List<CartItemSnapshotResponse> getCartItems(@PathVariable Long cartId) {\n        return cartItemRepository.findByCartId(cartId).stream()\n            .map(this::toResponse)\n            .toList();\n    }\n\n    private CartItemSnapshotResponse toResponse(CartItem item) {\n        return new CartItemSnapshotResponse(\n            item.getId(),\n            item.getSeatId(),\n            item.getGaClaimId(),\n            item.getEbTicketClassId(),\n            item.getBasePrice().amount().longValue(),\n            item.getBasePrice().currency().toLowerCase(),\n            item.getQuantity()\n        );\n    }\n\n    public record CartItemSnapshotResponse(\n        Long itemId,\n        Long seatId,\n        Long gaClaimId,\n        String ticketClassId,\n        Long unitPrice,\n        String currency,\n        Integer quantity\n    ) {\n    }\n}\n"

## Entry 673

**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**

{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T17:49:33.565Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call",
    "name": "request_user_input",
    "arguments": "{\"questions\":[{\"header\":\"Compat\",\"id\":\"compat_strategy\",\"question\":\"For the hot-path refactor, should we keep existing internal HTTP endpoints as fallback/compat, or remove them fully once interfaces are wired?\",\"options\":[{\"label\":\"Keep both (Recommended)\",\"description\":\"Use shared interfaces for runtime path, keep HTTP endpoints temporarily for backward compatibility and easier rollback.\"},{\"label\":\"Remove HTTP now\",\"description\":\"Delete internal HTTP controllers/clients immediately after interface wiring for a cleaner architecture.\"},{\"label\":\"Keep HTTP only for one path\",\"description\":\"Refactor one hot path to interface now, defer the other for a later release.\"}]}]}",
    "call_id": "call_DhG9DhxZAwfFE3J3TxTqzagS"
  }
}
```

## Entry 683
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-04T17:50:44.976Z
**Role/Type:** event_msg
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T17:50:44.976Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "item_completed",
    "thread_id": "019cb9da-05bc-70b0-88a4-1048f2c0e12d",
    "turn_id": "019cb9f7-9bfa-73c1-bddd-abc166905717",
    "item": {
      "type": "Plan",
      "id": "019cb9f7-9bfa-73c1-bddd-abc166905717-plan",
      "text": "# Plan: Refactor FR5 Hot Paths from Internal HTTP to Shared Reader Interfaces\n\n## Summary\nRefactor the 2 identified hot paths from cross-module REST to in-process shared interfaces, following the same pattern already used by `SlotSummaryReader` / `SlotPricingReader`.  \nChosen compatibility strategy: **remove HTTP now** (no dual-path fallback).\n\nGoals:\n1. Eliminate internal HTTP overhead on payment confirmation and watchdog loops.\n2. Preserve module boundaries via `shared/common/service` interfaces (no direct module-service imports).\n3. Remove now-obsolete internal controllers/HTTP client classes.\n\n## Scope\nIn scope:\n1. `payments-ticketing` confirm flow cart snapshot read.\n2. `booking-inventory` payment-timeout watchdog payment status read.\n3. Shared contracts/interfaces to support both paths.\n4. Removal of replaced internal HTTP components.\n\nOut of scope:\n1. Cancellation slot-timing path (`CancellationService`) stays as-is (not hot).\n2. Public external API behavior changes.\n3. Database schema/migrations.\n\n## Interface and Contract Changes (Decision-Complete)\nAdd these shared contracts:\n\n1. New DTO: `shared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/common/dto/CartItemSnapshotDto.java`\n- Fields:\n  - `Long itemId`\n  - `Long seatId`\n  - `Long gaClaimId`\n  - `String ticketClassId`\n  - `Long unitPrice`\n  - `String currency`\n  - `Integer quantity`\n\n2. New interface: `shared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/common/service/CartSnapshotReader.java`\n- Method:\n  - `List<CartItemSnapshotDto> getCartItems(Long cartId);`\n\n3. New interface: `shared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/common/service/PaymentConfirmationReader.java`\n- Method:\n  - `boolean isPaymentConfirmed(Long cartId);`\n\nNo public HTTP API additions.  \nInternal HTTP endpoints below will be removed:\n\n1. `GET /internal/booking/carts/{cartId}/items`\n2. `GET /api/v1/internal/payments/by-cart/{cartId}`\n\n## Implementation Plan\n\n### 1) Implement owning-module readers\n1. Add `booking-inventory` implementation:\n- File: `booking-inventory/.../service/CartSnapshotReaderImpl.java`\n- Implements `CartSnapshotReader`.\n- Uses `CartItemRepository.findByCartId(cartId)`.\n- Maps `CartItem` -> `CartItemSnapshotDto`.\n- Annotate with `@Service` and `@Transactional(readOnly = true)`.\n\n2. Add `payments-ticketing` implementation:\n- File: `payments-ticketing/.../service/PaymentConfirmationReaderImpl.java`\n- Implements `PaymentConfirmationReader`.\n- Uses `BookingRepository.existsByCartIdAndStatus(cartId, BookingStatus.CONFIRMED)`.\n- Annotate with `@Service` and `@Transactional(readOnly = true)`.\n\n### 2) Rewire consumers to interfaces (remove REST runtime path)\n1. Update `PaymentService`:\n- Replace `RestClient` dependency with `CartSnapshotReader`.\n- Remove `@Value(\"${app.internal-base-url...}\")` constructor arg.\n- Replace `fetchCartItems` REST call with direct interface call.\n- Preserve existing behavior for empty cart and mapping logic.\n\n2. Update `PaymentTimeoutWatchdog`:\n- Replace `PaymentStatusClient` dependency with `PaymentConfirmationReader`.\n- Keep existing null/cart-not-found safe behavior.\n- Update comments/Javadocs to reflect interface-based read.\n\n### 3) Delete obsolete HTTP components\n1. Delete `booking-inventory/api/internal/InternalCartQueryController.java`.\n2. Delete `payments-ticketing/api/internal/InternalPaymentController.java`.\n3. Delete `booking-inventory/service/client/PaymentStatusClient.java`.\n\n### 4) Security cleanup\n1. Remove `\"/api/v1/internal/**\"` matcher from `SecurityConfig` since endpoint is removed.\n2. Keep `\"/internal/**\"` because other non-hot internal endpoints still use it.\n\n### 5) Build/package hygiene\n1. Remove unused imports and dead DTOs tied to deleted REST clients/controllers.\n2. Ensure no references remain to deleted classes/endpoints.\n\n## Tests and Validation\n\n### Update/Delete tests tied to removed HTTP path\n1. Delete `InternalPaymentControllerTest` (or rewrite only if replacement controller exists; in this plan it is deleted).\n2. Delete `PaymentStatusClientTest` (class removed).\n\n### Add/Update tests for interface path\n1. Add `CartSnapshotReaderImplTest` in `booking-inventory`:\n- Returns mapped list for existing cart items.\n- Returns empty list when cart has no items.\n- Validates seat/GA/ticketClass/currency/quantity mapping.\n\n2. Add `PaymentConfirmationReaderImplTest` in `payments-ticketing`:\n- Returns `true` when booking exists with `CONFIRMED`.\n- Returns `false` otherwise.\n\n3. Update `PaymentService` service tests:\n- Mock `CartSnapshotReader`.\n- Confirm booking confirmation still creates `BookingItem` and `ETicket`.\n- Confirm empty cart still throws `CartItemsFetchException`.\n\n4. Update `PaymentTimeoutWatchdog` tests:\n- Mock `PaymentConfirmationReader`.\n- Verify confirmed carts skip release.\n- Verify unconfirmed carts release locks and audit logs.\n\n### Acceptance checks\n1. `rg` shows no runtime references to:\n- `/internal/booking/carts/`\n- `/api/v1/internal/payments/by-cart/`\n- `PaymentStatusClient`\n- `InternalPaymentController`\n- `InternalCartQueryController`\n2. Maven compile passes for `shared`, `booking-inventory`, `payments-ticketing`.\n3. Relevant module tests pass.\n\n## Risks and Mitigations\n1. Risk: silent behavior drift in cart item mapping.\n- Mitigation: dedicated mapper-focused tests in `CartSnapshotReaderImplTest`.\n\n2. Risk: watchdog semantics change.\n- Mitigation: keep boolean semantics identical and cover with watchdog tests.\n\n3. Risk: accidental module coupling.\n- Mitigation: only `shared/common/service` interfaces injected; no direct module service import.\n\n## Assumptions and Defaults\n1. This is a single-JVM modular monolith deployment, so interface implementations are discoverable at runtime.\n2. No other modules rely on the two removed internal HTTP endpoints.\n3. “Remove HTTP now” means immediate deletion in same refactor PR, not deprecation.\n4. Existing cancellation flow internal endpoint remains untouched because it is not part of the two hot paths.\n"
    }
  }
}
```

## Entry 684
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-04T17:50:44.977Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T17:50:44.977Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "message",
    "role": "assistant",
    "content": [
      {
        "type": "output_text",
        "text": "<proposed_plan>\n# Plan: Refactor FR5 Hot Paths from Internal HTTP to Shared Reader Interfaces\n\n## Summary\nRefactor the 2 identified hot paths from cross-module REST to in-process shared interfaces, following the same pattern already used by `SlotSummaryReader` / `SlotPricingReader`.  \nChosen compatibility strategy: **remove HTTP now** (no dual-path fallback).\n\nGoals:\n1. Eliminate internal HTTP overhead on payment confirmation and watchdog loops.\n2. Preserve module boundaries via `shared/common/service` interfaces (no direct module-service imports).\n3. Remove now-obsolete internal controllers/HTTP client classes.\n\n## Scope\nIn scope:\n1. `payments-ticketing` confirm flow cart snapshot read.\n2. `booking-inventory` payment-timeout watchdog payment status read.\n3. Shared contracts/interfaces to support both paths.\n4. Removal of replaced internal HTTP components.\n\nOut of scope:\n1. Cancellation slot-timing path (`CancellationService`) stays as-is (not hot).\n2. Public external API behavior changes.\n3. Database schema/migrations.\n\n## Interface and Contract Changes (Decision-Complete)\nAdd these shared contracts:\n\n1. New DTO: `shared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/common/dto/CartItemSnapshotDto.java`\n- Fields:\n  - `Long itemId`\n  - `Long seatId`\n  - `Long gaClaimId`\n  - `String ticketClassId`\n  - `Long unitPrice`\n  - `String currency`\n  - `Integer quantity`\n\n2. New interface: `shared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/common/service/CartSnapshotReader.java`\n- Method:\n  - `List<CartItemSnapshotDto> getCartItems(Long cartId);`\n\n3. New interface: `shared/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/shared/common/service/PaymentConfirmationReader.java`\n- Method:\n  - `boolean isPaymentConfirmed(Long cartId);`\n\nNo public HTTP API additions.  \nInternal HTTP endpoints below will be removed:\n\n1. `GET /internal/booking/carts/{cartId}/items`\n2. `GET /api/v1/internal/payments/by-cart/{cartId}`\n\n## Implementation Plan\n\n### 1) Implement owning-module readers\n1. Add `booking-inventory` implementation:\n- File: `booking-inventory/.../service/CartSnapshotReaderImpl.java`\n- Implements `CartSnapshotReader`.\n- Uses `CartItemRepository.findByCartId(cartId)`.\n- Maps `CartItem` -> `CartItemSnapshotDto`.\n- Annotate with `@Service` and `@Transactional(readOnly = true)`.\n\n2. Add `payments-ticketing` implementation:\n- File: `payments-ticketing/.../service/PaymentConfirmationReaderImpl.java`\n- Implements `PaymentConfirmationReader`.\n- Uses `BookingRepository.existsByCartIdAndStatus(cartId, BookingStatus.CONFIRMED)`.\n- Annotate with `@Service` and `@Transactional(readOnly = true)`.\n\n### 2) Rewire consumers to interfaces (remove REST runtime path)\n1. Update `PaymentService`:\n- Replace `RestClient` dependency with `CartSnapshotReader`.\n- Remove `@Value(\"${app.internal-base-url...}\")` constructor arg.\n- Replace `fetchCartItems` REST call with direct interface call.\n- Preserve existing behavior for empty cart and mapping logic.\n\n2. Update `PaymentTimeoutWatchdog`:\n- Replace `PaymentStatusClient` dependency with `PaymentConfirmationReader`.\n- Keep existing null/cart-not-found safe behavior.\n- Update comments/Javadocs to reflect interface-based read.\n\n### 3) Delete obsolete HTTP components\n1. Delete `booking-inventory/api/internal/InternalCartQueryController.java`.\n2. Delete `payments-ticketing/api/internal/InternalPaymentController.java`.\n3. Delete `booking-inventory/service/client/PaymentStatusClient.java`.\n\n### 4) Security cleanup\n1. Remove `\"/api/v1/internal/**\"` matcher from `SecurityConfig` since endpoint is removed.\n2. Keep `\"/internal/**\"` because other non-hot internal endpoints still use it.\n\n### 5) Build/package hygiene\n1. Remove unused imports and dead DTOs tied to deleted REST clients/controllers.\n2. Ensure no references remain to deleted classes/endpoints.\n\n## Tests and Validation\n\n### Update/Delete tests tied to removed HTTP path\n1. Delete `InternalPaymentControllerTest` (or rewrite only if replacement controller exists; in this plan it is deleted).\n2. Delete `PaymentStatusClientTest` (class removed).\n\n### Add/Update tests for interface path\n1. Add `CartSnapshotReaderImplTest` in `booking-inventory`:\n- Returns mapped list for existing cart items.\n- Returns empty list when cart has no items.\n- Validates seat/GA/ticketClass/currency/quantity mapping.\n\n2. Add `PaymentConfirmationReaderImplTest` in `payments-ticketing`:\n- Returns `true` when booking exists with `CONFIRMED`.\n- Returns `false` otherwise.\n\n3. Update `PaymentService` service tests:\n- Mock `CartSnapshotReader`.\n- Confirm booking confirmation still creates `BookingItem` and `ETicket`.\n- Confirm empty cart still throws `CartItemsFetchException`.\n\n4. Update `PaymentTimeoutWatchdog` tests:\n- Mock `PaymentConfirmationReader`.\n- Verify confirmed carts skip release.\n- Verify unconfirmed carts release locks and audit logs.\n\n### Acceptance checks\n1. `rg` shows no runtime references to:\n- `/internal/booking/carts/`\n- `/api/v1/internal/payments/by-cart/`\n- `PaymentStatusClient`\n- `InternalPaymentController`\n- `InternalCartQueryController`\n2. Maven compile passes for `shared`, `booking-inventory`, `payments-ticketing`.\n3. Relevant module tests pass.\n\n## Risks and Mitigations\n1. Risk: silent behavior drift in cart item mapping.\n- Mitigation: dedicated mapper-focused tests in `CartSnapshotReaderImplTest`.\n\n2. Risk: watchdog semantics change.\n- Mitigation: keep boolean semantics identical and cover with watchdog tests.\n\n3. Risk: accidental module coupling.\n- Mitigation: only `shared/common/service` interfaces injected; no direct module service import.\n\n## Assumptions and Defaults\n1. This is a single-JVM modular monolith deployment, so interface implementations are discoverable at runtime.\n2. No other modules rely on the two removed internal HTTP endpoints.\n3. “Remove HTTP now” means immediate deletion in same refactor PR, not deprecation.\n4. Existing cancellation flow internal endpoint remains untouched because it is not part of the two hot paths.\n</proposed_plan>"
      }
    ],
    "phase": "final_answer"
  }
}
```
